title: Witchery
content: Welcome to Witchery, a mod for Minecraft that allows players to explore the magical art of witchcraft and though it, to learn to control the natural magic present in the world (and sometimes other dimensions). Witchery provides several branches of witchcraft for a new witch or warlock to try their hand at . Witchcraft does not really resemble the flashy spell-casting performed by wizards and their ilk. There are no lightning wands or spell books, enchanted armor or flaming swords; instead, a well tended witch's garden will provide a lot of helpful ingredients for magic that will get you out of the occasional scrape . There are of course darker sides to magic; and although these may bring power more quickly, the cost may be too high. Making bargains with demons should never be undertaken lightly ! Quick Links Download Witchery Mod (for Minecraft 1.6.4/1.7.2/1.7.10)* Minecraft Forums Thread Getting Started Guide FAQ Version History (with download links) Thoughts & Acknowledgements * by downloading and/or using this file you are agreeing to the Terms & Conditions of use . News Witchery 0.24 released Adds Magic Mirrors with a few reflection-based magical tricks. Posted Apr 12, 2015, 5:24 AM by Barry Hedgefund Witchery 0.23.0 released Adds vampirism for players, winged monkeys and towns. See the version history for all changes. Posted Mar 8, 2015, 8:26 AM by Barry Hedgefund Witchery 0.22.0 released Add Werewolves and silver weapons to fight them with. Curse of the Wolf can turn players into werewolves with shapeshifting! Posted Jan 11, 2015, 8:31 AM by Barry Hedgefund Showing posts 1 - 3 of 24 . View more » The disciplines of witchcraft Poppet Making - Sympathetic magic , creating links between beings and objects. Dream Weaving - Magic that influences while you sleep and adds benefits when players wake. Circle Magic - Powerful magical rites performed with circles and sacrificial items. Brews & Infusions - Infusing worldly power into objects and players and making other helpful brews .

title: About the Wiki Archive
content: Witchery Wiki Preservation Project This is a preserved static archive of the original Witchery Mod Wiki , hosted to keep documentation accessible for Minecraft players. 🔮 What is Witchery? Witchery is a Minecraft magic mod by Emoniph based on the mystical arts of Witchcraft, including brewing, circle magic, infusions, and shapeshifting. 🗂️ Why this Archive? The original wiki on Google Sites became inaccessible after platform updates. This static archive preserves the official documentation for players. ⚙️ Key Improvements Better Structure : Cleaned up the repository layout, grouping all 400+ wiki pages in a nested wiki/ directory. Instant Search : Added a custom client-side search engine that searches all 419 pages instantly with highlight matches. GitHub Pages Compatible : Added configuration to ensure all stylesheets and assets load correctly. 💬 Contributing If you find broken links or missing assets, please submit a pull request or open an issue on the GitHub repository.

title: Alder Leaves
content: Alder leaves are harvested from Alder trees in the same way vanilla leaves are harvested (i.e. with shears). Bashed or decaying Alder leaves have a chance to drop Alder saplings . Alder leaves will decay like vanilla leaves when all logs on a Alder tree are removed. Alder trees are grown from Alder saplings .

title: Alder Log
content: Alder logs are harvested from Alder trees in the same way vanilla logs are harvested (i.e. with an axe). Alder trees are grown from Alder saplings and can be made into Alder planks .

title: Alder Planks
content: Alder planks behave like all vanilla wooden planks and can be used in a majority of recipes that require wooden planks. Alder planks are crafted from Alder logs in the same way all other woods are crafted from logs.

title: Alder Sapling
content: Alder saplings grow into Alder trees when planted. Alder trees can be harvested for Alder logs and Alder leaves (which yield more Alder saplings). Alder saplings cooked in an oven yield Wood Ash , and have a chance of producing Reek of Misfortune . Alder saplings can initially be acquired only by using Mutandis on another planted sapling (does not matter which type). Given the mutation of the sapling is random, it may take a few goes before Alder saplings appear.

title: Alder Wood Door
content: An alder wood door looks exactly like a normal wooden door, but it has one key difference, instead of reacting to redstone signals it will generate one when it is opened. This makes the Alder Wood Door the perfect tool to create traps and events based on opening the door. The Alder wood door is only colored differently when held; when placed, it looks like a normal vanilla door (assuming you are not using a resource-pack of course!). The alder wood door is crafted using Alder planks and Reek of Misfortune .

title: Alluring Skull
content: The Alluring Skull is the product of Necromantic magic, creating an object that attracts the attention of otherwise passive undead creatures. The skull must be placed in the world and activated by using a Necromantic Stone on it. Its face will light up when active. When the skull is active all undead in a wide area will be drawn towards it. This influence is only powerful enough to draw undead when there is nothing more interesting, such as a nearby player. The skull is deactivated and broken by using a Necromantic Stone on it a second time. The skull cannot be broken by normal other means. The Alluring Skull is crafted using a Skull , a Necromantic Stone , a Voodoo Poppet and Glowstone Dust .

title: Altar Block
content: Six altar blocks are used to create an altar . Altar blocks are crafted using Stone Brick , a Water Bottle , Rowan Logs , Breath of the Goddess and Exhale of the Horned One .

title: Altar
content: An altar is a focal point to concentrate the magic present in the surrounding area into single usable source of power. Since witchcraft is a method to harness the power of nature, placing an altar in a location with little or no living things will yield very little magic to work with. There is a reason why most witches huts are found deep in dark forests or swamps! An altar is created by placing six altar blocks in a 2x3 pattern; you will know it is correct when a red cloth appears over the top of the altar. Activating the altar by right-clicking on it will bring up a screen showing how much power the altar has collected, how much it can collect and its current recharge rate. It is important to check that there is sufficient available power to perform a given rite or use a distillery or kettle recipe. The maximum amount of power available is determined by how many natural items are in the vicinity of the altar, dirt, grass, trees, plants, etc. all contribute; stone and sand do not. The important thing is to have a diverse mix of different plants, trees and crops (plants from other mods are supported to an extent as well). Range of an Altar An altar has a range of about 14 (to 16) blocks to collect power and a range of 14 (to 16) blocks to distribute power (the distribution range can be extended to 28 (to 32) blocks by placing an Arthana onto the altar). Any Heart Glyphs, Distilleries , Spinning Wheels or Kettles must be within this range in order to draw power from the altar. Devices can only draw power from a single altar ; it is therefore important to upgrade the altar rather than making multiple altars. Devices can share a single altar. Upgrading an altar Certain objects (or foci), placed onto an altar, will boost the focusing capabilities of an altar, either improving the amount of magic it can focus, the rate at which it replenishes or the range it can distribute power. Only one of each type of item (skull, chalice, etc.) will have an effect. There may also be other less savory foci that dark magic practitioners could make use of. Boosting maximum power Skull + 1x base power Wither Skull + 2x base power Chalice + 1x base power Filled Chalice + 1x base power Boosting recharge rate Skull + 1x base recharge rate Wither Skull + 2x base recharge rate Torch + 1x base recharge rate Candelabra + 2x base recharge rate Pentacle 2x recharge rate Boosting power distribution range Arthana 2x distribution range Finding a skull Because vanilla Minecraft does not support skull drops for skeletons ; witches and warlocks will use an Arthana to strike the killing blow on skeletons in order to try and acquire a skull (the chance is quite low, but improved with a Looting enchantment). Unfortunately, this weapon does not change the chances with Wither Skeletons (the looting enchantment has been known to improve that chance!)

title: Anguish of the Dead
content: Anguish of the Dead is an effect produced with a Brazier , it causes all creature standing near to the burning brazier to cause more damage. To produce this effect, add Tear of the Goddess , Bone and Blaze powder to a Brazier and light with a Flint & Tinder. The brazier requires Altar power.

title: Anointing Paste
content: Anointing Paste is smeared onto a regular cauldron to turn it into a Witches' Cauldron , ready for brewing interesting potions and spells. Simply place the Cauldron in the world, and then use the Anointing Paste on it to convert it into a Witches' Cauldron . Anointing Paste is made in a Crafting Bench using Mandrake Seeds (M), Belladonna Seeds (B), Snowbell Seeds (S) and Water Artichoke Seeds (A) in any order. Crafting Bench -------------- M B S A

title: Apple of Sleeping
content: An Apple of Sleeping can be eaten to allow a player to enter the Spirit World . The dreamer will always experience a nightmare after eating this apple. The apple is crafted normally using a Brew of Sleeping , Wormy Apple , Mutandis and Reek of Misfortune .

title: Armor Protection Poppet
content: The Armor Protection Poppet will prevent a piece of armor that the bound player is using from breaking, and destroy the poppet in its place. The armor will be completely repaired in the process. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. The poppet is crafted from a Poppet , Reek of Misfortune , Drop of Luck and Diamond Vapor .

title: Arthana
content: An Arthana is a ritualistic knife use by witches and warlocks to both collect special ingredients and to perform some rites . It has the same general properties as a golden sword (damage, enchantability, etc.), although it has the durability of an iron sword. It is repaired with a Gold Ingot . The main purpose of the dagger is to collect specialist ingredients used in witchcraft, and to this end it will dramatically improve the chance of a mob dropping such ingredients (e.g. Wool of Bat , Tongue of Dog , etc.). It also gives a small chance of undead dropping Spectral Dust . When only using vanilla Minecraft with Witchery (and no other mods), an Arthana dagger is the only way to get a skull to drop when a skeleton is killed, the drop chance is about 5% (the skull is a key upgrade for an altar ). The dagger even gives a very small probability for other head drops (from mobs and players). All drop chances are increased with the Looting enchantment . An Arthana does not change the drop chance for Wither Skeleton Skulls, that will require the Looting enchantment as usual. Some Circle Magic rites use an Arthana as a foci, the Arthana is not consumed in the process. An Arthana is crafted using a Gold Ingot , Gold Nuggets , an Emerald and a Stick .

title: Attuned Stone
content: An attuned stone, is a diamond that has been infused with natural magic in a fiery medium. Attuned stones can be used to link non-magical devices to the magical currents present in the world, and are one of the main ingredients in crafting distilleries , kettles , altar foci and poppet shelves . The other main use of attuned stones is to act as a portable power source for circle magic rites, when a nearby altar is not available. To act in such a way, the attuned stone must first be charged with a Rite of Charging . An attuned stone is crafted using a Diamond , a Whiff of Magic and a Lava Bucket .

title: Baba Yaga
content: Baba Yaga is a witch (or one of a trio of sisters of the same name), who appears as a deformed and/or ferocious-looking elderly woman. She flies around in a mortar and wields a pestle . She dwells deep in the forest, in a hut usually described as standing on chicken legs, with a fence decorated with human skulls. Baba Yaga may help or hinder those that encounter or seek her out. Baba Yaga and her sisters are assumed to be immortal, although one can drive them off with enough persistence. Expect a very hard fight if she chooses to hinder. Baba Yaga tends to take an interest in those who meddle in the future. Baba Yaga usually drops her hat when defeated in a fight.

title: Baba Yaga's Hat
content: Defeating Baba Yaga will allow her hat to be acquired. This hat is curious in that it adds 25% to the chance to create a second brew when brewing with a kettle, and additionally it gives a 25% to create a third brew! When brewing with a Witches' Cauldron, the hat will give significantly more bottled brews when worn by a very skilled witch (it can double the number of bottles in such cases). The hat will also try to keep its wearer out of harms way. If the player is infused, it will occasionally teleport the player a short distance away from harm at the cost of some infusion energy. In all other respects the hat behaves like other witches hats, being reparable with Impregnated Leather and providing the same defense as leather armor.

title: Banshee
content: Banshees may be summoned with a Brazier . The scream when creatures are nearby, causing damage. Using Graveyard Dust on this creature, if controlled by the player, will increase its maximum health by two points, to a maximum of 50 health.

title: Bark Belt
content: The Bark Belt is infused with the spirit of nature and will grow pieces of protective bark on the wearer if they stand on grass or mycellium. Bark will only grow on clothing made from Impregnated Leather such as Witches and necromancer robes, witches hats, Icy Slippers, Ruby Slippers or Seeping Shoes. Two pieces of bark can grow per armor clothing piece worn (to a maximum of eight pieces). When a wearer of the belt is hit by anything except a wooden weapon, one or two pieces of bark will be knocked off instead of the wearer taking damage! This effect takes place before other witchcraft-based damage mitigation effects. The Bark Belt has the same durability and protection as leather armor and can be dyed and enchanted like leather armor too. Bark belts will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if a normal belt was worn. Other players however will not be able to see the belt! The belt is crafted from a Biting Belt , Emerald , Ent Twigs , Creeper Heart and Brews of Flowing Spirit . Crafting a damaged Biting Belt with four Impregnated Leather will repair the belt. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Beartrap
content: A Beartrap is a way to capture creatures (and players). Place one on the ground and then set it, the next creature that walks over it will be stuck fast for some seconds and take a small amount of damage. Once triggered the trap will need to be set again. Once a trap has been set, only the player who set it will be able to easily see it. Beware, once set, there is a short delay before it can be triggered. A Beartrap is made in a crafting bench using Iron Ingots, a Heavy Weighted Pressure Plate and Shears.

title: Belladonna Seeds
content: Belladonna seeds can be planted and eventually grow into the multistage belladonna plant. The fully grown belladonna plant can be harvested to obtain more Belladonna seeds and a Belladonna flower . Belladonna seeds can also be obtained as a random drop, by bashing tall grass .

title: Belladonna
content: Belladonna is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is harvested from a fully grown Belladonna plant that must be grown from Belladonna seeds .

title: Binky's Skull
content: Binky is Death's steed, and can be summoned by using his Skull on the ground. When Binky is killed, hist skull will again fall to the floor and may be used another time. Binky is a skeletal horse that has a lot of heath and moves a little faster than a normal horse. Binky's Skull is a possible drop each time Death is defeated.

title: Biome Note
content: A Biome Note is a piece of paper inscribed with details of a particular biome, it can be used in Brewing and Ritual recipes. A Biome Note is created by crafting a Piece of Paper with the book, Book of Biomes (Extended Edition) . Whichever page was last viewed in the book will be copied to the biome note when it is created.

title: Biting Belt
content: The biting belt is a piece of clothing placed in the legs slot, that is made from a Parasytic Louse , and can be fed potions in the same way, up to two potions can be fed to the belt. When the player is subsequently hit, the belt will bite either the player or the attacker and inject the potion (beneficial potions apply to the wearer, negative potions the attacker). The belt will only bite if the target is not already under the effects of that potion. The Biting Belt has the same durability and protection as leather armor and can be dyed and enchanted like leather armor too. Craft the belt with a potion to feed it to the belt. Biting belts will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if a normal belt was worn. Other players however will not be able to see the belt! The belt is crafted from Impregnated Leather , Mellifluous Hunger and Parasytic Louse and Golden Thread . Crafting a damaged Biting Belt with four Impregnated Leather will repair the belt. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Blood Crucible
content: A Blood Crucible is a stone bowl used by vampire covens to collect their victims blood for the exclusive use of the master of the coven. If a vampire player level 10, places one near to where they have created their vampire "children", when the vampires return from their nightly hunt, they will partially fill the crucible. When the crucible is full, the vampire player may use one of the following ingredients on it, to receive up to 5 charges of the corresponding effect, which they may activate at any time from their vampire hot bar. Item Power Water Artichoke Call storm - When activated will start a thunder storm, which allows a vampire to go outside at daytime. Bone Teleport - When activated away from their spawn point, will teleport the vampire to their spawn. When activated near their spawn point, will teleport the vampire to the closest village. Wool of Bat Bat Swarm - When activated will summon a swam of bats, if the player then looks at a creature or player, the bats will fly towards it and attack. A Blood Crucible is made form Stone Bricks, Stone Brick Slab and Stone Brick Stairs.

title: Blood Rose
content: The Blood Rose is rose with a secret, it will bite any player passing over it and keep a small sample of their blood in a small sac. A single rose only has space to store the blood of a single player. The blood can be extracted using a taglock kit on the rose. Blood roses will be destroyed if harvested with anything other than a Boline . Four Blood Roses are created by placing a Leech Chest over still water and surrounding its four sides with Roses . Using a Mutating Sprig on the chest will cause the roses to be mutated into Blood Roses (the chest will be consumed).

title: Blood-stained Wool
content: Blood-stained wool is a wool block that has been splattered with Vampire blood. Blood-stained wool can be cooked in a furnace to make Woven Cruor . Blood-stained wool is made by placing a block of white wool into the world, then a vampire of level 4 or above with at least 125 blood in their Blood Pool, must use a bone needle on the wool block. The block can then be broken to pick it up.

title: Blooded Waystone
content: A Blooded Waystone, is a waystone that has been bound to a player or creature using the appropriate Rite of Binding . A Blooded Waystone may be used in a Rite of Transposition to teleport participants of the Rite to the current location of the bound entity. A Blooded Waystone may also be used to designate who and Owl must fly to. A blooded waystone may also be made by dropping a waystone into a hollow 3x3 circle of otherwhere runes, near to an altar with the creature standing very close by.

title: Bloodied Wicker Bundle
content: A bloodied wicker bundle is a wicker bundle that has been anointed with demon's blood. It is an aesthetic block that may be placed in the world, often used to make wicker men (modeled after the strawmen found in the world). It has been known that setting fire to such a bloodied wicker man has been known to attract huntsmen of the Wild Hunt ! A Bloodied Wicker Bundle is crafted from Wicker Bundles and Demonic Blood .

title: Boline
content: A Boline is a tool with two purposes, the first is to aid in collecting ingredients. To this end is functions as shears with the additional benefit that it will harvest cobweb without turning it to string. It can also be used to harvest Blood Roses and Trapped Plants without breaking them. Its second purpose is its use Circle magic rites connected with necromancy. A Boline can be used as a weapon with the same durability as an iron sword, but the damage of a wooden sword. It can be enchanted like an iron sword. A Boline is crafted using an Iron Ingot, Bone and Emerald.

title: Bone Bolt
content: A Bone Bolt is ammunition for the Witch Hunter Pistol Crossbow . They about 3 hearts base damage. To undead and demonic creatures it will do and additional 50% damage after any enchantment bonus have been applied. Bone bolts are made using Wooden Bolts , Bones and a Ghast Tear.

title: Bone Needle
content: Bone needles are used in other recipes such as Poppets and Taglock Kits , or in combination with a Voodoo Poppet . Bone needles are crafted from Bone and Flint .

title: Book of Biomes (Extended Edition)
content: The Extended Edition of the Book of Biomes, has much more information about each biome, than the simpler version of the book. More critically, it contains enough information to create Biome Notes . Biome Notes are created by selecting a page in the Book of Biomes (Extended Edition) and then crafting the book with a blank piece of paper. The Book of Biomes (Extended Edition) is created in a crafting bench using a Book of Biomes (B) and 4 stone blocks (S). Crafting Table -------------- S S B S S

title: Book of Biomes
content: The Book of Biomes, describes the many biomes that can be found in the overworld. It is an essential companion to the Rite of Shifting Seasons because it lists the Foci Items and Glowstone Dust cost of a biome. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , a Stone Block and an Oak Sapling .

title: Bottle of Lilith's Blood
content: The Bottle of Lilith's Blood is a non-craftable item that a player may drink. When drunk by a vampire player , it will increase their vampire level by one. If the player is already at the maximum vampire level, it will restore eight droplets of blood to their blood pool instead. if drunk by a non-vampire character, it will turn them into a vampire at level one. This item is intended for adventure maps or quest-based mod packs that want to control vampire progression themselves. To disable the default witchery vampire quests, the following witchery configuration options (in the config file) should be set to false: B:AllowVampireQuests=false B:AllowVampireRitual=false The first disables the quests a vampire may undertake to level up (making the Book Observations of an Immortal redundant). The second stops the ritual necessary to become a vampire in the first place from working.

title: Bottle of Warm Blood
content: The Bottle of Warm Blood is a non-craftable item that can be drunk by a player. If the player is a vampire, they will restore 2 droplets of blood to the blood pool. If the player is not a vampire they will get a hunger debuff. This item allows creative players to quickly get blood, or it can be used as a reward in adventure maps or quest-based mod packs.

title: Brazier
content: A Brazier is the tool a witch (or warlock) uses to perform conjuration magics, specifically to commune with the spectral dead. Braziers are typically placed within range of an altar, filled with three ingredients and then lit with a Flint & Tinder to produce the desired effect either as the Brazier burns, or when it is burnt. A water bucket can be used to extinguish a brazier and clear its current ingredients. Lighting a brazier without valid ingredients will just cause smoke. Lighting it when it requires altar power and has none, will just cause flame until power becomes available. The brazier is made using Iron Ingots, a Necromantic Stone and Wooded Sticks. The following effects can be performed: Graveyard mist . Summon Spectre . Summon Banshee . Deathly Veil . Anguish of the Dead . Fortification of the Corpse . Drain Growth . There may be other less savory effects.

title: Breath of the Goddess
content: The Breath of the Goddess is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Birch saplings in an oven .

title: Brew Bag
content: A Brew Bag is a convenient way for a witch (or warlock) to store and deploy the myriad brews they need to carry. A brew bag can store up to 8 stacks of throwable brews. To open the inventory of a brew bag, use the bag while sneaking (right-click with shift-held). This will open the inventory, where throwable brews can be moved into and out of the bag. Using the bag (holding the right-mouse button) while not sneaking will show a radial menu on screen containing the brews in the bag (the position of the brews is dictated by where i the bag they are stored). Push the mouse in the direction of the desired brew to select it and release the use button to throw the brew. If a brew is selected, sneak to clear the selection (hold shift). A brew bag gives a lot of flexibility when using it, but rapidly throwing many of the same brew one after the other is still best achieved directly from the hot bar. A brew bag can be dyed by crafting it with a dye powder. A brew bag is crafted with Impregnated Leather and Golden Thread .

title: Brew of Bats
content: The Brew of Bats is a throwable potion that will cause explode into a swarm of bats, causing all creatures and players in the area of effect to be momentarily stunned. The Brew of Thorns is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw a Concentrated Bat Ball , Wool of Bat , an Apple , Sugar , a Fermented Spider Eye and Gunpowder . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Bodega
content: The Brew of Bodega is a throwable potion that can be thrown at a player or creature. Doing so will cause a flock (or parliament) of owls to descend on the target and begin attacking. If the creature dies the owls will disappear. The Brew of Bodega is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Brown Mushroom , Wool of Bat , Seeds , a Feather , Brew of Thorns and Owlet's Wing . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Cursed Leaping
content: The Brew of Cursed Leaping is a throwable potion that when it hits something explodes causing all players and creature in the area to be thrown into the air and additional granted a jumping buff. The main issue with this brew is that the jump buff is not as strong as the initial leap and so some fall damage is inevitable. The Brew of Cursed Leaping is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw a Bone , Belladonna Flower , Apple , a Feather , Brew of Sprouting and a Raw Fish . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Endless Water
content: The Brew of Endless Water, while not creating a truly endless supply of water, does allow for about 100 applications of its watery contents. Using the Brew on the ground will place a water block at that location. Using it on a regular Cauldron, Witches' Cauldron or Kettle will fill the container with water (for only a single charge). Placing the Brew into a Dispenser, will dispense a single block of water when the dispenser receives a redstone signal. If the dispensor is facing an empty Witches' Cauldron or Kettle when the water is dispensed, it will fill the container with water instead of placing the water block. The Brew of Endless Water is created in a Witches' Cauldron according to the recipe in the book Witches' Brews .

title: Brew of Erosion
content: The Brew of Erosion is a throwable potion; it is a volatile acid that melts a surface when it hits one, causing all nearby blocks to be destroyed . The notable exceptions to this are obsidian, lava and water. Lava and water will be unaffected by the acid, whereas obsidian will only be broken (instead of being destroyed). If a creature or player is hit by a Brew of Erosion they will take a large amount of damage and their metal weapons and armor will be significantly damaged (this is not a splash effect). The Brew of Erosion is a great, if expensive, way to tunnel quickly (as long as you don't mind being unable to pick up the blocks!). It is also an excellent way to quickly mine obsidian (as long as the problem of the obsidian dropping into lava can be solved). The Brew of Erosion is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw 2 x Oil of Vitriol , Quicklime , Magma Cream , Belladonna and Yellow Flower . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Flowing Spirit
content: The Brew of Flowing Spirit captures the essence of the Spirit World in liquid form. It can only be brewed in the Spirit World ! The Brew can be thrown at the ground and the liquid will burst out (the source block can later be picked up in a bucket). Standing in the liquid will have a minor healing effect for most creatures. Demonic or undead creatures will be weakened. Nightmares standing in the liquid will lose their invulnerability effect! Having a pool of flowing spirit near to a Dream Weaver of Nightmares will decrease the chance of a nightmare by 10% for each source block (up to 3). Into a kettle, throw a Fanciful Thread , Spanish Moss , Water Artichoke Globe , Glint Weed , Mandrake Root and Wool of Bat .

title: Brew of Frog's Tongue
content: The Brew of Frog's Tongue is a throwable potion that explodes when it hits anything, causing all players and monsters in the area of effect to be pulled violently towards the thrower. The Brew of Frog's Tongue is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw a Red Mushroom , Wheat , Brew of Webs , Water Artichoke Globe , Slimeball and Toe of Frog . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Frost
content: The Brew of Frost is a throwable potion that explodes into an icy block when it hits anything. If a creature or player is hit, they will be encased in ice. If the ground is hit, a wall of ice will form. If water is hit, it will freeze. Certain creatures hit by the brew may cause unexpected effects. The Brew of Frost is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw an Icy Needle , Glistering Melon , Snowball , Red Mushroom , Water Artichoke Globe and Odour of Purity . Wear a Witches Hat (and robes) while brewing to get a chance of double brewing.

title: Brew of Hollow Tears
content: The Brew of Hollow Tears may be throw to spill the Hollow Tears liquid. It behaves like the opposite of Flowing Spirit, buffing undead and demonic creatures that stand in it and weakening players. Throwing a Solidifying Brew at the liquid will solidify it into another material. Hollow Tears may be picked up in a bucket. Hollow tears are made by distilling Brew of Flowing Spirit with Oil of Vitriol .

title: Brew of Infection
content: The Brew of Infection is a throwable potion that has different infection effects depending on what it hits. If a stone block, cobblestone block or stone brick block is hit, the block will be infected with a silverfish , which will emerge when the block is broken. If the potion hits a villager it will be infected and turn into a zombie villager . If the potion hits any other player or creature, they will be momentarily frozen as they clear the worms off themselves. The Brew of Infection is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Toe of Frog , Creeper Heart , a Wormy Apple , Belladonna Flower , Rotten Flesh and Mutandis . This brew has no chance to get a bonus brew.

title: Brew of Ink
content: The Brew of Ink is a throwable potion that explodes into an explosion of ink when it hits anything, causing blindness to players and monsters in the area of effect. The duration of the blindness is longer the closer to the point of impact. Not all monsters will be affected by blindness, notable exceptions are creatures with no eyes (such as slimes) and powerful demonic entities. The Brew of Ink is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Ink Sac , Quicklime , Oil of Vitriol , Slimeball , Belladonna and Rowan berries . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Love
content: The Brew of Love is a throwable potion that causes farm animals and villagers to get their luvin' on. When used on animals the effect is the same as if all hit animals were fed and their breeding cooldown is reset. In the case of villagers, two (or more) must be hit by the same brew. The Brew of Love may be thrown at a group of two or more enthralled zombies (created with a Brew of Raising or enthralled with the Infernal Infusion power), which will cause pairs of them to produce a baby zombie. It will also cause them to become a zombie villagers. Zombie villagers can be turned back to villagers either using the normal method, or by casting a Rite of Fertility . The Brew of Love is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw a Rose , a Golden Carrot , Whiff of Magic , a Lily Pad , a Water Artichoke Globe and Cocoa Beans . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Raising
content: The Brew of Raising is a throwable potion that will cause undead to raise from the ground where it lands. If the brew hits a wall it will bounce off doing nothing; it can be picked up again if this occurs. The raised undead creatures will not be hostile towards the player who raised them, although otherwise they will behave normally. The raised undead can be ordered to attack using a Necromantic Stone . The Brew of Raising is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Wool of Bat , a Bone , Rotten Flesh , Oil of Vitriol , Redstone and Mutandis . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or Necromancers robes ) while brewing to get more brews than three.

title: Brew of Revealing
content: Brew of Revealing may be thrown to reveal all invisible creatures and players in its area of effect. Brew of Revealing is made in a Kettle using Carrot, Potion of Night Vision (3:00), Spiders' Eyes, Brown Mushroom and Odour of Purity .

title: Brew of Sleeping
content: The Brew of Sleeping is the normal way for a Witch to enter the Spirit World . Needless to say an unprepared witch will almost always experience a nightmare when the head to the spirit world this way. The witch must drink the brew and they will fall into a deep sleep, leaving their body and items behind and entering the world of spirits. Into a kettle, throw Purified Milk , a Cookie , a Brew of Love , Whiff of Magic , an Icy Needle and a Water Artichoke Globe .

title: Brew of Sprouting
content: The Brew of Sprouting is a throwable potion that causes a huge tree branch to grow when it hits a surface. The branch will grow out away from the surface hit, so be careful not to get hit by it!. The Brew of Sprouting is a great way to instantly create bridges, or if thrown at the feet, a rapid way to ascend. The Brew of Sprouting is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Rowan Sapling , Red Flower , Alder Sapling , Mandrake Root , Hawthorn Sapling and Tongue of Dog . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Substitution
content: The Brew of Substitution allows unplaced blocks to be swapped with placed blocks. Simply drop any number of blocks onto the floor near to where you want to work, then throw the brew at the target area. All blocks in the area of effect of the same type as the block hit, will be substituted in a spiral pattern with the blocks that were dropped. This brew is made in a kettle using Ender Dew , and egg, Mutandis Extremis , Magma cream and an Ent Twig .

title: Brew of the Depths
content: The Brew of the Depths is a drinkable potion that allows the drinker to breath underwater for around 5 minutes. The think to beware of is that for the duration the drinker will no longer be able to breath air ! This effect will be removed only when it expires or the drinker dies. Milk does not clear the effect . The Brew of the Depths is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Mandrake Root , Tear of the Goddess , Water Artichoke Globe , Lily pad , Odour of Purity and an Ink Sac . Wear a Witches Hat (and robes) while brewing to get a chance of double brewing.

title: Brew of the Grotesque
content: The Brew of the Grotesque must be drunk to have an effect. After it has been drunk all creatures (with the exception of players, dragons, demons, golems and witches) will be so horrified by what they see, that they will not be able to get very close to the player who drank the brew. The brew's effects last for five minutes. To create the brew, into a kettle, throw Mutandis Extremis , Mandrake Root , a Water Artichoke Globe , a Golden Apple and a Poisonous Potato . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Thorns
content: The Brew of Thorns is a throwable potion that will cause a four block high cactus to grow where it lands. If the brew hits a creature it will instead surround the creature with one block high cacti. If the brew hits a wall it will bounce off doing nothing; it can be picked up again if this occurs. Hitting a cactus with the brew will cause it to grow higher! The Brew of Thorns is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Cactus Green , Brown Mushroom , Oil of Vitriol , Odour of Purity , Red Flower and Mandrake Root . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Vines
content: The Brew of Vines is a throwable potion that causes a vine to instantly grow up (and down) the vertical surface it hits. This brew is an excellent way to quickly ascend or descend a vertical wall. The vines can climb or descend step formations in rock walls so long as they do not move back more than one block per block risen. This means that a sheer wall that ascends six blocks then there is a step and then another ascent of six blocks will be scaled by a single brew of vines. Having a toad familiar increases this to two blocks distance. Vines will tunnel though leaves, allowing an easy way to ascend most trees. If the brew does not hit a vertical surface, it will bounce off and can be picked up again. The Brew of Vines is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Add to the kettle Vines , Brown Mushroom , Red Mushroom , Tongue of Dog , Wheat and Reek of Misfortune . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brew of Wasting
content: The Brew of Wasting is a throwable potion that will cause anything in its area of effect to waste away. Foliage will dissolve (dropping anything they normally do when bashed). Monsters are withered and players get very hungry indeed. The Brew is made in a kettle using, Mellifluous Hunger , Ember Moss , Rotten Flesh , a Poisonous Potato , Belladonna Flower and a Spider's Eye .

title: Brew of Webs
content: The Brew of Webs is a throwable potion that explodes into a mass tangle of webs when it hits anything. This brew is the perfect way to stop a pursuer dead in its tracks, or to create an instant landing pad when jumping down from high places. The Brew of Webs is created in a kettle using the recipe found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Dense Web , Red Mushroom , Wool of Bat , Yellow Flower , Whiff of Magic and Belladonna . Wear a Witches Hat (and/or robes ) while brewing to get more brews than just three.

title: Brewing
content: Brewing is perhaps what witches (and warlocks) are most famous for, and there are certainly a few options beyond the normal potions available to the would-be practitioner. This article covers brewing in a kettle, it is also possible to make custom brews in a Witches' Cauldron in Witchery 0.21 and above. Creating a brew in a Kettle A kettle must be set up in the correct way, before it may be used. Firstly, a fire must be set under the kettle ( netherrack is best for this, but wood will do just as well). Next the kettle must be filled by using a Water-filled Bucket on it. Thirdly, each of the six ingredients for the brew or infusion must be thrown into the kettle, one at a time (the liquid will change color according to the recipe being used). Finally, a glass bottle must be used on the kettle to bottle the resulting brew, this must be done multiple times depending on the recipe. It is important to keep the fire going constantly while the recipe is being prepared, otherwise it will be ruined. Swirls coming from the kettle is a sign all is well, black smoke (and brown liquid) indicates the brew is ruined. When a brew is ruined, the kettle must be emptied with a glass bottle , or just filled with more water, and the whole process repeated. When a brew is complete, the liquid will be the color of the resulting brew and will splutter (in addition to white swirls). Some recipes will require magical energy from a nearby Altar before they are bottled. If the brew is bottled when insufficient energy is available, the brew will be ruined. Black swirls coming from the Kettle is a good sign there is not enough power. A kettle must be within 14 (to 16) blocks of an Altar in order to obtain power from it (it can obtain power only from a single altar). Witches Hats, Robes and Other buffs If a Witches Hat is worn while brewing, the recipe for a brew will yield an additional potion. Infusions and Redstone Soup will not get a benefit. Witches robes will also increase the chance of an additional brew not connected with necromancy (there Necromancers Robes will help). Having a Toad familiar also increases brewing bonuses. Brew recipes The following brews can be created, the recipes for which can be found in the book, Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions : Brew Effect Brew of Thorns Instantly grow a cactus where the brew hits, or surround the hit mob with cacti Brew of Vines Instantly grow a vine up and down a surface Brew of Webs Cause an explosion of webs where the brew hits Brew of Ink A splash potion that causes blindness to most creatures and players Brew of Sprouting Instantly grow a tree branch from the surface hit; can be ridden upwards Brew of Erosion Dissolve blocks, damage creatures and players (melt their armor and weapons) Brew of Raising Raise undead creatures from the ground Brew of Love Causes hit creatures to get their luvin' on Brew of the Grotesque Creatures cannot approach too close Brew of Frost Causes creatures to be encased in ice, freezes water and creates ice walls Brew of the Depths Drink to breath underwater, beware you will no longer be able to breath air though Brew of Infection Infects stone with silverfish, villagers become zombies and stalls other creatures Brew of Sleeping Sends a player into a deep sleep allowing them to enter the spirit world Brew of Flowing Spirit Captured essence of the Spirit World (only brewable in the Spirit World ) Brew of Hollow Tears What is left when the good and bad properties of Flowing Spirit are removed Brew of Wasting Causes foliage to wilt away and creatures to die of hunger Brew of Bats Explodes into a swam of bats, stunning nearby creatures Brew of Substitution Replaces blocks in its area of effect with items nearby Brew of Revealing Reveals invisible players and creatures Solidifying Brews Solidifies Hollow Tears to dirt, stone, sand, sandstone or erodes it. Infused Brew Base The base of Infused Brews Infused Brew of Soaring Allows better control of an Enchanted Broom (stacks with an Owl familiar) Infused Brew of the Grave Allows better control of a necromantic stone (and feasting on dead creatures) Brew of Frog's Tongue Pulls all creatures in the splash area towards the thrower Brew of Bodega Owls descend and attack the hit creature Brew of Cursed Leaping All creatures in the are of effect get a jump buff, but are also thrown up quite high Redstone Soup The base for infusions , also has a regeneration effect if drunk

title: Broom
content: The broom is a simple instrument used to sweep floors, it is also the base ingredient for an Enchanted Broom . Hitting a glyph block drawn with chalk while holding a broom, will remove the glyph instantly rather than taking a few seconds. A broom is crafted using Sticks and Hawthorn Saplings .

title: Candelabra
content: The candelabra is an upgrade for an altar that improves its recharge rate. The chalice will increase the recharge rate by 2x the base recharge rate. The candelabra is an upgrade to a torch. Only one torch upgrade will have an effect on an altar . A candelabra is crafted with Iron Ingots , Torches and an Attuned Stone .

title: Cane Sword
content: A Cane Sword is essentially cane with a sword hidden inside of it, used by vampires to further their dual nature. Normal on the outside, and a monster within. A Vampire's Cane sword contains a small reservoir that will collect the blood of any suitable victim. This extra blood pool, can be consumed by the vampire when needed. When drawn, the cane sword has the same damage and other properties as a diamond sword. When sheathed, it will only do 1 point of damage per hit and cannot block. It is repaired with diamond. To draw the sword, the player should sneak-use (Shift Right-Click) the Cane Sword. When deployed, the sword behaves like a normal sword. To Sheathe a drawn sword, the player should sneak-use (Shift Right-Click a second time). To consume any collected blood, the player should use (Right-Click) the Cane Sword when it is Sheathed. A Cane Sword is made from Woven Cruor , a diamond sword and a gold ingot.

title: Chalice
content: The chalice is an upgrade for an altar that improves the maximum amount of power an altar can hold. The chalice will increase the maximum power by 1x the base power rate. The chalice upgrades to the Filled Chalice . Only one chalice upgrade will have an effect on an altar . A chalice is crafted with Gold Ingots , Gold Nuggets and an Attuned Stone .

title: Charged Attuned Stone
content: An attuned stone that has been charged makes an excellent portable power source for use in Circle Magic rites. Rites that can use charged attuned stones can be found in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic. To charge an attuned stone , one must use the Rite of Charging which can be found also in the pages of the Witchcraft: Circle Magic book.

title: Charm of Fanciful Thoughts
content: The Charm of Fanciful Thoughts is the only way to protect oneself from some of the worst effects a nightmare can throw at you. It just needs to be in a players inventory for it to function. A Charm of Fanciful Thoughts is crafted using Sticks and Fanciful Thread .

title: Circle Magic
content: A magic circle is circle or sphere of space marked out by practitioners of many branches of ritual magic, which will contain energy and form a sacred space, or will provide the user with a form of magical protection, or both. The circle is marked physically, drawn with a special type of chalk to a size appropriate to the ritual that is to be performed. Foci shape the magic associated with the ritual that will be drawn from a nearby altar and are often consumed in the process. Rituals are varied, although they are typically organized into rites associated with an aspect of one of the dimensions and the foci used are most likely related to this association. The altar An altar is a focal point to concentrate the magic present in the surrounding area into single usable source. Since witchcraft is a method to harness the power of nature, placing an altar in a location with little or no living things will yield very little magic to work with. There is a reason why most witches huts are found deep in dark forests or swamps! Certain objects, placed onto an altar, may boost the focusing capabilities of an altar, either improving the amount of magic it can focus, or the rate at which it replenishes. Drawing a circle A circle is made up of magical glyphs drawn using a special type of chalk. The type of chalk used influences the properties of the glyph and therefore the types of rites that may be performed with a specific circle. It is important to remember that a circle must always be drawn with the same type of chalk. Golden Chalk Ritual Chalk Otherwhere Chalk Infernal Chalk At the center of a circle, a heart glyph is always drawn using Golden Chalk . Activating this central glyph is what begins a rite. A heart glyph may be activated either by interacting with it or by giving it a redstone signal. Some more complex rites may require large circles, or in the most complex cases, multiple circles drawn inside one another. When drawing circles of different sizes around a central heart glyph there is no restriction on the type of glyph used for each complete circle. The three sizes of circle are 7x7, 11x11 and 15x15, the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic contains images of the necessary patterns. The image above shows the 7x7 pattern. The image in the section Combining Rites below, shows all three circle patterns. Performing a rite and the Book of Rites All rite are found in the book entitled Witchcraft: Circle Magic , which must be crafted by the player. Once a rite has been selected from the book: The circle (or circles) shown in the book, must be drawn with the correct chalk. The items listed in the rite must be placed within the bounds of the circle. A nearby Altar must have sufficient power stored within it (some portable rites do not require power from an altar, they use charged attuned stones instead). A rite can use power only from a single altar , the closest altar will always be preferred. Altars must be within 14 (to 16) blocks of the heart glyph. Finally the heart glyph must be activated (right-click on it once) to begin the ritual, then wait. The rite will now begin with the items being consumed one after the other (an Arthana will not be consumed, and a charged attuned stone will lose its charge when used in a rite). Once the last item is consumed, power will be drained from the Altar (if necessary). Finally the rite's effect will begin. There is about a one second gap between each step. Grassper plants may also be placed within a circle and any items that they hold can be consumed instead of dropping the item on the floor. In this way, automation of rites may be possible. Combining rites Where multiple rites use compatible circles (either the same size and colored circles, or differing sizes), the foci objects for multiple rites can be laid out. Upon activation of the heart glyph the rites will be performed in sequence. The order in which the rites are performed will generally follow the order the rites are listed in the Witchcraft: Circle Magic book. In the image on the right, a fully upgraded altar and three circles can be used for a combined rite to erect a barrier, transpose a villager and use the villager to summon a demon. This combined rite uses the Rite of Protection , Rite of Transposition and Rite of Summoning together. Binding a circle to a Circle Talisman A circle talisman allows one or more circles to be bound into the talisman, allowing the circles to be easily transported and recreated when the talisman is later used. Use the Rite of Binding described in Witchcraft: Circle Magic , with an empty circle talisman to bind the circles to the talisman. All circles and the heart glyph will be moved into the talisman. When the talisman is used on a block, all contained circles will be placed into the world centered on the block. Covens Circle magic can be made stronger if more witches from the same coven are involved in performing a rite. If a witch or warlock wants to use their coven to perform a rite, they must use a Seer Stone to activate the heart glyph, instead of just activating it normally (which will continue to perform the normal version of the Rite). Once the ritual is started with a Seer Stone , the members of the coven will appear one-by-one and then the ritual will begin normally. Rites that have an area of effect will be larger. Curses and counter curses will start at a higher level. There are also some rites that can only be performed by a group of witches. These rites, also become more powerful the larger the coven. Some are not even possible before a large coven is assembled. To build a coven, a witch (or warlock) of sufficient skill (having a familiar helps), must find witches in the world and talk them into joining their coven. Such witches will often require a task to be fulfilled. Note that the really bad witches that live in the huts in swamps will never join a coven, so don't even bother. Using waystones to redirect rites Some rites can use an optional Bound Waystone to send the effects of the rite to the location of that waystone. When a waystone is added to a compatible rite it must not exceed the maximum range of the coven performing the rite. A rite has a maximum range of 50 blocks plus an additional 50 blocks per coven witch involved in the rite (to a maximum of 350 blocks). Refer to the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic to find out which rites support Waystones in this way. Rites All of the necessary steps to perform the following rites can be found in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic . Rite Effect Rite of Binding Binds something to an item (the circles, the location, etc.) Rite of Charging Charges an item or the player Rite of Glyphic Transformation Changes the type of glyphs in one of the circles around the ritual Rite of Infusion Infuses an infusion into a player or item Rite of Summoning Summons spectral and infernal beings Rite of Protection Creates an impenetrable dome Rite of Imprisonment Prevents creatures leaving the circle Rite of Sanctity Prevents creatures entering the circle Rite of Icy Expansion Causes a large sphere of ice to expand outwards (filled with air) [requires 2 additional witches] Rite of Broken Earth Causes the earth to rupture, creating a fissure in a defined direction Rite of Moving Earth Raises a column of earth Rite of Earth's Wrath Causes a volcano to erupt Rite of Sky's Wrath Causes a thunderstorm after a lot of focused lightning Rite of Broiling Cooks food placed in the circle. May overcook some of it Rite of Transposition Teleports players, creatures and items in various ways Rite of Manifestation Allows a player to manifest as a ghost by passing through Spirit Portals in the Spirit World Rite of Beastial Call Transposes animals from a wide area around to the heart glyph [requires 3 additional witches] Rite of Total Eclipse Causes nighttime to descend Rite of Necromancy Create a Necromantic Stone to control raised undead Rite of Banishing Sends demons back whence they came Rite of Fertility Makes land in a wide are receive a dose of bone meal, heals zombie villagers and players Rite of Nature's Power Terraforms the immediate vicinity, turning desert and caves into grottoes Rite of the Forest Grows a forest at the target location based on the sapling used. Rite of Shifting Seasons Changes the biome, and can cause snow or melting [requires 4 additional witches] Rite of Remove Curse Removes a Curse of Misfortune, has a chance to make things worse Curse of Misfortune Causes periodic random effects to inflict the victim, can only be removed with magic Curse of Insanity Causes the victim to see and hear imaginary monsters Curse of Sinking Causes the victim to sink faster and surface slower in water Curse of Overheating Causes the victim to occasionally catch fire in hot places Curse of Waking Nightmare Cuases the victim to be visited by their nightmares Curse of Corrupt Poppet Destroys Voodoo protection poppets bound to the victim Curse of Blindness Blinds creatures and players in a wide area Curse of Blight Kills plants and animals, infects villagers, causes nausea in players Curse of Hell on Earth Allows creatures from the nether to pour into the overworld Curse of Raining Toads Causes toads to rain down and explode into a poisonous fog [requires 1 additional witch] Curse of the Wolf Causes a player of Villager to contract lycanthropy [requires 6 additional witches, a full moon and a cat familiar]

title: Circle Talisman
content: A circle talisman allows a pattern of glyphs drawn onto the ground to be bound instead to the talisman (the glyphs will disappear) and later be redrawn at a new location when then talisman is used. This makes it very easy for the witch or wizard to prepare time consuming circles in advance for rapid deployment later. To bind a circle talisman perform the Rite of Binding described in Witchcraft: Circle Magic with any pattern of circles. To deploy a bound talisman, right-click on the ground in a place with enough space to contain the pattern. You will hear a fail sound if there are obstructions and the circles cannot be placed. A circle talisman is crafted using a Diamond , Gold Ingots and Gold Nuggets .

title: Clay Jar
content: A clay jar is a useful receptacle for holding sometimes unstable materials. Clay jars are made by placing a Soft Clay Jar into a furnace. The jars are most often used in ovens and distilleries to collect materials produced in their respective operation.

title: Coffin
content: A coffin can be used by vampires to sleep in. They differ from normal beds, for they allow the vampire player to sleep thought the day. In order to sleep in a coffin, the player must first open it, by sneak-using it (shift right-click), and then right clicking on it as normal. Sleeping in a coffin sets the player's spawn point to the location of the coffin. Taglock Kits work on a coffin in the same way as they do other beds. Sleeping though the day on a multiplayer server, requires all players to sleep. A vampire may sleep in a bed to sleep though the night as usual on a mlutiplayer server. A coffin is made from a bed, wood logs and wooden planks.

title: Concentrated Bat Ball
content: A Concentrated Bat Ball is a highly condensed amalgamation of bats and slime. It is an ingredient used in the production of the Brew of Bats. A concentrated Bat Ball can be thrown quite a long way by just using it (right-click), possibly throwing it to someone else. If a player is hit with a Concentrated Bat Ball in their inventory, they will drop it. A Concentrated Bat Ball is crafted with Critter snares containing trapped bats and slimeballs .

title: Condensed Fear
content: Condensed Fear is an ingredient used in other recipes, it is created by distilling Brew of Flowing Spirit with Oil of Vitriol .

title: Congealed Spirit
content: Congealed Spirit is an ingredient used to make a Spectral Stone , it is made in a Kettle using Subdued Spirit and Brew of Hollow Tears .

title: Cooked Lambchop
content: Cooked Lambchop is a food item that is obtained by cooking Raw Lambchop . The Cooked Lambchop may be eaten to restore some lost hunger.

title: Coven Witch
content: Witches that have not completely turned to evil can often be encountered in the forests and villages of the world. These witches will often recognize other practitioners of the Art, and may give a task to them. Successful completion of this task will result in the witch joining the players coven. Witches in a coven can be called upon to help out in a fight, by using a Seer Stone . Coven witches will occasionally appear and potter around when the player is at home. A player's coven may be called upon to perform Circle Magic , by using a Seer Stone to activate the Heart Glyph.

title: Credits & Licenses
content: This mod features: The song, Loved (ft. Mykle Anthony) by wellman. Licensed to the public under http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/3.0/. Verify at http://ccmixter.org/files/wellman/24289. Adapted version of the sound bite lunch_of_monsters(bones) from http://www.freesound.org/people/Taira%20Komori/sounds/216920/. Sound effects licensed from FreeSFX (http://www.freesfx.co.uk).

title: Creeper Heart
content: A creeper heart is a very rare drop when a creeper is killed. The chance can be increased by killing a creeper using an Arthana (especially with the Looting enchant). Creeper hearts are used to make Witches Robes . A Creeper Heart can be placed into a crafting table to produce Gunpowder. Eating a Creeper heart can have explosive consequences!

title: Critter Snare
content: A Critter Snare is a plant that traps small creatures such as silverfish, bats and small slimes that move into it. Creature can be released by right-clicking while sneaking (you may have to have an empty hand to avoid placing the held object) on the snare. Snares can be broken and moved with the creature still in it. Snares containing caught critters are often used in mutation recipes. Four Critter Snares are created by placing a spider web over still water and getting a zombie to be trapped in the web. The web must be surrounding on four sides by Alder Saplings . Use a Mutating Sprig on the spider web to mutate the saplings into Critter Snares.

title: Crystal Ball Step-by-Step
content: To create a Crystal Ball , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 2000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Happenstance Oil in the kettle Go to the nether to collect quartz and make a Quartz Sphere Get a gold ingot Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Ritual Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Ritual Chalk Drop the Happenstance Oil , Quartz Sphere and Gold Ingot in the middle circle, check there is still 2000 power in the altar Wait until night time Activate the heart glyph and wait for the Crystal Ball to appear

title: Crystal Ball
content: A crystal ball is a crystal or glass ball believed by some people to aid in the performance of clairvoyance. Holding a Taglock Kit containing the taglocks of another player or creature will allow the witch (or warlock) to see through the eyes of that being, so long as they are not too far away (within 100 - 150 blocks). Holding a Taglock Kit containing their own taglocks will allow the witch to view the immediate area from an increasingly wide bird's eye perspective. Holding a Bound Waystone will allow the witch to view the area around the waystone from an increasingly wide bird's eye perspective, so long as that location is not too far away (within 100 - 150 blocks). The crystal ball may also be used without a Taglock Kit to glimpse into the future of the player (or if another player is nearby, into their future). Predictions made by the player when looking into the ball may be positive or negative, but they will happen. Using such powerful magic concerning fate may attract unwanted attention however. If reading the future of another nearby player, the closest other player to the Crystal Ball will be the one who is read. To predict the future, a crystal ball requires power from an altar. Only people who have made a crystal ball will be able to use one to predict the future. A crystal ball is made combining a Quartz Sphere , Gold Ingot and Happenstance Oil in the Rite of Infusion .

title: Curse of Blight
content: The Curse of Blight is a terrible thing to inflict. In a very wide are of effect, plants and animals will die, earth will decay to sand, villagers will become ill with the zombie plague and players will become nauseous. A Voodoo Protection Poppet held by a player in the area of effect will stop the curse immediately unless this was cast by a witch with a Cat familiar , in which case two undamaged poppets will be needed to stop the curse. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Blindness
content: The curse of blindness is very dark magic, it will cause all creatures an players in a very wide area to become blind. A Voodoo Protection Poppet held by a player in the area of effect will stop the curse immediately unless this was cast by a witch with a Cat familiar , in which case two undamaged poppets will be needed to stop the curse. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Corrupt Poppet
content: The Curse of Corrupt Poppet is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player or creature. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Poppet Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will destroy up to ten active Voodoo protection poppets bound to the taglocked player. This curse is used by a witch or warlock to clear the way for other curses. The Curse of Corrupt Poppet can only be performed if the player has an active Cat Familiar . The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Hell on Earth
content: The Curse of Hell on Earth opens a portal to the Nether and welcomes its denizens to the overworld. Every few seconds for the duration of the curse, a hellish creature will come through the portal. This rite is extremely expensive to perform. If performed by a witch with a cat familiar a wave of fire will accompany the curse. This can be disabled in the Witchery config file because it can cause massive server lag if fire spread is not reduced. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Insanity
content: The Curse of Insanity is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Voodoo Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will periodically make the player see monsters that do not really exist, until such a time as the curse is removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . To be clear, this curse will continue to apply even after death. The strength of the curse is largely down to the skill of the witch or warlock casting the curse. Also, each time a curse fails to be removed, it will get progressively stronger. The stronger the curse the more monsters will be seen. The Curse of Insanity can only be performed in a thunderstorm! This curse will start one level stronger if cast by a witch (or warlock) with a Cat familiar . It will also require an undamaged Voodoo Protection poppet to stop it in this case. If a coven performs the curse, it will start one level stronger for every two additional witches in the coven. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Misfortune
content: The Curse of Misfortune is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player or creature. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Voodoo Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will periodically apply random debilitating effects to its victim until such a time as the curse is removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . To be clear, this curse will continue to apply even after death. The strength (therefore the variety, power and duration) of the debilitating effects is largely down to the skill of the witch or warlock casting the curse. Also, each time a curse fails to be removed, it will get progressively stronger. Effects observed to-date include slowness, weakness, blindness, sickness and fumbling. The Curse of Misfortune can only be performed in a thunderstorm! This curse will start one level stronger if cast by a witch (or warlock) with a Cat familiar . It will also require an undamaged Voodoo Protection poppet to stop it in this case. If a coven performs the curse, it will start one level stronger for every two additional witches in the coven. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Overheating
content: The Curse of Overheating is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Voodoo Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will cause a player in a hot biome to occasionally catch fire for a short period, until such a time as the curse is removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . To be clear, this curse will continue to apply even after death. The strength of the curse is largely down to the skill of the witch or warlock casting the curse. Also, each time a curse fails to be removed, it will get progressively stronger. The stronger the curse the higher the chance of the player catching fire, and the longer the fire will burn. The Curse of Overheating can only be performed in a thunderstorm! This curse will start one level stronger if cast by a witch (or warlock) with a Cat familiar . It will also require an undamaged Voodoo Protection poppet to stop it in this case. If a coven performs the curse, it will start one level stronger for every two additional witches in the coven. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Raining Toads
content: The Curse of Raining Toads will cause it to start raining and toads to begin dropping from the sky. The toads will explode into a poisonous mist after a short time. This rite must be performed with at least 1 additional witch. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Sinking
content: The Curse of Sinking is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Voodoo Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will cause a player in water to sink faster and take longer to swim to the surface, until such a time as the curse is removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . Being heavy can cause other problems too! To be clear, this curse will continue to apply even after death. The strength of the curse is largely down to the skill of the witch or warlock casting the curse. Also, each time a curse fails to be removed, it will get progressively stronger. The stronger the curse the faster a player will sink. The Curse of Sinking can only be performed in a thunderstorm! This curse will start one level stronger if cast by a witch (or warlock) with a Cat familiar . It will also require an undamaged Voodoo Protection poppet to stop it in this case. If a coven performs the curse, it will start one level stronger for every two additional witches in the coven. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of the Wolf
content: The Curse of the Wolf is a targeted curse that will afflict a single player. A taglock must first be collected using a Taglock Kit , and that must then be used as a foci in the rite to target the being to whom the taglock belongs. A Voodoo Protection Poppet bound to a player will stop the curse from taking effect. When the curse takes hold, it will cause a player to be infected with lycanthropy , until such a time as the curse is removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . To be clear, this curse will continue to apply even after death. The curse of the Wolf is one of the most powerful curses that are currently known, and requires a full coven of six other witches to perform, in addition to a Cat familiar . The Curse of the Wolf can only be performed at night under a full moon! The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Curse of Waking Nightmare
content: The Curse of Waking Nightmare is a directed curse that requires the Taglock of the intended target to be used in the rite. The victim of the curse will periodically see their Nightmare manifest before them. Nightmares in the overworld are much weaker than in the dream world. Beware, this curse requires 10000 altar power to cast! The curse can be removed with a Rite of Remove Curse . Higher levels of this curse increase the frequency at which nightmares are experienced. The necessary circles and foci items for this curse can be found in the book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic .

title: Death Protection Poppet
content: The death protection poppet will redirect the death (irrespective of cause) of the bound player or creature to the poppet, destroying it in the process. This poppet is the only way to prevent certain types of death. The poppet will also give a short buff against the cause of death (fire, drowning, etc.) and heal a bit of life, allowing the player a small amount of breathing space to get out of trouble. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. The poppet is crafted using a Poppet , Gold Nuggets , Diamond Vapor and a Drop of Luck .

title: Death
content: Do not look onto death, for he may see you! Death can be summoned by attempting to bind five of each spectral undead to a fetish. Death may drop the Hand of Death , Death's Hood , Death's Robes , Death's Footwear or Binky's Skull when defeated. If a player is wearing all of Death's clothes and wielding the Hand of Death , they may be summoned when death is called.

title: Deathly Veil
content: Deathly Veil is an effect produced with a Brazier , it makes creatures near to the brazier invisible so long as the brazier burns. To produce this effect, add Ender Pearl, Spider Eye and Blaze Rod to a Brazier and light with a Flint & Tinder. The brazier requires Altar power.

title: Death's Footwear
content: Part of Death 's accoutrements, Death's Footwear changes the wearer's legs and feet to resemble that of Death. Death's Footwear has the same armor value as Iron armor but with the durability of Diamond armor. It can be repaired in an Anvil using Iron Ingots. While wearing Death's Footwear the player can walk on water and other liquids. This ability is precarious since it requires concentration, getting hit may result in the magic being temporarily broken. Death's Footwear is a possible drop each time Death is defeated. Wearing all Death's accoutrements and wielding the Hand of Death can result in the player being summoned in place of Death when death is called.

title: Death's Hood
content: Part of Death 's accoutrements, Death's Hood changes the wearer's face to resemble that of Death. Death's Hood has the same armor value as Iron armor but with the durability of Diamond armor. It can be repaired in an Anvil using Iron Ingots. If the wearer stares at a creature or player, that being will feel Death's gaze and find it much harder to move (this feature can be disable din the config file). Also the wearer will find it much easier to see in dark places. Death's Hood is a possible drop each time Death is defeated. Wearing all Death's accoutrements and wielding the Hand of Death can result in the player being summoned in place of Death when death is called.

title: Death's Robes
content: Part of Death 's clothing, Death's Robe changes the wearer's torso and arms to resemble that of Death. Death's Robe has the same armor value as Iron armor but with the durability of Diamond armor. It can be repaired in an Anvil using Iron Ingots. While wearing Death's Robe the player is immune to fire and lava. Death's Robe is a possible drop each time Death is defeated. Wearing all Death's clothing and wielding the Hand of Death can result in the player being summoned in place of Death when death is called. Wearing all of deaths clothing will also cap incoming damage to 7 points.

title: Demon Heart
content: The heart of a demon is a powerful and entirely evil thing; it is the main ingredient when creating an Infernal infusion . A demon heart can also be eaten, to grant the powers of a demon for a short period of time. Beware however, that the human form is not designed to hold such power, nor cope with with the constant hunger that demons possess. Be especially cautious when the effects wear off! A demon heart can also be placed in the world by using it while sneaking (Right-click with shift key held). A placed demon heart provides power for an Altar and can also influence dreaming . A demon heart is acquired either though bargaining with a demon , or by killing one. To maximize the chances of a demon dropping a heart when it dies, the killing blow should be made with an arthana .

title: Demon
content: Demons are powerful, evil beings who dwell in the infernal dimensions. To date, none have been seen roaming the Nether, where their minions: ghasts, blazes and their ilk seem to dominate. To meet a demon without a way to protect oneself is almost certain death; they are known to hit like a proverbial truck, and require a significant amount of damage to kill. Some demons have even been seen shooting fireballs! They are, of course, also immune to fire themselves.
