Why then, you may ask, would someone who has studied the rites in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic , even consider trying to summon one of these terrible creatures with the Rite of Summoning ? Because of what they can offer as a deal when they have no other choice. Trapped demons, when interacted with, may offer powerful magical knowledge or rare ingredients for a small price. Indeed, bargaining with a demon is one of only two ways to obtain a Demon Heart ; the main ingredient for an Infernal infusion . The other way is of course to slay a demon (preferably with an arthana ). Beware of making bargains with demons... they may try to trick you!

title: Demonic Contract
content: A demonic contract is written on parchment made for the resurrected flesh of the dead. The contract is signed in the blood of the individual to whom it pertains. A plain demonic contract is used to make a deal with Flame Imps . The basic contract is crafted with a Taglock Kit containing the blood of the witch (or warlock). When performing Imp Magic , a plain contract is first crafted with reagents pertaining to the spell effect, and then signed in the blood of the target by crafting it with a Taglock Kit containing the targets blood. The basic contract is crafted in a crafting bench using Paper, String and Raw Porkchop?

title: Dense Web
content: Dense Web is an ingredient used in the kettle recipe for a Brew of Webs . It is crafted using string and a cobweb (cobweb can be harvested either using an Arthana or a Silk Touch enchanted tool).

title: Devils Tongue Charm
content: The Devils Tongue Charm is a variation of the Polynesia Charm that can even charm Demons . The Devils Tongue Charm has all the properties of a Polynesia Charm (in that it allows trading with animals), but when held, Demons will be significantly less likely to attack. Also when bargaining, a demon is likely to reduce its prices if a Devils Tongue Charm is held in the players hand. Each demonic trade will damage the charm (it has about 10 uses for trading). Beware when it expires! Please note: Due to a glitch, the damage bar does not change while in the trading screen, beware how many trades have been made! The Devils Tongue Charm is crafted with a Polynesia Charm , a Skull , Tongue of Dog , Blaze Powder , Refined Evil and a Demon Heart .

title: Diamond Vapor
content: Diamond vapor is an ingredient made by distilling diamond in a distillery according to the recipe in the book, Witchcraft: Distilling , using a Diamond and Oil of Vitriol .

title: Disorientation Fetish
content: A Disorientation Fetish causes creatures who near it to become confused, behaving erratically possibly even assaulting their friends, or just wandering off in random directions. Players unarmed and unarmored will not be perceived as a threat and will be unaffected by the fetish. A Disorientation Fetish is created by binding 3 spirits , and 2 poltergeists to a fetish object using the Rite of Binding .

title: Distillery
content: A distillery used by a witch is a construct used for distilling magical ingredients. It is unlike a normal distillery, because it heats itself using energy obtained from a nearby altar , the distillery must be within about 14 (to 16) blocks of an altar to draw power from it, a small icon is shown in the interface if no power is available. One or two ingredients are distilled together and will result in up to four distillates; or resulting materials. A supply of clay jars is also needed to capture the some or all of the distillates. A distillery is crafted using Iron Ingots , Gold Ingots , an Attuned Stone and Clay jars . Distillery recipes All of the distillery recipes can be found in the book, Witchcraft: Distilling . Although distillation is the only way to produce Glowstone Dust , Gypsum , Oil of Vitriol , Diamond Vapor , Refined Evil , Ender Dew and Tear of the Goddess .

title: Disturbed Cotton
content: Disturbed Cotton is found by harvesting Wispy Cotton in the Spirit World when one or more players are experiencing nightmares (i.e. the world is dark). Disturbed Cotton can be spun into Tormented Twine . It cannot be replanted.

title: Drain Growth
content: Drain Growth is an effect produced with a Brazier , it causes all crops standing near to the burning brazier to shrivel, with the energy gained from them allow the brazier to burn longer. Any undead creatures near to the brazier (including witches under the influence of the Infused Brew of the Grave) will be healed each time a crop is withered. This effect does not require the Brazier to be near an Altar. To produce this effect, add Condensed Fear , Wormy Apple and Graveyard Dust to a Brazier and light with a Flint & Tinder.

title: Dream Weaver of Fasting
content: The Dream Weaver of Fasting will add a boost to the saturation of a player when they wake up from a good nights sleep, essentially meaning they will not get hungry for the duration of the effect. If the dream weaver is corrupted by a nearby Dream Weaver of Nightmares , the weaver will instead add a hunger effect to the player. The dream weaver is crafted using an Item Frame , Fanciful Thread , Tormented Twine , Feathers , Diamond Vapor , and Splash Potion of Healing II and Mellifluous Hunger .

title: Dream Weaver of Fleet Foot
content: The Dream Weaver of Fleet Foot will add a speed boost to a player when they wake up from a good nights sleep. If the dream weaver is corrupted by a nearby Dream Weaver of Nightmares , the weaver will instead add a slowdown effect to the player. The dream weaver is crafted using an Item Frame , Fanciful Thread , Tormented Twine , Feathers , Diamond Vapor , and Long-Duration Splash Potions of Swiftness and Slowness .

title: Dream Weaver of Intensity
content: The Dream Weaver of Intensity has two effects, firstly it will add a very short night vision effect (or blindness if corrupted by a nightmare) to a player when they wake up from a good nights sleep. Secondly, it will intensify all other nearby dream weavers so that they have either a stronger (but shorter lasting) effect or their duration is increased. The dream weaver is crafted using an Item Frame , Fanciful Thread , Tormented Twine , Feathers , Diamond Vapor , Brew of Flowing Spirit and Brew of Sleeping .

title: Dream Weaver of Iron Arm
content: The Dream Weaver of Iron Arm will add a mining speed boost to a player when they wake up from a good nights sleep. If the dream weaver is corrupted by a nearby Dream Weaver of Nightmares , the weaver will instead add a mining speed slowdown effect to the player. The dream weaver is crafted using an Item Frame , Fanciful Thread , Tormented Twine , Feathers , Diamond Vapor , and Long-Duration Splash Potions of Strength and Weakness .

title: Dream Weaver of Nightmares
content: The Dream Weaver of Nightmares has two effects, firstly it will add a short weakness effect to a player when they wake up from a good nights sleep. Secondly, it will corrupt all other nearby dream weavers so that they have the opposite effect. If the dream weaver is itself corrupted by another nearby Dream Weaver of Nightmares, it will add a short blindness effect instead of nausea. The Dream Weaver of Nightmares can also be used to fend off nightmares when a Brew of Sleeping is drunk. Having the Dream Weaver nearby will reduce the chance of a nightmare to 50% (this can be decreased further by other means!) The dream weaver is crafted using an Item Frame , Tormented Twine , Feathers , Diamond Vapor , and Long-Duration Splash Potions of Night Vision and Poison .

title: Dream Weaving
content: A dreamcatcher, or dream snare, is a handmade object normally made from a wooden loop, on which is woven a loose net or web of imbued sinew. The dreamcatcher is then decorated with sacred items such as feathers and beads. The resulting "dream catcher", hung above the bed, is used as a charm to protect sleeping people from nightmares by sifting the sleepers dreams and in some cases weaving them into something new and beneficial to the dreamer. But beware, there is but a fine line between dreams and nightmares, and a less scrupulous practitioner of the art might find ways to sieve the dreams to leave only nightmares. This is not a easy skill to learn, first-hand experience of the Spirit World is required to manipulate dreams through the crafting of dream catchers. Crafting Dream Catchers and the Spirit World Creating dream catchers requires the use of special strings that are spun from Wispy Cotton . This plant only grows in the Spirit World , a place than can only be reached while sleeping (and only then with the aid of a Brew of Sleeping ). The necessary string is then spun from the cotton using a Spinning Wheel . A comprehensive understanding of the Spirit World is necessary, especially the nightmarish-side, to successfully create Dream Catchers. Using dream catchers Dream catchers must be placed on the wall near to the players bed. When the player sleeps though the night and wakes the effect associated with the dream catcher will then be applied as a buff to the player. Dream Catcher Effect Dream Weaver of Fleet Foot Adds a movement speed buff on waking Dream Weaver of Iron Arm Adds a dig speed buff on waking Dream Weaver of Fasting Adds a hunger prevention buff on waking Dream Weaver of Intensity Makes other weaver effects more potent Causing nightmares A Dream Weaver of Nightmares placed near to the bed of a sleeping player will have two effects, firstly when the player wakes they will have a short period of nausea. Secondly, any other dream weavers near by will have their effects become detrimental. A second Dream Weaver of Nightmares will also make the the first cause blindness instead of nausea!

title: Drop of Luck
content: Drop of Luck is an ingredient crafted using mandrake root, netherwart, tear of the goddess, refined evil and mutandis extremis. Recipe prior to 0.21.0 using Refined Evil , Mutandis Extremis and a Water Bottle .

title: Duplication Grenade
content: This grenade when thrown (or dispensed) will create a duplicate of the bound player. Any mobs targetting the player will immediately drop aggro and target the copy instead. This grenade is made by using a Quarts Sphere on a Magic Mirror (containing a demon), with Altar Power available.

title: Earmuffs
content: Where would a Herbologist be without a trusty pair of Earmuffs? As soon as it comes time to harvest a crop of Mandrake, the farmer has two choices, either do it at night (and risk the typical nightly terrors), or don a pair of Earmuffs, and harvest away without fear of the Mandrake's screams. Indeed, Earmuffs are so effective at what they do, the wearer is unlikely to hear pretty much anything. This property may have benefits when dealing with Banshees too. Earmuffs count as leather armor in terms of protection and durability. Earmuffs are crafted in a Crafting bench from 5 pieces of leather (L) and 2 blocks of white wool (W). Crafting Bench -------------- L L L L L W W

title: Earth Protection Poppet
content: The earth protection poppet will redirect deadly falling damage from the bound player or creature to the poppet, destroying it in the process. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. The poppet is crafted from a Poppet , Feathers , Clay and a Dirt Block .

title: Ember Moss
content: Ember Moss is a plant that grows on most surfaces. If left alone on grass or sand, the moss will spread. The moss is an ingredient in Fire Protection Poppets and Infernal Chalk . The moss is obtained with a random chance by using Mutandis on another plant, or by using shears on a placed Ember Moss block (if shears are not used to harvest the block, it will be destroyed). Ember Moss gets its name from its native defense mechanism; setting fire to creatures and players who walk onto it.

title: Enchanted Broom Step-by-Step
content: To create an Enchanted Broom , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 3000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Flying Ointment in the kettle Craft a Broom Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Ritual Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Ritual Chalk Drop the Flying Ointment and the Broom in the middle circle, check there is still 3000 power in the altar Wait until night time Activate the heart glyph and wait for the Enchanted Broom to appear

title: Enchanted Broom
content: Flying around on a broomstick is certainly one of the main characteristics associated with witchcraft, and this is exactly what an Enchanted Broom is. Place the broom on the ground and activate it to mount it, then move forward or backwards, steering left and right as required. Look up and push forward to ascend, look down and push forward to descend. Press the sneak key (normally shift) to dismount. When dismounting, the broom will continue to move, so come to a stop first. Also, it is not advisable to dismount in the air for two reasons, first, the fall might kill you and second, the broom will stay where it was, so it might be hard to recover. To recover a dismounted broom, bash it until it breaks, then pick it up. Hitting a glyph block drawn with chalk while holding a broom, will remove the glyph instantly rather than taking a few seconds. Brooms can also be named using an anvil; for all those ballgame fans who absolutely must have a Nimbus 2000 or a Firebolt. The color of a brooms bristles can be changed by using a dye on the broom when it is placed in the world. A witch (or warlock) that has an Owl familiar will find controlling a broom to be much easier with better deceleration and the ability to strafe. Crafting an Enchanted Broom requires a broom and flying ointment for use in the Rite of Infusion ; as described in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic . There is also a step-by-step guide how to make it .

title: Ender Bramble
content: Ender Bramble is a mutated plant that has a very unique property: anything walking into the bramble will be teleported randomly to a location within 500 blocks. Once placed, the bramble is rather hard to break when not using an axe. Ender Brambles are mutated from a Sugar Cane plant up to 16 blocks high that is surrounded by Spanish moss on four sides. The moss must be over still water blocks. Between each Spanish moss block must be a Grassper plant holding an Ender Pearl . Using a mutating sprig on the bottom most block of the Sugar Cane will mutate up to 16 sugar cane blocks (vertically stacked) into Ender Bramble.

title: Ender Dew
content: Ender dew is an ingredient used in the Rite of Transposition . It is distilled in a distillery from an Ender Pearl .

title: Ent Twig
content: An Ent Twig will be dropped when an Ent is killed. They are used to make Mutating Sprigs and Mystic Branches .

title: Ent
content: An Ent is a tree that has been animated by a woodland spirit, often disturbed by the destruction of other trees. They will appear occasionally when trees such as Rowan, Hawthorn and Alder are felled in large quantities. Ents drop Saplings and Ent Twigs when they are eventually killed. Ents are rather hard to kill by normal means, but they do have a healthy hatred of certain tools for some reason or another.

title: Exhale of the Horned One
content: Exhale of the Horned One is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Oak saplings in an oven .

title: Fairest
content: The wonderful people, truly have a heart of gold . They are very rare and may occasionally be found by asking a Magic Mirror for their whereabouts.

title: Familiars
content: Familiars are super natural beings that assist witches and cunning folk in their practice of magic. In some cases familiars are attracted to practitioners of their own accord, but the most effective way to acquire a familiar is to bind a minor demon (or spirit) to an animal. As part of such a binding, a sympathetic link is created between the bound familiar and the witch (or warlock). Familiars take on the characteristics of the animal to which they are bound, allowing certain enhancements to a witches practice depending on the animal. Binding a Familiar A familiar is summoned and bound to an animal using a Rite of Binding as described in the book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic . Both the witch and a tamed animal of the desired type ( Cat , Owl or Toad ) must stand in the Circle when the Rite is performed. If the Rite is successful the animal will be inhabited by the familiar entity with the effects listed below. Common Effects Because the animal is inhabited by a supernatural creature it is far stronger than a normal creature of the same type. Familiars all have 50 life points and 5 points of armor. All familiar types share a sympathetic link with the witch to which they are bound. Should the witch (or warlock) be damaged, a small portion of this damage (1%) will be sent to familiar instead of the player. If the familiar is nearby, the amount of damage redirected will be significantly higher (10%). The bidirectional nature of this link means that if the familiar "dies" so too will the witch. If a familiar is "killed" or their witch dies, the familiar will merely be dismissed as described below. A dismissed familiar can be summoned using a Rite of Summoning . Cat Familiar Cat familiars require a tamed ocelot to be present in the Rite of Binding . A cat familiar allows a witch to master the art of curses and curse removal. All curses cast by such a witch will be stronger, yielding more debilitating effects and being harder for other witches to remove. Likewise, such witches will find it easier to remove curses from players. Witches with a cat familiar can also perform the Curse of Corrupt Poppet , allowing them to destroy Voodoo Protection Poppets unless the player has a Poppet protection poppet . Finally, a cat familiar imparts the knowledge to prepare the Brew of Cursed Leaping in a kettle . Owl Familiar Owl familiars require a tamed Owl to be present in the Rite of Binding . An Owl familiar allows a witch to master the use of an Enchanted Broom , allowing better deceleration, acceleration and top speed (this stacks with the Infused Brew of Soaring ). Witches with an Owl familiar are able to prepare the Brew of Bodega in a kettle . Toad Familiar Toad familiars require a tamed toad to be present in the Rite of Binding . An toad familiar allows a witch to master brewing and the use of brews. When such a witch prepares brews, there is a 5% chance (cumulative to the bonuses from wearing witches clothing) that an extra brew will be produced. When a witch with a Toad familiar uses a brew, that brew will have slightly stronger effects, brews with an area of effect will have a slightly larger area. The brews of sprouting and thorns will grow slightly larger plants. The Brew of Vines will allow more horizontal traversal. The brew of erosion will do more damage to creatures hit. This familiar has little effect on certain brews like those associated with necromancy or other familiars. Witches with an toad familiar are able to prepare the Brew of Frog's Tongue in a kettle . Dismissing a Familiar A familiar can be dismissed by interacting with it (right-clicking) while sneaking (shift held) when it is sitting. A familiar will automatically be dismissed if it or the bound witch dies. When a familiar is dismissed, the witch will not benefit from any of its effects. Summoning a Familiar A dismissed familiar can be summoned at any time using the appropriate Rite of Summoning .

title: Fanciful Thread
content: Fanciful Thread is spun from Wispy Cotton using a Spinning Wheel . It is an ingredient used to make Dream Catchers . It is made in a Spinning Wheel using Wispy Cotton , String and Odour of Purity .

title: Frequently Asked Questions
content: Q: I am noticing items are not stacking that should be, what could this be? A: This is most likely an interaction between death-chest style mods. Witchery has a ritual that allows players to recover despawned items dropped during death. Given the obvious problems two such death-chest mods running simultaneously can cause, the ritual gets disabled if another death chest mod is detected, this is not infallible! Because of the way witchery tags items that are dropped, and clears this when they despawn or are picked up, if this process is interrupted (say by a death chest mod slurping the items and not generating itempickup events), the tag data can be left in the item in the death chest making such an item appear unique and not stackable. Dropping the item and picking it up should fix the issue. Disabling the ritual (in the config), if using other deathchest mods, is the way to go. Q: Vampire witches are forcing constant night , can something be done? A: Forcing constant night is normally accomplished using the Witchery Rite of Eclipse, there is a config setting I:RiteOfEclipseCooldownInSecs that can be used to set a cooldown on how often this rite can be used in a world. Once a player uses the rite in a world, all players in the same world will have to wait for the cooldown to run down before any of them may use the Rite again (does not matter where in the world the Rite is performed). The option can be set to zero for no cooldown, or up to 3600 for the maximum cooldown of once per hour. Q: Do vampires work with the Morpheus mod ? A: No, in general a server that has vampires enabled (you can disable access to this feature), will want to ensure that both vampire players and non-vampire players have sufficient safe play time (day for normal players, night for vampires). Mods like Morpheus that allow non-vampires to force daytime without vampire players consent, can cause descent. It is recommended if enabling the vampire feature to consider the necessity for night. See the previous question for how to stop vampire witches forcing night all the time. Q: Odd stuff is happening with potion effects , especially when mesmerizing villagers as a vampire, or null pointer exceptions when entities tick, help! A: This is normally due to conflicts with potion ids. Minecraft only has a limited number of ids that can be used for potions and their effects (like slow, strength, night vision, etc.). This means that there can only ever be 127 potion effects added. Vanilla Minecraft already adds a fair few, and the remainder must be carefully assigned. Most mods will (in the order they are added), look for an unused id and assign their potion effect to it. Other mods will always assign to a fixed id and normally allow you to change it in their config file. Witchery will try to do both, first scanning for free ids, then adding those ids to the config file so they can be changed by hand later (or deleted to get witchery to scan again). If you think you are running into problems, first use NEI and in the NEI screen select OPTIONS > TOOLS > DATA DUMPS > Potions: DUMP. This will then add a file to the dump folder in your .minecraft folder (where the mods folder is) called potions.txt (.minecraft/dumps/potions.txt). In this file you will see all ids and which potion effect is assigned to each (if any). Now look in the Witchery config file (normally .minecraft/config/witchery.cfg), and in the potions section you should see each potion and the id it is being assigned to, make sure the numbers all match up in both files. If one of the witchery potions is not in the NEI file, make a backup of your world then find an id in the NEI file that is free and change the incorrect id in the wichery.cfg file to it. You probably want to choose numbers between 32 and 127, to avoid any conflicts with vanilla ids. Save and restart Minecraft. An important note, many mods say they have extended the range of valid ids to beyond 127, by and large this is not true . In actuality, there are three things than need to be changed and most mods only change one; which at best has no effect, at worst will causes crashes. To correctly extend the range (by increasing the potions array length, patching the entity NBT save format and patching the potion effect sync network message), requires the mod to become a core mod with all the side effects that entails. Most mods will not do this, and there here are only a couple of mods to my knowledge that do this correctly, but both have other caveats so install at your own risk. Witchery does not extend the potion range in a way that allows for more than 127 ids. This problem has many symptoms that you may be experiencing with witchery, these include: Flying Villagers when being mesmerized Ars magica 2 or witchery spells have the wrong effects Reports of Null Pointer Exceptions when the player or other mobs are being ticked ... Q: Why does my werewolf natural armor not appear to be protecting me? A: First of all, the natural armor of a werewolf only applies if you are in wolf or wolfman form. Secondly, the armor has two parts, a damage reduction part like normal armor (which increases with werewolf level) and a max damage limit, that will significantly limit all non-silver weapon damage. It has been discovered that some Bukkit plug-ins ignore the Forge damage event system , and as a result these bonuses will get overwritten by such plug-ins. If this is an issue for you, please ask your server Admin to check which plug-ins are ignoring the Forge damage events system (often ones that provide safe areas), and ask them to ask the plug-in authors to support forge hurt and death events correctly. Q: Why does my screen go dark , or all weird when I go into wolf form ? A: This is normally due to witchery and another mod using the same mechanism to change the users viewpoint. Witchery tries to be compatible with other mods that use methods to change viewpoint (such as Ars Magica 2 and Morph), but some other mods will change the view always, and in ways that are simply incompatible. Mods that use the Weather Mod have shown this behavior, for example. If this is happening to you, you should try to see which exact mods cause the issue and then make some hard choices. Sometimes this may be a bug that can be worked around, other times not, post your findings on the Witchery thread on Minecraft Forums, and perhaps we can help out.

title: Fetishes
content: A Fetish is an object believed to have supernatural powers. Fetishes can take many forms but the most common in Witchcraft are the Scarecrow , Trent Effigy and Witch's Ladder . Creating a Fetish To create a Fetish a witch must first craft the object ( Scarecrow , Trent Effigy and Witch's Ladder ). Then spectral creatures must be summoned, and finally a Rite of Binding performed to trap the chosen creatures into the object. The combination of creatures present when the rite of binding is performed determines the powers the Fetish will have. The book, Witchcraft: Conjuration & Fetishes explains the different effects and required bindings. Fetishes in the Spirit World It is possible to carry a bound fetish into the Spirit World if the witch has manifested via a Spirit Portal . The witch must just pick up the Fetishes and then return to the Spirit World. The fetishes may never return to the overworld however (at least not by means known to witchcraft). Fetishes placed in the Spirit World may also materialize it the overworld at the same location. They will have the same effect just be non corporeal. To modify their effects the original in the Spirit World must be changed. Placing and controlling a Fetish Once a fetish is placed it will be inactive, using a Boline on it will cycle between its various ways of activation. Using a bound Taglock Kit on the Fetish will make it aware of that being. Using a water bucket on the fetish will clear all taglocks from it. One of the following activation mods can be chosen: Activate if a player is nearby whose taglock is unknown Activate if a player is nearby whose taglock is known Activate if a player or creature is nearby whose taglock is unknown Activate when some known creatures are nearby Activate when all know creatures are not nearby Deactivated Fetish Effects Voodoo Protection Sentinel Disorientation Shrieking Ghost Walking

title: Filled Chalice
content: The filled chalice is an upgrade for an altar that improves the maximum amount of power an altar can hold. The chalice will increase the maximum power by 2x the base power rate. The filled chalice is an upgrade to the a chalice . Only one chalice upgrade will have an effect on an altar. A chalice is crafted with a Chalice and Redstone Soup .

title: Filtered Fume Funnel
content: A Filtered Fume Funnel is an upgrade to the Fume Funnel that increases the chance of a by-product being produced by a connected witches Oven by a further 5% above the normal Fume Funnel (for a total of +10% cook speed and +30% by-product chance). As with a Fume Funnel, an oven can only accept two Fume Funnels (whether they are upgraded or not). The Filtered Fume Funnel is crafted with a Fume Funnel and a Fume Filter .

title: Fire Protection Poppet
content: The fire protection poppet will redirect deadly burning damage from the bound player or creature to the poppet, destroying it in the process. Continuing to swim in lava once the poppet has gone is probably not a good idea! For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. The poppet is crafted from a Poppet , Wool of Bat and Ember Moss . In version 0.5.1 and earlier, this poppet was crafted with Torches in place of the Ember Moss.

title: Flame Imp
content: Flame Imps are demons strongly associated with fiery magic. Not as physically strong as their dimmer cousins, flame imps are mischievous creatures who are very easily swayed with the promise of personal gain. They may even be bound into the service of a witch (or warlock) if the offer is good enough. Flame Imps are summoned using a Rite of Summoning, and they are most likely to attack the first person they see. It is advisable make a deal with them quickly. Making a deal To bind a Flame Imp into service, a witch must write the agreement into a Demonic Contract and sign it with their own blood (craft with a Taglock Kit ). This contract must then be given to the Flame Imp, who will require a down-payment of Experience Levels (typically 25). Once a deal is struck, the imp will be bound to the location of the deal, not able to wander too far away. The Imp will attack anyone hostile to its master. Imp Magic Imps like receiving valuable gifts, once suitably impressed an imp may be prepared to perform various flame related spells at the request of their master. Spells take the form of contracts describing what must be done and to whom. Simply craft a Demonic Contract with the necessary components and then the resulting contract with a taglock. Then give the contract to the the Imp. So long as the creature is in the same dimension, the magic will take hold. Spell Effect Components Fiery Touch Causes random fires to erupt when the victim interacts with objects Blaze Rod, Ember Moss Melting Touch Causes harvested blocks to be instantly smelted (and has a chance to double ores) Lava Bucket Living Flame Summons a Blaze to attack the victim Blaze Rod, Hint of Rebirth Evaporation Causes the victim to evaporate nearby water Blaze Rod, Magma Cream Fiery Tolerance Grants a long duration resistance to heat and fire Blaze Powder Imp magic effects generally will be removed by death or the consumption of a Frozen Heart . Gifts of demonic souls A Flame imp may also occasionally give a gift in return for being given valuable things. These have been known to include the souls of demons that can be consumed to allow a witch to cast demonic spells . A very well treated Flame Imp may even yield knowledge to summon an Lord of Torment . Power overwhelming A Flame imp may be given a Demon Heart to consume. When eaten, the Flame Imp will become much stronger for around 30 minutes. While so boosted, the Imp will be unable to perform Imp magic. using an Icy Needle on the Imp will return it to its normal size.

title: Flying Ointment
content: Flying ointment is an ingredient used in a Rite of Infusion to create an Enchanted Broom . It is very poisonous, so drinking it is not advised. The ointment is created in a kettle using the recipe in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Long-duration Potion of Swiftness , Diamond , Feather , Wool of Bat and Belladonna .

title: Focused Will
content: Focused Will is an ingredient used in other recipes, it is created by distilling Brew of Flowing Spirit with Oil of Vitriol .

title: Fortification of the Corpse
content: Fortification of the Corpse is an effect produced with a Brazier , it causes all creature standing near to the burning brazier to recieve less damage. To produce this effect, add Tear of the Goddess , Rotten Flesh and Blaze powder to a Brazier and light with a Flint & Tinder. The brazier requires Altar power.

title: Foul Fume
content: A foul fume (commonly known as sulfur dioxide) is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning food item, logs or jungle saplings in an oven .

title: Frozen Heart
content: Frozen hearts are a crafting ingredient used to make Icy Slippers . The heart is crafted from an Icy Needle , Creeper Heart and a Ghast Tear .

title: Fume Filter
content: A Fume Filter is used to upgrade a Fume Funnel to a Filtered Fume Funnel in order to increase the by-product chance of a Witches Oven . A Fume Filter is crafted using Iron Ingots , Glass and a Charged Attuned Stone .

title: Fume Funnel
content: A fume funnel is an upgrade for the Witches Oven that increases that increases the speed (over a normal furnace) by an additional 10%, and increases the chances of a by-product being produced by a further 25%. The Fume Funnel must be positioned next to the left or right of the witches oven with the same facing. Up to two funnels can be attached to one oven. It is possible to place a funnel between two ovens for it to benefit both. A third fume funnel can also be placed on top of an oven to make it look better and also to increase its speed by a further 10%. The Fume Funnel can be upgraded to a Filtered Fume Funnel by crafting it with a Fume Filter . The Fume Funnel is crafted with Iron Blocks , Empty Buckets , a Lava Bucket and a Glowstone block.

title: Garlic Garland
content: A garlic garland is a block that may be placed in the world for decoration and also to keep Vampires away. A vampire will move away from a garlic garland, unable to pass it. Also a player vampire will not be able to break (or pick up) a placed garland, it will actually burn them if they do. A garlic garland is made from Garlic and string.

title: Garlic
content: Garlic is a plantable crop that can be used to make tasty food, and to deal with vampires. Garlic can be obtained by breaking tall grass, although it has a very low drop chance. It may be planted on tilled farm land, and will grow in five stages. When full grown the plant may be harvested for up to three dropped garlic bulbs. Which may either be replanted, or used in a crafting recipe. Garlic bulbs can be used to make meaty stew , a particularly nourishing meal. It is also the main ingredient in making a Garlic Garland , a useful tool in warding off vampires . Finally, garlic can be used by witches in a Witches' Cauldron to make a vampire Weakening brew, and by Witch Hunters, to make vampire resistant armor .

title: Getting Started
content: Installation Install Minecraft Forge (follow the installation guide ) Download the latest Witchery mod and place the zip into your Minecraft Mods directory Starting Witchery Some things that can be done to get going with the Witchery mod: Make a witches oven to cook meat, charcoal and produce other materials (ovens can be upgraded) You will need some clay jars Create some Mutandis using Mandrake root Use the Mutandis to get Spanish Moss (tip: you need shears to harvest it) Use the Spanish moss to make some poppets Use the Mutandis to get Rowan saplings and grow Rowan trees Build an Altar Build a Distillery Make Gypsum Create some Ritual Chalk and some Golden Chalk Read Witchcraft: Circle Magic and try out some rites Build a Kettle Read the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions and make some Brews Build some Dream Catchers Experiment with Necromancy Upgrade your altar with a candelabra and filled chalice Create some otherwhere chalk and infernal chalk and try some more complex rites ( teleporting with Waystones is fun). Make an enchanted broom Hunt a witch and acquire a witches hand Create an infusion and perform the Rite of Infusion on yourself (don't forget a Death Protection Poppet !) Summon a demon (and maybe make a deal...) Make some attractive garments, like hats , robes and slippers Try your hand at symbol magic Fight a boss of the wyld hunt Take a look into the future Mutate some animals and bind a familiar

title: Ghost of the Light
content: Ghost of the Light is used in the Rite of Infusion to perform the Infusion of Light as described in the book Witchcraft: Circle magic . Ghost of the Light is created in a kettle using the recipe shown in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Long-duration Invisibility Potion , Long-duration Fire Resistance Potion , Fire Protection Poppet , Torch and Tongue of Dog .

title: Ghost Walking Fetish
content: A Ghost Walking Fetish causes witches who have manifested from the Spirit World via a Spirit Portal to not lose any of their manifestation time so long as they stay near the Fetish A Ghost Walking Fetish is created by binding 3 spirits , and 1 banshee and 1 spectre to a fetish object using the Rite of Binding .

title: Glass Goblet
content: A Glass Goblet is used to collect blood for various uses. Most commonly is is used by a level 9 vampire player to fill with their own blood in order that they may turn a villager or another player. To turn a villager, the player must be vampire level 9, must take the villager to a safe place near a coffin, must almost fully drain the villager of blood, must mesmerize the villager, must use the empty Glass Goblet, to fill it, and then must use the full glass Goblet on the Villager. To turn another player, the vampire must be vampire level 9, must drain the player almost fully of blood, must the use the Empty Glass Goblet to fill it with their own blood, must then give the full goblet to the other player who must then use it to drink it. It has been reported that Glass Goblets are referred to several times in the book Observations of an Immortal .

title: Glint Weed
content: Glint Weed is a plant that gives of a natural light, almost the same as a torch. Glint weed is found by using Mutandis on another plant. Glint Weed will spread if planted on grass, dirt or sand. Glint Weed can be placed on most materials as a light source alternative to a torch. It can also be placed onto ceilings. Glint Weed can be harvested just by bashing it (shears are not needed).

title: Glowstone Dust
content: Glowstone is created in a distillery by distilling a Blaze Powder with Gunpowder .

title: Golden Chalk
content: Golden chalk is used to draw the heart glyph at the center of a ritual circle. It has no other uses. Use the chalk on the ground to draw the glyph. Golden chalk is crafed in a witches' cauldron using mandrake root, a gold nugget and ritual chalk. Recipe prior to 0.21.0 Golden chalk is crafted from Ritual chalk , Redstone , Gold Nuggets and Dandelion Yellow .

title: Golden Thread
content: Golden Thread is spun from hay using a Spinning Wheel . It is an ingredient used to make witches clothing. Golden thread can be smelted in a furnace to make a Gold Nugget . In a spinning wheel, place a Hay Bale and Whiff of Magic .

title: Grassper
content: Grassper plant, is a simple plant into which a single object can be placed, by using the object on the plant. The plant will hold onto the item until the plant is used (right-clicked) a second time which will cause it to drop the object. The Grassper plant is used in various mutation recipes to hold items used in the mutation. Four Grassper plants are created by placing a chest over still water and surrounding its four sides with tall grass . Using a Mutating Sprig on the chest will cause the tall grass to be mutated into Grassper plants (the chest will be consumed).

title: Graveyard Dust
content: Graveyard Dust is an ingredient used in necromancy. It is made from Spectral Dust , Bonemeal and Mutandis .

title: Graveyard Mist
content: Graveyard Mist is an effect produced with a Brazier , it covers the area of the brazier with a thick mist. To produce this effect, add Gunpowder, Quicklime and Glowstone dust to a Brazier and light with a Flint & Tinder. The brazier requires Altar power.

title: Gulg's Gurdle
content: Gulg's Gurdle is an epic artifact sometimes dropped by Gulg's avatar . The Gurdle is worn in the legs slot, and functions as leather armor (with much highter durability and repaired with leather). When striking with an empty hand, the bearer will causes 4 damage (2 hearts), and launch the target into the air. Being close to another player wearing Mog's Quiver , grants a resistance buff.

title: Gypsum
content: Gypsum is an ingredient used to create Ritual chalk (and subsequently the other chalk types). It is created in a distillery by distilling a Foul Fume with Quicklime .

title: Hand of Death
content: Part of Death 's accoutrements, the Hand of Death grants the user Chill Touch when they strike a creature with the hand. Chill Touch causes damage equal to 15% of the targets maximum health and ignores armor. If the Hand of Death is used (Right-Clicked) while the player is wearing Death's Hood , Death's Robe and Death's Footwear , Death's Scythe will be summoned. Using the Hand of Death a second time will dismiss the Scythe. Every second that Death's Scythe is held, a point of hunger will be lost. Swinging the Scythe will hit multiple creatures and cause the chill touch effect on each, the damage caused will be increased the lower the wielder's hunger bar. Also, hunger and health may be restored to the player if a creature is hit, based on how low the wielders hunger bar is. The Hand of Death is a possible drop each time Death is defeated. Wearing all Death's accoutrements and wielding the Hand of Death can result in the player being summoned in place of Death when death is called.

title: Happenstance Oil
content: Happenstance Oil is an ingredient used in the production of a Crystal Ball . It is created in a kettle using Redstone Soup , Potion of Night Vision (8:00) , Eye of Ender , Golden Carrot , Spider Eye and Mandrake Root .

title: Hawthorn Leaves
content: Hawthorn leaves are harvested from Hawthorn trees in the same way vanilla leaves are harvested (i.e. with shears). Bashed or decaying Hawthorn leaves have a chance to drop Hawthorn saplings . Hawthorn leaves will decay like vanilla leaves when all logs on a Hawthorn tree are removed. Hawthorn trees are grown from Hawthorn saplings .

title: Hawthorn Log
content: Hawthorn logs are harvested from Hawthorn trees in the same way vanilla logs are harvested (i.e. with an axe). Hawthorn trees are grown from Hawthorn saplings and can be made into Hawthorn planks . Hawthorn also has a nice trait that it is rather hard to catch on fire.

title: Hawthorn Planks
content: Hawthorn planks behave like all vanilla wooden planks and can be used in a majority of recipes that require wooden planks. Hawthorn planks are crafted from Hawthorn logs in the same way all other woods are crafted from logs. Hawthorn also has a nice trait that it is rather hard to catch on fire.

title: Hawthorn Sapling
content: Hawthorn saplings grow into Hawthorn trees when planted. Hawthorn trees can be harvested for Hawthorn logs and Hawthorn leaves (which yield more Hawthorn saplings). Hawthorn saplings cooked in an oven yield Wood Ash , and have a chance of producing Odour of Purity . Hawthorn saplings can initially be acquired only by using Mutandis on another planted sapling (does not matter which type). Given the mutation of the sapling is random it may take a few goes before Hawthorn saplings appear.

title: Heart of Gold
content: Heart of Gold is a drop that can occur when an NPC dies that a Magic Mirror has determined is the fairest of them all. A Heart of Gold can be used in a potion brewed with the Witches' Cauldron to add effects that attract animals (drawing them to the drinker and instantly taming them). When inverted, this ingredient repulses animals, and untames those not owned by the drinker.

title: Hellhound
content: A Hellhound is an aggressive creature found in the nether, often in packs. It will attack remorselessly, and its bite can cause its victim to catch fire. Hellhounds will ignore players unless they attack or get too close. When killed, a hell hound can drop Tongue of Dog and occasionally a Hellhound Head . Hellhounds can be turned into normal wolves, by first inflicting them with a Potion of Weakness and then feeding them a Golden Apple. After some time, the conversion will occur, accelerated if the Hellhound is in a cage.

title: Hint of Rebirth
content: A Hint of Rebirth is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Spruce saplings in an oven .

title: Hobgoblin
content: Hobgoblins are small creatures who can be found pottering about in forests, they are almost certainly the product of some devious magical experimentation. They are rather cowardly, although in groups they have been known to raid and take over nearby villages. Hobgoblins are inherently social creatures, and will settle down given a chance (and a few huts nearby). They are also very likely to want to trade items they have scavenged. Koboldite is a material that Hobgoblins value more than gold and jewels, they have a knack of finding small deposits of the stuff when digging, and they alone know how to smelt it into a useable metal. In addition to forming useful trading partnerships, a less scrupulous individual, may find that using a leash on a hobgoblin inclines them to perform more menial work. Such as picking up dropped items, and placing them into nearby chests. Also giving a leashed Hobgoblin a pickaxe, will force them to start digging (with the chance of discovering deposits of Koboldite Dust ). Hobgoblins are also known to worship their gods: Mog and Gulg . An ingenious witch (or warlock) may find ways of exploiting this power to their own ends using a Statue of Hobgoblin Patron to boost their Mystic Branch power. Care must be taken when doing this, in order to not attract the attention of Mog and Gulg themselves.

title: Horn of the Hunt
content: The Horn of the Hunt is an object given as part of the Wolf Alter quest chain. When used it will summon a Horned Huntsman and then disappear. Hold the use button (right-mouse) until you hear the horn sound. If no Horned Huntsman appears you may need to try again in a more open area.

title: Horned Huntsman
content: A Horned Huntsman is a member of the Wild Hunt, a powerful entity created by the Horned God. If one is encountered, prepare for a hard fight, or better yet, flee. The occasionally drop their spear when defeated.

title: Hunger Protection Poppet
content: The hunger protection poppet will redirect almost-deadly starvation damage from the bound player or creature to the poppet, destroying it in the process. It will also refill the hunger bar. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. This poppet is of most use in harder difficulty modes, where hunger can be deadly. The poppet is crafted using a Poppet , Glistering Melon and Rotten Flesh .

title: Hunter Dawn Armor
content: Hunter Dawn Armor is an upgrade to Silvered Hunter Armor which adds significant protection from vampires and werewolves. When the wearer is attacked by a vampire or werewolf: The armor rating of the armor is doubled. If the attacker is a werewolf, will take a small amount of fire damage. The armor will take next to no damage from the attack. Hunter Dawn Armor is crafted with the Silvered Hunter Armor piece and Garlic .

title: Icy Needle
content: An icy needle is an occasional drop when a Snowbell plant is harvested. Icy needles are used to make Brew of Frost and Frozen Hearts .

title: Icy Slippers
content: Icy Slippers are worn in the foot slot and have the same durability and protection as leather boots. They can be dyed and enchanted like leather boots too. Icy slippers will immediately freeze any water they come into contact with, allowing the wearer to effectively walk on water. Getting trapped in a water filled container is probably a very bad idea! Icy slippers will also turn Lava into Obsidian when walked over. This can be problematic because it will also damage the slippers, and there is always a risk of being set on fire (however small). Icy slippers will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if normal shoes were worn. Other players however will not be able to see the slippers! The Icy Slippers are crafted from Impregnated Leather , Diamond Vapor , Odour of Purity , Golden Thread and a Frozen Heart . Crafting damaged slippers with four Impregnated Leather will repair the slippers. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Impregnated Leather
content: Impregnated Leather is required to make magical apparel such as Witches Hats. It is crafted using Leather , Whiff of Magic and Diamond Vapor .

title: Infernal Animus
content: The Infernal Animus is used in the Rite of Infusion to perform the Infernal Infusion as described in the book Witchcraft: Circle magic . The Infernal Animus is created in a kettle using the recipe shown in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Potion of Harming II , Demon Heart , Voodoo Poppet , Refined Evil and Blaze Rod .

title: Demonic Blood
content: Demonic blood is a powerful ingredient used in certain pagan rites. It is distilled in a distillery from a Demon Heart using Diamond Vapor . Demonic Blood can be crafted with Wicker Bundles to create Bloodied Wicker Bundles . It can also be used on a Wicker Bundle that has been placed in the world to make it and up to four adjacent bundles into Bloodied Wicker bundles if they are not already bloodied (this works diagonally too). Demonic Blood can also be crafted with gravel to create netherrak.

title: Infernal Chalk
content: Infernal chalk is used to draw each of the red infernal glyphs that make up an infernal circle. Infernal chalk and glyphs have a strong affinity with the Nether dimension and rites involving them will often concern fire or demons. To draw an infernal glyph, use the chalk on the ground. Infernal glyphs of various types will be drawn, although they all have the same function. If you use the chalk on an existing glyph, a new one will be drawn in its place. Infernal glyphs should be arranged in the roughly circular patterns as illustrated in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic . Infernal chalk is crafted from netherwart, blaze powder and ritual chalk. Recipe prior to version 0.21.0 Infernal chalk is crafted from Ritual Chalk , Ember Moss , Blaze Powder and Rose Red . In version 0.5.1 and earlier this chalk was crafted with Glowstone Dust in place of Ember Moss.

title: Infernal Infusion Step-by-Step
content: To infuse yourself with the Infernal Infusion , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 4000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Infernal Animus in the kettle Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Infernal Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Infernal Chalk Create a Death Protection Poppet and use a Taglock Kit on your Bed , then bind it to yourself Stand in the inner circle, drop the Infernal Animus , check there is still 4000 power in the altar Activate the heart glyph and wait for the effects Get a Witches Hand and begin using the Infernal Infusion

title: Infernal Infusion
content: The Infernal infusion, infuses the power of the nether into the player. This power grants the ability to enthrall creatures to do your bidding, and to optionally sacrifice them to acquire their power for a time. The infusion is performed using the Rite of Infusion described in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic , using an Infernal Animus that has been brewed in a kettle according to the recipe in Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . This infusion has no default passive powers, although passives may be obtained depending on the last sacrificed creature. To use the active powers a witch hand must be equipped. Left-click a mob to order all nearby enthralled mobs to attack that mob. Left-click while sneaking (holding shift) on a normal mob to enthrall the mob (weaker mobs (see below) will no longer be hostile towards you and you will see particles emanating from the mob). Left-click while sneaking (holding shift) on an enthralled mob to sacrifice it and steal its powers. Right-click to use the current creature power from the last sacrificed mob. Right-click while sneaking (holding shift) on the ground to order nearby weaker enthralled mobs (see below) to go to that location. Not all creatures can be enthralled, notable exceptions include: Players, the Ender dragon, Withers, Demons, Iron golems and Snow golems. Not all enthralled creatures can be fully controlled, see the table below (Attacks: Can be ordered to attack, Ignore: Will not attack the player, Moves: Can be ordered to move). Creature Powers Sacrifice and enthralled creature to acquire its power, each sacrificed create gives ten uses of the active power. Creature Active Powers (Right-Click) Passive Powers Attacks Ignores Moves Spider, Cave spider Shoot Web Climb walls yes daytime daytime Bat Night Vision Buff Jump to fly, Shift to avoid fall damage yes Creeper Explode Recharge infusion power if hit by lightning yes yes yes Squid Blind targeted player (ink) Swim faster, Breathe underwater yes yes Zombie Damage and resistance buff yes yes yes Pig zombie Large damage and resistance buff Immune to fire yes yes yes Ghast Shoot large fireballs Fire resistance yes Blaze Shoot three small fireballs Fire resistance yes Slime, Magma cube Jump boost buff Jump higher, Immune to fall damage Silverfish Sprint boost buff Move faster on land sometimes Cat, Dog, Horse Sprint boost buff Move faster on land yes yes yes Skeleton Shoot magic arrows Lesser breath underwater yes yes yes Enderman Teleport short distances yes sometimes Cow, Pig, Chicken, Sheep, Villager Heal yes yes yes

title: Infinity Egg
content: The infinity egg is a creative only block that when place don an altar will provide 1000 power, increase power multiplier by x10 and increase recharge rate by x10.

title: Infused Brew Base
content: Infused Brew Base is the base ingredient for making Infused Brews as part of a Rite of Infusion. Infused Brew Base has two recipes, the first is expensive to make, the second uses the first to produce more at a cheaper cost. Infused Brew Base is made in a Kettle initially using Redstone Soup , Toe of Frog , Brew of Flowing Spirit , Owlet's Wing , Creeper Heart and Tongue of Dog . Subsequent batches can be brewed using Infused Brew Base , Brew of Flowing Spirit , Hint of Rebirth , Mandrake Root , Wool of Bat and Wild Bramble .

title: Infused Brew of Soaring
content: The Infused Brew of Soaring may be drunk to allow significantly better Enchanted Broom control (top speed, turning and deceleration) for 120 minutes of server time. These bonus stack with those of an Owl Familiar. Only a single Infused Brew can be active at a time, with the newer one replacing the older one. The Infused Brew of Soaring is made using a Rite of Infusion with Infused Brew Base , Broom , Feather, Arthana and an Owl .

title: Infused Brew of the Grave
content: The Infused Brew of the Grave can be drunk to allow a Necromantic Stone to be used to take control of normal undead creatures (not just those raised with a brew of raising). This ability can be used once every 30 seconds. Additionally, when a witch under the influence of this brew kills and animal, villager or other player, they will regain hunger and a little life as the feed off their victim. The brew lasts 120 minutes of server time. Only a single Infused Brew can be active at a time, with the newer one replacing the older one. The Infused Brew of the Grave is made using a Rite of Infusion with Infused Brew Base , Bone, Dense Web , Necromantic Stone and a Zombie.

title: Infusion of Light Step-by-Step
content: To infuse yourself with the Infusion of Light , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 2000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Ghost of the Light in the kettle Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Ritual Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Ritual Chalk Create a Death Protection Poppet and use a Taglock Kit on your Bed , then bind it to yourself Stand in the inner circle, drop the Ghost of the Light , check there is still 2000 power in the altar Activate the Heart glyph and wait for the effects Get a Witches Hand and begin using the Infusion of Light

title: Infusion of Light
content: The Infusion of Light, infuses the power of control over light into the player. This power grants the ability to bend light to become invisible and to harden light to create solid barriers. The infusion is performed using the Rite of Infusion described in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic , using Soul of the Sky that has been brewed in a kettle according to the recipe in Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . This infusion has no default passive powers. To use the active powers a witch hand must be equipped. Hold right-click to become invisible (remember that armor remain visible). Right-click while sneaking (holding shift) on the top of a block to create a 3x3 barrier at that location (the barrier will disappear after a time). Right-click while sneaking (holding shift) on the side of a block to create a bridge out from that face. Right-click while sneaking (holding shift) on a nearby player or mob, to enclose them in a barrier (the barrier will disappear after a time). Left-click on a very close nearby player or mob, to raise them in the air enclose them in a barrier (the barrier will disappear after a time).

title: Infusion of Otherwhere Step-by-Step
content: To infuse yourself with the Infusion of Otherwhere , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 4000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Spirit of Otherwhere in the kettle Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Otherwhere Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Otherwhere Chalk Create a Death Protection Poppet and use a Taglock Kit on your Bed , then bind it to yourself Stand in the inner circle, drop the Spirit of Otherwhere , check there is still 4000 power in the altar Activate the Heart glyph and wait for the effects Get a Witches Hand and begin using the Infusion of Otherwhere

title: Infusion of Otherwhere
content: The Infusion of Otherwhere, infuses the power of the End into the player. This power grants the ability to teleport. The infusion is performed using the Rite of Infusion described in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic , using Spirit of Otherwhere that has been brewed in a kettle according to the recipe in Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . This infusion has no default passive powers. To use the active powers a witch hand must be equipped. Right-click to teleport a short distance to the targeted location or mob. Hold right-click to teleport a long distance to the targeted location or mob (you will hear a pling sound when it is ready) Hold right-click while sneaking (holding shift) until you hear a pling sound to store the current location as a recall point. Right-click while sneaking (holding shift) to teleport to the last saved recall point. Left-click on a very close mob or player to teleport them and you into the air (this can be done multiple times, but don't forget to teleport back to earth!) Left-click while sneaking (holding shift) on a very close mob or player to teleport them and you to the last saved recall point. Teleporting to a saved recall point (alone or with company) can be done across dimensions.

title: Infusion of the Overworld Step-by-Step
content: To infuse yourself with the Infusion of the Overworld , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 4000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Soul of the World in the kettle Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Ritual Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Ritual Chalk Create a Death Protection Poppet and use a Taglock Kit on your Bed , then bind it to yourself Stand in the inner circle, drop the Soul of the World , check there is still 4000 power in the altar Activate the Heart glyph and wait for the effects Get a Witches Hand and begin using the Infusion of the Overworld

title: Infusion of the Overworld
content: The Infusion of the Overworld, infuses the power of the overworld into the player. This power grants the ability to manipulate the earth and metals. The infusion is performed using the Rite of Infusion described in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic , using Soul of the World that has been brewed in a kettle according to the recipe in Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . This infusion has a passive effect that when falling on dirt or grass you will take no falling damage (the block will be broken instead). Holding shift (sneak-key) when falling may cause an explosion at the point of impact! To use the active powers a witch hand must be equipped. Left-click on a very close player or mob in metal armor to do a huge push back Right-click on a nearby player or mob holding a metal item to disarm them. Right-click on the ground to raise a column of earth Right-click on the side of an unobstructed block to launch it in the direction you face, causing damage to what it hits Hold right-click and release after a while to create a circular shockwave of earth that damages and pushes back players and mobs in its path. The longer the charge the further the wave travels. Hold right-click while sneaking (shift held) to pull all metal object lying on the ground around towards you (useful after a disarm), this will also pull all nearby ingots from any metal ore! Right-click on an metal ore block to pull two ingots from it.

title: Infusions
content: Infusions represent perhaps the highest tier of witchcraft, whereby the witch (or warlock) is able to capture the essence of an aspect of the world and imbue it into either an object or themselves. Magic to mediate such an infusion is complex and many safeguards are needed to avoid calamity, this is never more true than when the practitioner seeks to infuse aspects of dimensions beyond the world. Obviously, infusing the power of a dimension into oneself will provide some interesting effects, the least of which being the ability to use magic without the usual paraphernalia of spell crafting. Creation and use of an infusions is a multistage process. Creating an infusion Brewing of infusions is performed using a water-filled and heated witches kettle , into which the ingredients for a specific infusion must be thrown. Infusions always require power from a nearby altar . The process for brewing is described on the brews page . Once an infusion is complete and white swirls are issuing from the spluttering kettle, a glass bottle may be used on the kettle to bottle the infusion. Ruined infusions will result in a bottle of water. The following infusions can be created; the recipes for which can be found in the book, Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions : Ghost of the Light Soul of the World Spirit of Otherwhere Infernal Animus Flying Ointment Mystic Unguent Happenstance Oil It is almost certainly a bad idea to drink an infusion! Infusing an item Once an infusion has been created, it must actually be infused into the object. This step requires an appropriate Rite of Infusion as described in the Witchcraft: Circle Magic book. Infusion Item Infusable Ingredient Enchanted Broom Broom Flying Ointment Step-by-step Mystic Branch Ent Twig Mystic Unguent Step-by-step Crystal Ball Quartz Sphere , Gold Ingot Happenstance Oil Step-by-step Seer Stone Quartz Sphere , Obsidian Happenstance Oil Infusing a player Once an infusion has been created, it must actually be infused into a player. This step requires an appropriate Rite of Infusion as described in the Witchcraft: Circle Magic book. Infusion Infusable Ingredient Infusion of Light Ghost of the Light Step-by-step Infusion of the Overworld Soul of the World Step-by-step Infusion of Otherwhere Spirit of Otherwhere Step-by-step Infernal Infusion Infernal Animus Step-by-step Due to the immense power involved in the infusion process, a player is almost certain to die . Magical means of avoiding death are required to survive the process, and not waste the expensive ingredients. Using player infused powers Some of the powers granted by an infusion will be passive, others will need a witches hand in order to focus them. Get a witches hand off the corpse of a witch . Recharging your power All infusions will draw from the magical energy imbued at the time of the Rite of Infusion . When this is all used up, most of the powers will cease to function. To recharge this power level (shown on the screen as a power bar) a Rite of Charging must be used as described in the book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic .

title: Kettle
content: Nothing invokes the image of witchcraft more than a bubbling witches kettle (or cauldron), which is used to make witches brews and later infusions . A kettle is crafted using Sticks , String , a Cauldron and an Attuned Stone . A kettle must be set up in the correct way, before it may be used. Firstly, a fire must be set under the kettle ( netherrack is best for this, but wood will do just as well). Next the kettle must be filled by using a Water-filled Bucket on it. Thirdly, the ingredients for the brew or infusion must be thrown into the kettle (the liquid will change color according to the recipe being used). Finally, a glass bottle must be used on the kettle to bottle the resulting brew. It is important to keep the fire going constantly while the recipe is being prepared, otherwise it will be ruined. Swirls coming from the kettle is a sign all is well, black smoke (and brown liquid) indicates the brew is ruined. When a brew is ruined, the kettle must be emptied with a glass bottle , or just filled with more water, and the whole process repeated. When a brew is complete, the liquid will be the color of the resulting brew and will splutter (in addition to white swirls). Some recipes will require magical energy from a nearby Altar before they are bottled. If the brew is bottled when insufficient energy is available, the brew will be ruined. Black swirls coming from the Kettle is a good sign there is not enough power. A kettle must be within 14 (to 16) blocks of an Altar in order to obtain power from it (it can obtain power only from a single altar). All recipes can be found in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions , and they all require six ingredients. Automating the kettle with Pipes or Golems The kettle can be automated with buildcraft pipes and Thaumcraft Golems. It is a normal sided inventory. There are a few things to be mindful of though: You must pump 1000 mb of water into the kettle to begin (or use a decanting golem). You must only put one of each ingredient into the kettle (two in the case of some recipes). Before you can pull items out you must have sufficient glass bottles, these must also be placed into the kettle (with pipes or golems). This point differs from if you are doing it by hand, where you would use the empty bottle on the kettle. The witches hat bonus only applies if you decant by hand!

title: Koboldite Dust
content: Koboldite Dust is a rare material that can only really be found by Hobgoblins when they go digging. It can be formed into Nuggets and finally Ingots by Hobgoblins training in the process.

title: Koboldite Ingot
content: A Koboldite Ingot can only be forged from Koboldite Nuggets by a Hobgoblin that knows the secret. Simply trade with such a Hobgoblin, often including payment for their help. Koboldite Ingots are required to make a Statue of Goblin Patron , Koboldite Pickaxe and Pentacle .

title: Koboldite Nugget
content: A Koboldite Nugget can only be forged from Koboldite Dust by a Hobgoblin that knows the secret. Simply trade with such a Hobgoblin, often including payment for their help.

title: Koboldite Pickaxe
content: A pickaxe made from Koboldite is a very robust tool, acting to all extents as a Diamond Pickaxe in the hands of a player. Given to a Hobgoblin , its true properties become evident, slicing through materials like they were liquid. Mining speed for a Hobgoblins increases dramatically when holding a Koboldite Pickaxe, and they can auto smelt ores they mine, often at an increased yield. There is also a boost in the drop rate of Koboldite Dust. A Koboldite Pickaxe is made in a crafting bench using Koboldite Ingots , lava buckets, a Charged Attuned Stone and a Stick.

title: Lava Diviner
content: A lava diviner is a helpful tool for locating if there is a lava source somewhere below the clicked block. To use the diviner, right-click and hold on a block until either a success or fail sound is played (there will also be white or black smoke respectively). The lava diviner is crafted using a Water Diviner and Blaze Rods .

title: Leaping Lily
content: A Leaping Lily is a magically enhanced waterlily that can be placed on most surfaces. When a player or creature walks on the lily they will receive a very short-duration but extreme boost to jumping and also a very short-duration speed boost. A path made of these lilies is a quick way to move around or reach higher places safe from monsters. A leaping lily is crafted from Lily Pads , a Short-duration Potion of Speed , Breath of the Goddess and Glowstone Dust .

title: Leech Chest
content: A leech chest is a mutated plant that has been fused with a chest, to create a hybrid being that acts like a chest but takes a blood sample from any player who opens it. The chest is created by placing a Trapped Chest on the ground and surrounding it with Vines on it's four sides. The vines must themselves be over a water source block. When the setup is complete, the chest must be hit with a Mutating Sprig to mutate the vines and the chest together. See the image on the right for the correct set up. The Leech Chest behaves like a normal Trapped Chest , although it cannot join to a second chest to become a double chest. When the Leech chest is opened, a drop of blood from the player will be stored in one of the chests three blood sacs. Blood from the last three players to open the chest will always be stored, although only one sample from each unique player. If a Taglock Kit is used on a Leech Chest that has a player's blood sample in a sac, that sample will be removed and placed into the Taglock Kit for use in the normal ways ( poppets or curses ). Oddly, the chest will not allow a player to remove their own blood sample from it. Removal of a blood sample also requires the other player to be logged into the same world as the chest, although it is still possible to see the name of the player. If the Leech Chest is picked up and placed again, it will continue to store the last three blood samples it has taken.

title: Leonard's Urn
content: Leonard's Urns are magical artifacts that are rumored to allow the properties of a Brew that they filled with to be repeatedly cast using Mystic Branch. Tales have even been told, that urns can be combined together to allow up to four Brews to be added at a time. It seems that only a single Urn can be used at a time though.

title: Lilith
content: Lilith is a figure who crops up throughout folklore in various incarnations. She is purported to be the origin of the vampire curse, it being her blood that taints the original vampire in any family. Many have sought out her favor, only to disappear during the journey. It is rumored that she will grant a boon to whoever proves themselves worthy. Needless to say, should you chose to fight her, it may be hard. Some books mention an unnamed woman.

title: Lord of Torment
content: A Lord of Torment is a mighty demonic being that is able to create prisons to Torment its victims. Those foolish enough to summon one of these creatures must prepare for an arduous fight. A Lord of Torment may only be summoned using the Ritual outlined in a Torment Scroll . These scrolls are hard to come by, but it is rumored that well treated Flame Imps have occasionally given them up. Consuming the Soul of a Torment Demon grants the ability to Torment others.

title: Lycanthropy
content: A werewolf, also known as a lycanthrope, is a mythological or folkloric human with the ability to shapeshift into a wolf or a therianthropic hybrid wolf-like creature, either purposely or after being placed under a curse or affliction (from a bite or scratch from another werewolf). A coven of witches may perform the Curse of the Wolf to inflict lycanthropy on a player or villager , alternatively if a transformed werewolf that has been granted the ability to spread the curse, sufficiently damages (but does not kill) a victim, the curse may spread to them. It has been reported that appeals to the deity associated with this curse, may be answered with a way to obtain this curse. Players with lycanthropy The curse can progress through many stages as a werewolf develops in power, but to begin with, each night of a full moon, the player will be forcefully transformed into a full wolf form. When day comes around they will revert to their human form. The following changes will occur when transformed to a wolf: Wolf Form Shrunk to below one block in height (can walk under a 1 block high gap) Wolf model Cannot wear armor Much Faster movement speed Slightly Better jump (and leap) increased step height (no need to jump to ascend 1 block) Night vision Immunity to poison and disease Tougher (natural armor) Resistant to physical and magical damage Weak to silver attacks (take double damage and werewolf resistance is negated) Developing werewolf abilities The Curse of the Wolf can be very problematic for players, unless they learn to master it. Mastering the wolf requires the construction of a Wolf Altar and communing with the deity responsible for the curse. Once a Wolf Altar has been made and placed, using it will result in a task being set, completion of which will result in the player's werewolf abilities being improved. This can continue until all abilities are acquired. Each level brings a new ability, and increases the basic stats of existing abilities, such as attack damage, move speed, damage resistance, jump and leap, etc. Level Werewolf abilities 1 Player turns into Wolf Form on the night of a full moon, and returns to human form at other times 2 Player can carry a Moon Charm to prevent forced transformation, and use it to force transformation at will. Killing sheep in Wolf or Wolfman Form can result in Raw Lambchops being dropped. 3 Wolf Form gains the ability to instantly dig earth and sand (and occasionally find bones) 4 Kills from Wolf or Wolfman form will now be eaten, replenishing hunger loss 5 Player can now additionally transform to Wolfman Form using a Moon Charm (while sneaking) 6 Hitting while sprinting in Wolf of Wolfman form will now cause additional damage (crit from the air more so) 7 Wolfman Form gains the ability to sneak use (shift right-click) while looking straight up to howl and stun nearby creatures 8 Wolf Form gains the ability to sneak use (shift right-click) while looking straight up to howl to summon temporary wolf allies 9 Wolfman Form gains the ability to rend armor 10 Causing almost fatal damage while in Wolfman or Wolf Forms can now spread the curse to players or villagers. This can be disabled for player to player transmission by setting the configuration option: B:AllowPlayerToPlayerWolfInfection=false. Wolfman Form Wolfman model Cannot wear armor Will drop held object Slightly Faster movement speed Much higher jump (and leap) increased step height (no need to jump to ascend 1 block) Night vision Immunity to poison and disease Much tougher (natural armor) Significant damage increase Significant health increase Resistant to physical and magical damage Weak to silver attacks (take double damage and werewolf resistance is negated) Removing the Curse To-date only the Rite of Remove Curse has been know to remove this affliction.

title: Magic Mirror
content: Certain famous witches have been known to require a bit of a ego boost from time to time, and have found ways though magic to achieve this, in the form of the Magic Mirror. Such a mirror traps a demon who will provide certain services for its owner, including the aforementioned ego boosting. Once created, using a Rite of Infusion (a medium white circle containing a Brew of Hollow Tears , a Gold Ingot, a Glass Pane and a Demon ), the mirror may be placed on any two high wall (at the top block). Using the mirror will pose it the question of who is the fairest in the land, to which it may respond with the player, or perhaps another nearby NPC . If the player is not the fairest, they may have to take drastic measures to ensure they become so... The mirror when asked will always indicate the rough direction of the Fairest. Only the Fairest have Hearts of Gold (used in some potion making). Using the mirror will also reveal any players that may have stood in front of it since last it was used by the player who it deems the fairest. Using a Quartz Sphere on a mirror containing a demon will charge it with the players reflection creating a Duplication Grenade (assuming enough altar power is available). Throwing this sphere will create a duplicate of the bound player and cause mobs currently targeting the player to target that instead. Finally, using the Taglock Kit of another player on the mirror, when there is enough altar power available, will transform the player into a mirror image of the taglocked player. Using the players own Taglock Kit (or an empty one), can be used to turn the player back to their own form (this does not need altar power). Using other types of transformation (wolf, bat, etc.) will automatically reset the player's mirror form (so will Brews of Revealing). Use the config options: B:AllowChatMasquerading and B:AllowNameplateMasquerading to control these two aspects of this power. Stepping into a mirror Getting too close to a mirror can be risky, since the player can be sucked inside, and while this can be beneficial for an extra (and possibly portable) storage location, there is the problem of the bound demon to deal with. The demon in the mirror is a Reflection , and it will take on the form of the player who enters its mirror (and take revenge!).
