Slaying the reflection, makes the mirror hollow, which allows control of the space within the mirror, but now the mirror will no longer have a demon to ask questions or perform conversion magics. Hollow mirrors Mirrors without demons have another useful property when placed a short distance apart (up to 16 blocks) and back to back, in such a configuration they act as a doorway between each other. This effect can also occur vertically if placed over one another with the same facing. Mirrors within mirrors Placing a mirror on the wall while inside another mirror, does not appear to have any real effect, although altar power may allow it to break the barrier between two mirror spaces - ensure that you have a second mirror to place to allow the return journey (back to back mirrors do not need altar power). You may only exit mirror spaces by the mirror that you entered so keep this in mind, and if you do get stuck in a mirror with no way back, just chant: /chant mirror mirror send me home To go back to the original mirror space.

title: Mandrake Root
content: Mandrake Root is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is harvested from a fully grown Mandrake plant that must be grown from Mandrake seeds .

title: Mandrake Seeds
content: Mandrake seeds can be planted and eventually grow into the multi-stage mandrake plant. The fully grown mandrake plant can be harvested to obtain more Mandrake seeds and Mandrake root . Mandrake seeds can also be obtained as a random drop, by bashing tall grass .

title: Mandrake
content: According to the legend, when the root of the Mandrake plant is dug up it screams and kills all who hear it. This is only partially true. A fully grown Mandrake may scream causing immense discomfort to those around. This is why it is best to harvest the Mandrake root at night when the plant is more likely to be dormant. Mandrake is grown from Mandrake Seeds which may be obtained by breaking tall grass , or by breaking a partially (or fully) grown Mandrake plant. Breaking a fully grown Mandrake plant will either yield Mandrake Root or the Mandrake may awaken and cause all sorts of mischief (and pain to the ears!). Killing a Mandrake will yield Mandrake Root and Mandrake Seeds .

title: Meaty Stew
content: Meaty Stew is made by cooking Raw Meaty Stew in a furnace or Witches' Oven. Meaty Stew recovers a significant number of hunger bars and saturation. Also, despite using a bowl, it stacks to 64 items.

title: Mellifluous Hunger
content: Mellifluous Hunger is an ingredient used in other recipes. It is dropped when a Nightmare is killed.

title: Minedrake
content: The Minedrake plant is the insane result of crossing a mandrake with a creeper. Quite possibly one of the the most complex and dangerous of all of the plant mutations! Dropping a Minedrake bulb onto the ground will cause it to awaken within about 3 seconds. An awakened Minedrake will head towards the target of whomever awaken it and explode (the explosion is quite small and does not damage scenery). If a Minedrake bulb is not dropped by a player, but for example by harvesting, the Minedrake will still awaken after 3 seconds, but will chase after the nearest player! So pick the bulb up quickly. Minedrake bulbs can be planted in tilled earth, and will grow about 1.5 times slower than a mandrake plant. Harvesting a grown Minedrake plant will always drop a single bulb and has a chance to drop a second bulb. A lot of bone meal is required to fertilize Minedrakes. Mutating Minedrakes To obtain a Minedrake, a witch must first mutate a Creeper with a Mandrake . This is very tricky and hazardous. The Creeper and awakened Mandrake must be trapped in a Cobweb above Still Water. Fully grown Mandrake Plants must be located on the four sides of the Cobweb. Four Grassper plants must be located on the diagonal corners of the Cobweb. The Grassper plants must hold a Charged Attuned Stone , 2 Mutandis Extremis and a Focused Will , in any order.

title: Mog and Gulg
content: Mog and Gulg are the god-like beings that Hobgoblins worship. They may send their avatars to pay witches a visit who exploit their worship (with a Statue of Hobgoblin Patron). Rumor has it that using a Nether Star may make them take immediate notice. The avatars of Mog and Gulg should not be left close to one another! Gulg's Gurdle and Mog's Quiver are rumored to be powerful artifacts.

title: Mog's Quiver
content: Mog's Quiver is an epic artifact sometimes dropped by Mog's avatar . The Quiver is worn in the chest slot, and functions as leather armor (with much highter durability and repaired with leather). The quiver provides unlimited arrows for an equipped bow. Fired arrows travel much faster, and cause extreme damage to airborne (or jumping) creatures. Creatures hit will also get a weakness debuff. Being close to another player wearing Gulg's Gurdle , grants a resistance buff.

title: Moon Charm
content: A Moon Charm is an item that is gifted to werewolves via a Wolf Altar . When a player has achieved werewolf level 2 , and a Moon Charm is in a player's inventory, they will not be forced to Wolf Form during a full moon, nor to human form at other times. A second level werewolf that using a Moon Charm for a few seconds (right-click) will transform to wolf form, and back when used from wolf form. A fifth level werewolf that uses the Moon Charm while sneaking (shift right-click), will transform to Wolfman Form (and back to human when sneak-used from wolfman form). When under the effects of a Wolfsbane potion, transformation will not be possible. If a Moon Charm is lost or destroyed, a new one can be acquired by using a Wolf Altar while holding three Gold Ingots.

title: Mutandis Extremis
content: Mutandis Extremis is an upgraded version of Mutandis that is used by witches and warlocks to mutate plants into other species. Mutandis Extremis has all the same properties as basic Mutandis but it can even be used on multi-stage plants like wheat, cactus, etc. and can also mutate cooked meat. If Mutandis Extremis is used on a Grass block there is a chance it will mutate it into Mycelium and vice versa. If Mutandis Extremis is used on an underwater dirt block there is a chance it will mutate it (and nearby dirt) into clay blocks. Mutating plants is the only way to acquire Rowan , Hawthorn and Alder saplings. Some recipes require Mutandis Extremis as an ingredient. Mutandis is crafted using Mutandis and Netherwart . Recipe Prior to version 0.21.0

title: Mutandis
content: Mutandis is a mutagen that is used by witches and warlocks to mutate plants into other species. Simply use Mutandis on small plants, grass, flowers, saplings, etc. and it will be randomly converted to another plant. Mutandis cannot mutate multi-stage plants like wheat and cactus. Mutating plants is the only way to acquire Rowan , Hawthorn and Alder saplings. Mutandis has another interesting property when crafted with raw meat. It seems to mutate it into another type of raw meat. It even seems to have an effect on Rotten Flesh. Some recipes require Mutandis as an ingredient. Mutandis can be upgraded to Mutandis Extremis to have effect on a wider variety of plants. Mutandis is made in a Witches' Cauldron using Mandrake Root, Exhale of the Horned One and an Egg. Prior to version 0.21.0 Mutandis is crafted using Wood Ash , Bone Meal , Cactus Green , an Egg , Mandrake Root and a Hint of Rebirth .

title: Mutating Sprig
content: The Mutating Sprig brings a degree of order to the chaotic nature of Mutandis , allowing more limited, but also more predictable effects. It is a key tool in the creation of plant-based devices such as the Leech Chest . The Mutating Sprig is crafted in the crafting bench using an Ent Twig , netherwart and Mutandis Extremis . Use the Mutating Sprig on the following items to yield the corresponding effect. Block Effect Grass Mutates to Mycellium Mycellium Mutates to Grass Dirt (under water) Mutates to Clay Trapped Chest Mutates to a Leech Chest if surrounded with Vines fed by water (see Leech Chest page for set up image) Recipe prior to version 0.21.0

title: Mystic Branch Step-by-Step
content: To create a Mystic Branch , follow this step-by-step guide: Have an upgraded altar with at least 3000 power available (use a Skull and Chalice to increase power) Have a kettle built Create Mystic Unguent in the kettle Chop down Rowan , Hawthorn and Alder trees until an Ent comes. Kill it to get an Ent Twig Draw a heart glyph with Golden Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a small circle with Ritual Chalk Around the heart glyph draw a medium circle with Ritual Chalk Drop the Ent Twig and Mystic Unguent in the middle circle, check there is still 3000 power in the altar Wait until night time Activate the heart glyph and wait for the Mystic Branch to appear

title: Mystic Branch
content: A Mystic Branch allows a witch or warlock who has previously successfully applied a Rite of Infusion to themselves, to draw symbols into the air and produce limited magical effects. Each effect will drain a specific amount of their infused power. Certain symbols may be inaccessible depending on the type of infusion the player has. Symbols are drawn by holding the right mouse button and moving the mouse around in any direction. The symbols will appear in the air. If a pattern that makes sense is drawn, the name of the associated effect will appear. The effect can now be targeted normally and will occur when the mouse button is released. The Mystic Branch can also be used to deflect targeted effects produced by another Mystic Branch, in much the same way as a Ghast Fireball may be hit. Left-clicking with the branch held, just before the effect hits, will deflect the effect back at the caster. Some effects may be unblockable, so know your colors! Effects produced by symbol magic are much weaker that similar effects produced by other means (with one or two exceptions), but it is the utility of having so many possibilities in a single device that makes the Mystic Branch so interesting. The Swiss Army Knife of the witchcraft world, so to speak. Demonic effects A few spell effects can only be performed if the witch (or warlock) has consumed the soul of a specific type of demon, these demonic souls are obtained by dealing with Flame Imps or by defeating powerful demon lords . Boosting Power with Hobgoblin Worshipers Using a bound Statue of Hobgoblin Patron , it is possible to get Hobgoblins to worship it and redirect the power to the bound witch, instead of the Hobgoblin gods: Mog and Gulg . With a few worshipers, infusion power of the witch will recharge when near to the statue, with more worshipers, a symbol may be drawn larger to cause a more powerful (mostly AOE) effect. For example, defodio when drawn as up, up, up, left, left, down, down, right, right, will dig a 3x3 area. With many worshipers, even larger symbols may be drawn for even wider effects. For example, defodio when drawn as up, up, up, up, left, left, left, down, down, down, right right, right, will dig a 5x5 area. More worshipers also increases the chance of the avatars of Mog and Gulg paying the witch a visit. Gestures and effects Deflect branch effects Left click Grab item entity in world {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN} Create water {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN} Open door {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN} Fortify base by upgrading material type based on hardness {Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT} Lock a door by converting it to a harder to open door {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.RIGHT} Cause confusion {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT} Very minor damage (restricted use, expensive) {Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.RIGHT} Dig a block {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.DOWN } Remove stun effects {Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT} Heal at the cost of Hunger {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.RIGHT} Disarm {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.DOWN} Pushback {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.LEFT } Slows incoming person {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.RIGHT} Control creature (restricted use, expensive) {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN} Start fire on block {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN } Create light block { Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.RIGHT } Stop rain (very expensive) {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN} Break torches {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.UP} Shield (ground target) {Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP} Stun (slow to nothing) {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.RIGHT} Dark Mark (Shoot in the air, demonic magic consume Soul of Fear Demon ) {Stroke.UP, Stroke.UP, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT} Fleshy Feast (Gain power by consuming flesh, demonic magic consume Soul of Hunger Demon ) {Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN} Soulfire (Burn victims with the power of your own anguish, demonic magic consume Soul of Anguish Demon ) {Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.UP, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN} Torment (Torment your victim, demonic magic consume Soul of Torment Demon ) {Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.DOWN, Stroke.LEFT, Stroke.RIGHT, Stroke.RIGHT}

title: Mystic Unguent
content: Mystic Unguent is an ingredient used in a Rite of Infusion to create a Mystic Branch . It is very poisonous, so drinking it is not advised. The ointment is created in a kettle using the recipe in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Long-duration Potion of Strength , Diamond , Rowan Sapling , Creeper Heart and Demonic Blood .

title: Necromancers Robes
content: Necromancers robes are worn in the chest slot and have the same durability and protection as leather armor. They can be dyed and enchanted like leather armor too. If a necromantic brew (like the Brew of Raising ) is decanted while the robes are worn, there is a chance (about 45%) that an additional brew will be produced. This bonus stacks with the Witches Hat bonus (for a combined chance of 80%). While wearing the robes, undead creatures will generally ignore you ! Necromancers robes will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if normal chest armor was worn. Other players however will not be able to see the robes! The Necromancers robes are crafted from Impregnated Leather , Golden Thread and a Necromantic Stone . Crafting damaged necromantic robes with four Impregnated Leather will repair the robes. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Necromantic Stone
content: A Necromantic Stone is very dark magic indeed, it allows its user to force raised undead creatures to attack whatever it is used on. The Necromantic Stone is created using the Rite of Necromancy . To use the Stone, first raise some undead using a Brew of Raising . Then equip the stone and use it at range on a target player or creature. The raised undead will begin attacking the unfortunate creature. This stone does not work on undead creatures that the wielder has not summoned! If the stone is pointed at a block and used, raised undead will move to that block. The Necromantic Stone is also the only way to activate and deactivate an Alluring Skull .

title: News
content: Things that may be interesting concerning Witchery...

title: Nightmare
content: Nightmares are creatures of the Spirit World that are created when someone who enters the world is experiencing a nightmare as they dream. Only the player having a nightmare will be bothered by this being. This creature is fast and deadly and when hit will become invulnerable for a few seconds. Killing a Nightmare is a fruitless task, since another will almost instantly appear. The best defense is to run, or find a way to remove the bad dream. Nightmares also have the habit of making you feel naked. Protection comes in the form of a Charm of Fanciful Thoughts . Nightmares drop Mellifluous Hunger when killed.

title: Null Catalyst
content: Witch Hunter's long sought the answer to reducing the risk of fighting witches. It was the discovery of Null Catalyst, made from powdered nether star, that enabled them to create a new generation of weapons to combat their foe. Null Catalyst has two recipes, the first is made in a crafting table using a Nether Star, Magma Cream, Flint and a Diamond. The second recipe is made using Null Catalyst, Magma Cream and Blaze Powder.

title: Nullified Leather
content: Nullified Leather is an extremely durable form of leather that has a neutralizing effect on magic. Nullified Leather is made in a Crafting Table using Leather and Null Catalyst .

title: Nullifying Bolt
content: The Nullifying bolt is ammunition for the Witch Hunter Pistol Crossbow . The bolt is the Witch Hunters main weapon against Witches and other magic users. The bolt behaves like a Wooden Bolt in that it does 3 hearts base damage and penetrates Bark Belts and Baba yaga 's thorns. If fired by a player wearing the full Witch Hunter Armor set, it will also drain the target of 1/3 of their magical power and ignore any poppet-based protections they may have ( Vampiric and Death Protection Poppets ). It will also remove any magical potion effects (excluding poison and wither). Firing such a bolt will also completely drain the shooters magical power if they have any. The Nullifying Bolt is made using Bone Bolts , Null Catalyst and Belladonna .

title: Observations of an Immortal
content: This rare to find, often incomplete, book, seems to be the journal of a scholar who was a guest of a Vampire . What its contents can tell a reader can only be guessed at. The book is most often found in libraries and book archives, although Torn Pages from it have been found all over. Crafting the Pages with the book adds them to it.

title: Odour of Purity
content: Odour of Purity is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Hawthorn saplings in an oven .

title: Oil of Vitriol
content: Oil of Vitriol is an ingredient produced in a distillery by using Foul Fume and Quicklime .

title: Otherwhere Chalk
content: Otherwhere chalk is used to draw each of the purple otherwhere glyphs that make up an otherwhere circle. Otherwhere chalk and glyphs have a strong affinity with the Ender dimension and rites involving them will often concern transposition. To draw an otherwhere glyph, use the chalk on the ground. Otherwhere glyphs of various types will be drawn, although they all have the same function. If you use the chalk on an existing glyph, a new one will be drawn in its place. Otherwhere glyphs should be arranged in the roughly circular patterns as illustrated in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic . Otherwhere chalk is crafted in a witches' cauldron using netherwart, tear of the goddess, ender pearl and ritual chalk. Recipe prior to version 0.21.0 Otherwhere chalk is crafted from Ritual Chalk , Water Artichoke Globes , an Ender Pearl and Lapis Lazuli . In version 0.5.1 and earlier this chalk was crafted with Belladonna Seeds in place of Water Artichoke Globes.

title: Oven
content: The witches oven, made infamous in the tale of Hansel and Gretel, is a very practical device for the would-be witch or warlock. While unable to smelt metals , the oven is particularly good at cooking food and making charcoal ; cooking about 10% faster than a furnace . The second, and probably more important feature of a witches oven, is that it is able to capture the by-products produced in the cooking process . Activating the oven to show the interface screen, you will see an additional slot to the right of the fuel slot. Clay jars must be placed in this slot. If there are clay jars present when something is cooked, there is a chance a by-product will be captured in one of the jars. These by-products are important ingredients in other recipes. If there are no clay jars in the oven when something is cooked, no by-products will be produced. The oven is crafted using Iron Ingots and Iron Bars . The book, Witchcraft: Collecting Fumes describes the use of the oven to collect by-products. The by-products that can be produced by an oven depend on what is being cooked, the most important ones will com e from cooking tree saplings, which are a very important component in witchcraft. Oven by-products Cooked item Possible by-product Raw food Foul fume Logs Foul fume Oak Sapling Exhale of the Horned One Birch Sapling Breath of the Goddess Spruce Sapling Hint of Rebirth Rowan Sapling Whiff of Magic Hawthorn Sapling Odour of Purity Alder Sapling Reek of Misfortune Jungle Sapling Foul fume Upgrading an Oven An oven can be upgraded by placing either a Fume Funnel or a Filtered Fume Funnel to the left and right of it. Each Fume Funnel will increase the cooking speed by and additional 10% (over a furnace) and increase the chance of a by-product being produced by and additional 25% for a Fume Funnel or 30% for a Filtered Fume Funnel . An Oven can use at most two upgrades and they must have the same facing as the oven. A fully upgraded oven will therefore cook at 130% speed (compared to a furnace) and have a chance of a by-product of 90%. Recipe prior to verion 0.21 The oven is crafted using Iron Ingots , a Bucket and Iron Bars .

title: Owl
content: Owls are not naturally occurring ambient creatures that must be added to the world by mutating them from a pair of other creatures. Owls can be attracted with a Raw Porkchop or Raw Beef , but will fly off if a player moves. Owls may be tamed by giving them a Raw Porkchop or Raw Beef . Once tamed an owl will follow its owner, unless instructed to sit by right-clicking on it. The owl can be told to move again by right-clicking on it. Two tamed owls will breed (producing an owlet) if fed a Raw Porkchop or Raw Beef when they have full health. A tamed owl can be healed by feeding it a Raw Porkchop or Raw Beef . A tamed owl's feather color can be changed by using a dye on the owl. A tamed owl can be turned into a Witch's Familiar by using the Rite of Binding on it and the player. Owls drop feathers and, occasionally, Owlet's Wing when killed. Owls are created by mutating a wolf (or dog) with a bat . Catch 2 to 4 bats using a Critter Snare . Place a spider web above a still water block and trap a wolf (or dog) in the spider web. Surround the web on 2 - 4 sides with Critter Snare plants containing a trapped bat. Place Grassper plants on the diagonal corners of the web. Place a charged attuned stone in one of the Grasspers and Mutandis Extremis in the other three. Finally, use a mutating sprig on the web, an Owl will be created for each trapped bat (so 2 - 4). It is probably best to do this, in an enclosed space so they don't just fly off before they can be tamed! Delivering Items Tamed Owls can also be used to deliver an item to a location or player. Use the item on a owl to get it to take the item. Next use a Bound Waystone or a Blooded Waystone on the owl, to tell the owl where to go (the Waystone is not consumed when doing this). Finally, if the owl is sitting, stop it sitting. The owl will now fly to the target location, drop off the item, and return back to where it started (and sit). If the target creature or location is not loaded, the owl will not take flight. If the target creature or location is more than 128 blocks away, the owl will teleport some of the distance to save some time and reduce the risk of entering an unloaded chunk. If an owl is given a splash potion or a throwable brew , it will throw its cargo instead of just dropping it.

title: Owlet's Wing
content: Owlet's Wing is an occasional drop when an Owl is killed. It is an ingredient using in other recipes.

title: Parasytic Louse
content: A parasytic Louse is a hybrid of plant and silverfish that will movew towards and try to bite whoever is near. Its bite does almost no damage. A Parasytic louse can be picked up by interacting with it (right-click), or killed by hitting it. A Parasytic louse that has been picked up, can be placed in the world by using it on a block. A parasytic louse can be fed any drinkable potion by crafting the louse with the potion in a crafting table. If the louse subsequently bites a player, the potion will be applied to that player though the bite. The louse must now be fed another potion. If a louse has been fed a potion and the louse is on a players hot bar, if the player is hit by another creature, the louse will bite the player or the attacker and inject the potion (beneficial potions apply to the wearer, negative potions the attacker). Multiple louses can be placed on a player's hotbar, but only one will bite per hit. A Parasytic Louse is created by mutating a Silverfish with Lily Pads . Place a Critter Snare containing a silverfish on the ground and surrounding it with Lily Pads each above a still water block. Place Grassper plants on the diagonal corners of the Critter Snares. Place a charged attuned stone in one of the Grasspers, Tongue of Dog in another and Mutandis in the other two. Finally, use a mutating sprig on the Critter Trap, a Paraysitic Louse will be created from the Silverfish. Right-click to pick up the Louse.

title: Pentacle
content: A Pentacle refers to a pentagram circumscribed by a circle. This form of pentacle is formed upon a disk which may be used either upon an altar or as a sacred space of its own. The pentacle is representative of the Earth in occult usage. When placed on a Altar , the pentacle will double the Altar's rate of recharge. A Pentacle is made in a crafting table using Koboldite Ingots , Koboldite Nuggets and a diamond.

title: Pitfall Block
content: A Pitfall Block is a magically created replica of either a Dirt or Grass block, the major difference is that creatures will pass straight through the block if walked over or through. This property of the blocks make them ideal cover for Pitfall Traps and the like. Unlike their standard equivalents, plants and such will not grow on these blocks. A pitfall block is made in a Witches' Cauldron according to the recipe in Witches' Brews > Crafting > Pitfall Blocks.

title: Player Compass
content: This handy device can be crafted with the taglock of another player. When so activated, it will point in the direction that that player is in, in much the same way as a regular compass points to the player's spawn point. This ability only works when both players are in the same dimension. It is always possible to craft the compass with another player's taglock, to replace who the compass points at. If there is no taglock associated with the compass, not pointer will be displayed. If the player pointed at by the compass is not logged in, or is in a different dimension, the compass will points randomly (this random effect will also occur if the taglock of a creature is used in place of a player's). The Player Compass is created in a Witches' Cauldron , for the recipe see the page in Witches' Brews > Crafting > Player Compass.

title: Poltergeist
content: A Poltergeist may appear when summoning other spectral undead. The are invisible and like to throw objects around, open doors and cause mischief. The may even start opening the chest of their accidental summoner! Use a Brew of Revealing to spot them! Using Graveyard Dust on this creature, if controlled by the player, will increase its maximum health by two points, to a maximum of 50 health.

title: Polynesia Charm
content: The Polynesia Charm allows its user to communicate with animals (and other docile beasts like spiders). Through this communication, the user can trade with the animal, which provides an alternate way to acquire ingredients that may otherwise be hard to find. There is also a tiny chance that the creature may have found something truly exceptional. It may also be possible to trade with some types of monster if you can find one or two ways to make them ignore you. To use the Polynesia Charm, right-click on a non-aggressive creature while the charm is held. Only adult, non-aggressive creature will respond. To craft a Polynesia Charm, use a Raw Fish , Iron Ingot , Nether Wart , Whiff of Magic and Odour of Purity . The Polynesia Charm is a main ingredient in making the Devils Tongue Charm .

title: Poppets
content: In witchcraft, a poppet, sometimes known as a Moppet, Mommet or Pippy is a doll made to represent a person, for casting spells on that person, or aid them through magic. These dolls are fashioned from simple natural materials and stuffed with items of magical significance with the intent that actions performed upon the subject will be transferred to the effigy based on sympathetic magic. In rarer cases the sympathy may be reversed so that actions performed on the effigy will be transferred to the subject. Making a poppet Making a poppet is a three step process: First the basic doll is made Next the doll must be filled with items appropriate to the desired magic effect Finally the sympathetic link between the poppet and a living being must be created by filling it with a taglock Collecting taglocks Taglocks are items that represent the individual on which magic is to be performed. They typically take the form of a lock of hair, fingernail clippings or a drop of blood. Taglocks are collected with a taglock kit , either directly by using it on the individual, or by using it on a bed on which that person has slept. Binding a poppet to a player or creature In the following example we have an unbound Earth Protection Poppet , in order to bind this to a player (or creature) we must have a used taglock Kit , then simply craft the two together as shown. A Vampiric Poppet requires two Taglock Kits , one bound to the victim, the other the beneficiary. Place both Taglock Kits and the Poppet in the crafting table at the same time, with the beneficiaries' kit first and the victim's second. The tooltip for the resulting bound poppet should indicate the direction damage will flow. Protection Poppets The majority of poppets are protection poppets, that is they take an effect instead of the bound player. For this to occur, the poppet must either be held in the players inventory, or located on a special Poppet Shelf . Poppet Effect Earth Protection Poppet Avoid death from falling Water Protection Poppet Avoid death from drowning Fire Protection Poppet Avoid death from burning Hunger Protection Poppet Avoid death from starvation Voodoo Protection Poppet Avoid all voodoo effects Poppet Protection Poppet Avoid the effects of the Curse of Corrupt Poppet Tool Protection Poppet Avoid a tool breaking Armor Protection Poppet Avoid a piece of armor breaking Death Protection Poppet Avoid death (irrespective of cause) Black Magic Poppets Just as there is beneficial poppet making, so too is there a darker side to this art. Poppet Effect Voodoo Poppet Cause nasty effects on the victim involving needles and other things Vampiric Poppet Redirect damage to another being Poppet Shelves A Poppet Shelf is a special magical construct that overcomes the limitation that a poppet must be held for it to have an effect. This makes the poppet shelf very useful, especially because its effect can span across dimensions.

title: Poppet Protection Poppet
content: The poppet protection poppet will prevent the effects of the Curse of Corrupt Poppet from taking effect on the bound player or creature, causing some damage to the poppet in the process. The poppet will also redirect the magic back to its source in the form of a lightning bolt. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere. The poppet is crafted using a Voodoo Protection Poppet (unbound), Toe of Frog , Ender Dew , Drop of Luck and Gold nuggets .

title: Poppet Shelf Compass
content: The Witch Hunter's long ago discovered that the secret to a witch's power was their use of Poppets combined with Poppet Shelves . They developed a way to track down such devices and so cut off this capability. The Poppet Shelf Compass illuminates its crystals the closer the Witch Hunter gets to a Poppet Shelf . The Poppet Shelf Compass is made in a crafting table using a Clock, Gold Ingots, Diamonds and Null Catalyst .

title: Poppet Shelf
content: The poppet shelf overcomes the limitation that protection poppets must be held in the bound players inventory. This alone makes the object especially powerful, because there is now no limit to how many protection poppets that can be active for a player, and there is no longer an inconvenience that the poppets fill valuable inventory space. Because of this, a poppet shelf is very expensive, and has a limited inventory. Poppet shelves will function across dimensions. Poppet shelves will keep chunks containing them loaded, so use them sparingly! The poppet shelf is crafted using Attuned Stones , Nether Brick and Green Wool .

title: Poppet
content: A poppet is the basic representation of a being that will be specialized for a specific purpose. The poppet is crafted in version 0.6.0 and above using Wool , Spanish Moss , String and a Bone Needle . The poppet is crafted in version 0.5.1 and below using one less Spanish Moss and one more Wool.

title: Purified Milk
content: Purified Milk is an antidote in a similar way to normal milk, although it only has a chance of working and will only remove a single status effect . It is advantageous because it can stack to 64 items. Purified Milk is an ingredient in the Brew of Sleeping . Purified Milk is produced by mixing Milk with Odour of Purity in Clay Jars .

title: Quartz Sphere
content: A Quartz Sphere is a component used in the production of a Crystal Ball . It is made in a crafting grid using Nether Quartz , Blocks of Quartz and a Glass .

title: Quicklime
content: Quicklime is an ingredient used in distillation and other recipes. It is crafted by placing Wood Ash into a crafting grid (in much the same way bonemeal is crafted from bones). Quicklime is an irritant, it can be thrown by right-clicking with it equipped. Any creature hit by the Quicklime will be temporarily blinded and take a tiny bit of damage (if on full health). Slimes and Magma Cubes will take much more damage.

title: Raw Lambchop
content: Raw Lambchop is a food item that may be dropped when a player in Wolf (at level 2) or Wolfman Form kills a sheep. The Raw Lambchop may be eaten to restore some lost hunger, or cooked to obtain Cooked Lambchop which restores more hunger.

title: Raw Meaty Stew
content: Raw Meaty Stew is made by mixing most raw meats or fish, with carrot, potato, brown mushroom and garlic in a bowl. The result must then be cooked in a furnace or witches' oven, to make Meaty Stew . Eating Raw Meaty Stew will recover a minor amount of hunger bars, it is much better to cook it.

title: Raw Porkchop?
content: This "meat" is created by crafting Rotten Flesh with Mutandis in a crafting table. It can be cooked and eaten, but is most useful in the creation of Demonic Contracts .

title: Redstone Soup
content: Redstone soup is a witches brew that forms the base for all infusions , although it does have other uses. Filling a chalice with redstone soup will increase its magical affinity and upgrade it to a filled chalice , doubling its effectiveness. Drinking redstone soup will boost the health of the drinker for a few minutes. Redstone Soup is made in a kettle . Into the pot, throw Redstone , Drop of Luck , Wool of Bat , Tongue of Dog , Belladonna and Mandrake Root .

title: Reek of Misfortune
content: Reek of Misfortune is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Alder saplings in an oven .

title: Refined Evil
content: Refined Evil is an ingredient made in a distillery according to the recipe in the book Witchcraft: Distilling , using Diamond Vapor and a Ghast Tear .

title: Reflection
content: A Reflection is a type of demon that has been bound to a Magic Mirror . The demon will take on the form of the player who is closest to it, using their armor and weapons (and sometimes other abilities) against them. It is possible to pull such a demon from their mirror, by performing a Rite of Summoning on the mirror.

title: Remove Curse of the Wolf
content: Removing the Curse of the Wolf is reformed like a normal Rite of Remove Curse, with the foci listed in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic. There are however a few additional details that need to be known. First of all the rite must be performed under the light of a full moon. Secondly, and more importantly, if the target has progressed in their werewolf skill, then the removal will only take effect if they are standing within the circles when the rite is performed.

title: Rite of Banishing
content: The Rite of Banishing will return all Demons within the circle back to where they were summoned from. Unfortunately this does not work on the Wither . The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Beastial Call
content: The Rite of Beastial Call transposes most animals in a wide area to the location of the Rite. This rite must be performed with at least 3 additional witches. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Binding
content: The rite of binding is used to bind something in the vicinity to an item. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . Circle Talisman Using a Circle Talisman in the rite will bind all of the drawn circles around the heart glyph, to the talisman, so they may be drawn automatically at a later time. Waystone Using a Waystone in the rite will bind the location of the rite to the Waystone so that it can later be used to target the bound location; for example in a Rite of Transposition . Adding a bound Waystone to the rite will instead copy the binding to the blank Waystone . A final variation of this rite binds the location of a player, creating a Blooded Waystone . Familiar Using a suitable tamed animal (cat, toad or owl), this Rite will summon a minor demon to inhabit that creature becoming the witch's (or warlock's) familiar . Fetish Object A Scarecrow , Trent Effigy or Witch's Ladder can be used to bind spectral creatures in various combinations and to achieve various effects. See the book, Witchcraft: Conjuration & Fetishes for a complete list of recipes. Spectral Stone Up to three aggressive spectral creatures of the same type can be bound to a spectral stone and summoned from it at a later date when needed. Statue of Hobgoblin Patron Binds the witch standing in the circle to the Statue so that it can be used to redirect the power of Hobgoblin worship to the witch.

title: Rite of Broiling
content: The Rite of Broiling is a convenient way for a witch or warlock to quickly cook a batch of food, so long as they do not mind some of it getting burnt. A witches oven is another option with the added benefit of allowing the collection of Foul Fume. Any raw food items present in the circle when the rite is completed will be instantly cooked (some may get turned to charcoal though). The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Broken Earth
content: The Rite of Broken Earth causes the earth to rupture into a crevasse emanating from the edge of the circle. The initial direction of the crevasse is determined by the placement of the foci item relative to the heart glyph. Beware, as the earth parts it may turn back on the caster. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Charging
content: The Rite of Charging has many flavors depending on what needs to be charged. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . Charging an attuned stone The simplest form of this rite involves transferring the power from an altar into an attuned stone , so that it may be used later in rites instead of needing an altar to be present. Charging an infused player The most complex version of this rite will recharge a player that has been infused with a dimensional aspect so that they may resume using its power. Once the rite has started, it will continue so long as there is enough power in the altar . Infusion power will regenerate each second the player stands in the innermost circle.

title: Rite of Earth's Wrath
content: The Rite of Earth's Wrath will cause a volcano to erupt out of the circle. This rite also requires that the heart glyph is drawn over a lava pool (of reasonable depth and size) , since it is the already existing lava that is brought to the surface. A lava diviner is the perfect tool to locate suitable sites for this rite. This rite is a convenient way to rapidly transport lava (technically it's magma) to the surface. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Fertility
content: The Rite of Fertility will cause all blocks in a very wide area around the circle to act as if they have had bonemeal applied to them. Also any zombie villagers in the area of effect will be cured (turned back to normal villagers ). Any players in the area of effect under the effects of Nausia, Blindness or Poison will also be cured. If this rite is cast by a witch with a Cat familiar , all hit players will also be granted a regeneration and saturation buff. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Glyphic Transformation
content: The Rite of Glyphic Transformation is an expensive, but convenient way to change the type of glyphs in a magic circle. The rite requires an undamaged chalk of the desired type to be added to the foci items. 1 chalk stick will convert a small circle. 2 chalk sticks a medium circle and 3 chalk sticks a large circle. This Rite will consume a single chalk stick regardless of how many are used. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Icy Expansion
content: The Rite of Icy Expansion causes a sphere of ice to expand outwards, filling with air when it is complete. This rite is a great way to create a breathing space under to ocean . This rite must be performed with at least 2 additional witches. More witches make the sphere significantly larger. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Imprisonment
content: The Rite of Imprisonment will prevent monsters from exiting the confines of the circle. Once a monster enters the circle they will be trapped. This rite does not prevent trapped monsters from shooting out of the circle however. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Infusion
content: The Rite of Infusion has many flavors depending on the infusion that is to be performed, although all have the same effect of infusing the essence of a dimensional aspect into all players standing within the circle. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . Infusing an item When infusing an item (such as a broom , twig , mirror or quartz sphere ), both the item and the infusion must be placed in the circles before the rite is activated. The resulting item will appear on completion of the rite. Infusing players When infusing players , the players and the infusion must be placed in the circle before the rite is activated. When the rite is completed, all players will either be infused or dead (if they do not have a magical means of preventing death available to them). Infused Brews Using an Infused Brew Base and other specialized ingredients (often including a living component), it is possible to create very long duration brews such as the Infused Brew of Soaring and the Infused Brew of the Grave .

title: Rite of Manifestation
content: The Rite of Manifestation allows players standing in the circle when it is cast to pass through Spirit Portals for a limited duration. Spirit Portals can be created in the Spirit World as doorways into the real world. If a player that has undergone this rite passes though a portal they will manifest as a ghostly figure in the real world for a limited time. When this time expires they will return to the spirit world. The duration is 2.5 minutes. Although this increases by 25 seconds for each Witch in the players coven .

title: Rite of Moving Earth
content: The Rite of Moving Earth raises a column of earth the diameter of the circle some distance into the air. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Nature's Power
content: The Rite of Nature's power will cause plants and trees to begin growing in the immediate area of the circle. If the surrounding area is sand or stone, it will be terraformed into grass with the occasional pool. This rite is a way for a witch or warlock to establish a grotto in a desert or cave. The terraforming process can take a while, but the rite will eventually stop. As of witchery 0.23 the blocks that can be replaced by this rite can be expanded by adding there unlocalized name to the config file using the key: NaturesPowerReplaceableBlocks.

title: Rite of Necromancy
content: The Rite of Necromancy is used to create both a Necromantic Stone from an Attuned Stone and a Spectral Stone from a Necromantic stone; to allow some control over the undead. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . At night time, using a small 7x7 circle drawn with Ritual Chalk and an Altar with 1000 power, place the following foci in the circle: an Attuned Stone , a Bone , Rotten Flesh , Wood Ash , Iron Sword and Spectral Dust . Then begin the ritual.

title: Rite of Prior Incarnation
content: The Rite of Prior Incarnation is a practical use of necromancy. The rite summons a prior incarnation of a player at the time that they died, the truly useful thing about this rite, is that the rite also resurrects any items that may have despawned after the last death. This rite must be performed within 16 blocks of where the player died, and the player in question must stand in the circle. Only despawned items from the last death will be recovered. Don't die on the way back! The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . The Rite of Prior incarnation can be enabled/disabled in the Witchery config file, and it will automatically be disabled if a known Death Chest style mod is found (or Keep Inventory mode is active). In general, having more than one death-chest style effect active at once will cause problems (please note that OpenBlocks also includes such a feature!)

title: Rite of Protection
content: The Rite of Protection raises a shimmering dome around above and below the circle that nothing may cross. This rite requires a constant source of power after its initial period, or it will fade. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Remove Curse
content: Variants of the Rite of Remove Curse are some of the only known way to cure a player (or creature) inflicted with a Curse of Misfortune , a Curse of Insanity or any of the other known curses. Removing the Curse of the Wolf , does require a little more knowledge however. The rite is also incidentally the only way to make the curses worse. To perform the rite, a taglock from the cursed being must be collected with a Taglock Kit and used as a foci in the rite. The Rite of Remove Curse is one of only two ways to convert a player from being a Vampire . The other way requires a Demon's intervention. If the rite is successful, white particle effects will be seen in the circle. If the rite fails, fiery effects will be seen, and the curse affecting the player (or creature) will get worse. If the witch or warlock performing the Rite of Remove Curse is as skilled as the player who cast the curse, there is a good chance the curse will be removed. If their skill is less (and the curse has become stronger) the chance of curing the curse will drop dramatically, requiring significantly more attempts to perform the cure, with each failure causing the curse to strengthen! The cure only needs to succeed once for the complete curse (irrespective of how strong it has become) to be removed. This rite will start one level higher if cast by a witch or warlock with a Cat familiar . The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Sanctity
content: The Rite of Sanctity will prevent monsters from entering the bounds of the circle. It will not stop them firing upon circle occupants however. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Shifting Seasons
content: The Rite of Shifting Seasons will cause the biomes in a large circular area centered on the Ritual to change according to the foci used in the Rite. In addition to the basic foci for the rite, one of the following Foci needs to be added to determine the type of biome to convert to: Biome Group Foci Item Forest Oak Sapling Plains Tall Grass Mountain Obsidian Hills Stone Swamp Slimeball Water Bucket of Water Desert Cactus Frozen Icy Needle Jungle Jungle Sapling Wasteland Netherrack Beech Sand Mushroom Red Mushroom Magical Skull After this, zero or more Glowstone Dust can be added, this selects which biome of the selected type should be chosen. The final biome selected is dependent on which biomes are installed by any mods. Please note, vanilla minecraft has no magical biomes, so use of this rite to convert to a magical biome in vanilla would be a waste. The Book of Biomes is a useful reference guide for the costs of the different biomes. This rite must be performed with at least 4 additional witches, more than this increases the area of effect of the rite. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Sky's Wrath
content: The Rite of Sky's Wrath will call down the sky's fury in the form of an intense lightning storm. The foci used in the rite will determine of the lightning is focused - Either within the circle or around it. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Summoning
content: The Rite of Summoning is a way for a witch or warlock to manifest a being in our dimension that does not necessarily exist in that form elsewhere. In this respect it is different from the Rite of Transposition , where the being already exists and is merely moved from one place to another. A Rite of Summoning can be used to summon the following beings: Demon Flame Imp Wither Reflection Witch Spectral Familiar Dismissed witches familiar For a summoning to be successful the area around the heart glyph to a distance of four blocks must be mostly free of obstructions. The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of the Forest
content: The Rite of the Forest allows a witch (or warlock) to cause a forest to grow of a chosen type. The location of the forest can be selected by adding a bound waystone to the Rite. the type of forest can be selected by the Sapling used in the rite. The foci items and circles required can be found in the book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Total Eclipse
content: The Rite of Total Eclipse causes an eclipse of the sun to occur. To all extents and purposes, night has fallen. On a server with more than 2 players present in a world, the following configuration option can be set to add a cooldown between uses of this rite. The number can be a number of seconds between 0 (disabled) and 3600 (once per hour). I:RiteOfEclipseCooldownInSecs=0 The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic .

title: Rite of Transposition
content: The rite of Transposition has many forms, although each to do with transposing creatures and items from one place to another. The main foci for this rite is the Waystone . The circles, foci items and power needs of this rite, like all other rites , can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . Teleporting to another location Using a Waystone that has been bound to a specific location, or creature (using a Rite of Binding ), all beings and items in the circle will be teleported to the bound location or entity. Teleporting a creature to this location Using a Taglock Kit that has been bound to a player or creature; the being will be teleported to the location of the rite. Transposing iron to the surface All iron in ore blocks will be transposed to the surface ready for collection, leaving only stone behind.

title: Ritual Chalk
content: Ritual chalk is used to draw each of the white ritual glyphs that make up a ritual circle. It is also used as an ingredient in crafting the other types of chalk. To draw a ritual glyph, use the chalk on the ground. Ritual glyphs of various types will be drawn, although they all have the same function. If you use the chalk on an existing ritual glyph, a new one will be drawn in its place. Ritual chalk is crafted from Gypsum , Wood Ash and Tear of the Goddess . Ritual glyphs should be arranged in the roughly circular patterns as illustrated in the book Witchcraft: Circle Magic . With a heart glyph drawn with Golden Chalk in the center.

title: Rowan Berries
content: Rowan berries are edible and will restore half a drumstick of hunger. They will also substantially increase saturation. Rowan berries will sometimes drop from decaying Rowan leaves .

title: Rowan Door Key
content: A Rowan door key is created when a Rowan wood door is placed on the ground. Holding that key is the only way to open the placed door. Breaking and replacing the door in the same location is the only way to generate multiple keys. Multiple Rowan Door Keys may be combined together into a Rowan Keyring .

title: Rowan Keyring
content: The Rowan Keyring is a convenient way to combine all of a player's Rowan Door Keys into a single item. The keyring can hold an unlimited number of keys, and can be used just like the key to open any Rowan Wood Door for which it holds a key. Once a key is added to the keyring it can no longer be removed again. To craft place two Rowan Door Keys into a crafting bench. To add further keys craft the Keyring with another Rowan Door Key .

title: Rowan Leaves
content: Rowan leaves are harvested from Rowan trees in the same way vanilla leaves are harvested (i.e. with shears). Bashed or decaying Rowan leaves have a chance to drop Rowan saplings , and sometimes Rowan berries . Rowan leaves will decay like vanilla leaves when all logs on a Rowan tree are removed. Rowan trees are grown from Rowan Saplings .

title: Rowan Log
content: Rowan logs are harvested from Rowan trees in the same way vanilla logs are harvested (i.e. with an axe). Rowan trees are grown from Rowan saplings and can be made into Rowan planks .

title: Rowan Planks
content: Rowan planks behave like all vanilla wooden planks and can be used in a majority of recipes that require wooden planks. Rowan planks are crafted from Rowan logs in the same way all other woods are crafted from logs.

title: Rowan Sapling
content: Rowan saplings grow into Rowan trees when planted. Rowan trees can be harvested for Rowan logs and Rowan leaves (which yield more Rowan saplings). Rowan saplings cooked in an oven yield Wood Ash , and have a chance of producing a Whiff of Magic . Rowan saplings can initially be acquired only by using Mutandis on another planted sapling (does not matter which type). Given the mutation of the sapling is random it may take a few goes before Rowan saplings appear.

title: Rowan Wood Door
content: An Rowan wood door, apart from looking distinctive from a normal wooden door, also has a couple of unique differences. The first, when placed, a key will be produced, the door will only open when this key is used to open it. Destroy and place the door a second time to obtain another key . The second difference is that a redstone signal will only ever close the door, never open it. Multiple Rowan Door Keys may be combined together into a Rowan Keyring . The Rowan wood door is crafted using Rowan planks and Whiff of Magic .

title: Ruby Slippers
content: Ruby Slippers are worn in the foot slot and have the same durability and protection as leather boots. They can be enchanted like leather boots too. They cannot be dyed. Once every 30 minutes, if a player is infused , if the player chants " There's no place like home " into the chat or uses the command: /chant there's no place like home They will be teleported back to their spawn point in the same dimension, this ability costs 40% of the players total infused power (or 60% if the teleport is cross-dimensional). Alternatively, once every 60 seconds the player may drop a Bound Waystone near to themselves and use the above chant to teleport to the location of the waystone (the waystone will be consumed!). This ability saves the need to draw a circle to use the Rite of Transposition . This ability costs 20% of the players total infused power (or 40% if the teleport is cross-dimensional). Ruby Slippers will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if normal shoes were worn. Other players however will not be able to see the slippers! The Ruby Slippers are crafted from Seeping Shoes , Golden Thread , Attuned Stones and Demonic Blood . Crafting damaged slippers with four Impregnated Leather will repair them. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Scarecrow
content: A Scarecrow is an object that can be used as a Fetish when spectral undead are bound to it using a Rite of Binding . Using a dye on a placed scarecrow will change the color of its robe. It is made using Wool, Sticks, Tormented Twine and a Jack'o'lantern.

title: Seeping Shoes
content: Seeping Shoes are worn in the foot slot and have the same durability and protection as leather boots. They can be dyed and enchanted like leather boots too. If the wearer is standing on the ground, their Seeping Shoes will cause any poison effects to seep into the ground negating the poison and causing a bonemeal effect to the surrounding area. Seeping Shoes will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if normal shoes were worn. Other players however will not be able to see the shoes! The Seeping Shoes are crafted from Impregnated Leather , Golden Thread , Witches Hands , Buckets of Milk and Redstone Soup . Crafting damaged shoes with four Impregnated Leather will repair the shoes. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Seer Stone
content: A Seer Stone is a variation of a Crystal Ball, that allows witches to communicate with one another over great distances. It is mostly used by one witch to summon other members of their coven either to help out in a fight, or to help performing a ritual. The seer stone may also be used on another player to reveal if they are afflicted by a curse. To summon a coven witch for aid, use the Seer Stone while held. Holding for longer summons more coven members. If near a Witches' Cauldron , the summoned witches will circle it ready for a ritual. To call the entire coven to perform a rite. Start the rite normally while holding the Seer Stone. To determine if another player is cursed, to find out if they have a coven or familiar, and how much time they can manifest for, use the Seer Stone on them when they are within 3 blocks distance. Sneak-using the stone will show the same information for the bearer of the Stone. A Seer Stone is created by infusing a Quartz Sphere with Obsidian and Happenstance Oil using a Rite of Infusion .

title: Sentinel Fetish
content: A Sentinel Fetish defends the area in which it is placed, by send its trapped spectres to ward off unknown or unwanted beings. The spectres will return to the fetish when the creatures have been driven off. A Sentinel Fetish is created by binding 3 spirits , and 3 spectres to a fetish object using the Rite of Binding .

title: Shade of Leonard
content: Leonard is the demonic being who is rumored to have taught a cadre of German witches their trade, and often presided over their sabbats. A coven skilled with the Witches' Cauldron can summon a Shade of the Demon according to the method described in the Witches' Brews book. Leonard will significantly increase the range of spells cast from a Cauldron (removing the dimensional restriction of such castings). He will also increase the risk of side effects occurring during such castings. Once summoned, the Shade of Leonard will not leave without being forcefully removed, which is by no means an easy task. When "slain", Leonard has been known to drop one of his prized urns amongst other trappings.

title: Shaded Glass
content: Shaded Glass is an enhancement over normal Stained Glass, in that when it is powered by a redstone signal, it stops light passing through it. This effect can be used to control light levels in rooms, or to create effective daytime protection if you happen to be allergic to the sun . Shaded Glass comes in all 16 colors. Shaded Glass is made with Stained Glass of the appropriate color and Redstone Dust.

title: Shrieking Fetish
content: A Shrieking Fetish will scream when unwanted creatures approach, alerting the witch. When screaming the fetish will also emit a redstone signal. If a Witch's Ladder is used, no scream will be heard, but the signal will still be generated. A Shrieking Fetish is created by binding 3 spirits , and 2 banshees to a fetish object using the Rite of Binding .

title: Silver Bolt
content: A Silver Bolt is ammunition for the Witch Hunter Pistol Crossbow . It does around 3 hearts damage. A Silver Bolt is tipped with silver, so it is particularly effective against werewolves, doing double normal damage and ignoring a werewolf's normal resistance to attacks. Wooden Bolts are made from wooden bolts and silver deposits . An alternate recipe uses wooden bolts and a Silver Ingot.

title: Silver Deposits
content: Silver Deposits are an ingredient used in crafting offensive and defensive measures against werewolves. Deposits of silver can be extracted from the Gold smelting process by using a Silver Vat . If silver dust is available, silver deposits can be crafted together to make a single silver dust.

title: Silver Sword
content: A Silver Sword is a normal sword made from silver with a golden core. It has similar stats to that of a golden sword, although it is less enchantable (basically the same as an iron sword). Because it is made of silver it is very effective against werewolves, when a werewolf is hit by such a sword, the damage will be doubled and the resistance a werewolf has to normal damage will be ignored. A silver sword is made from Silver Deposits and a Golden sword. An alternative recipe uses a Golden Sword and 2 Silver Ingots above it (if they are available).

title: Silver Vat
content: A silver vat is an attachment for a Furnace that is used to capture any silver deposits that are left over when smelting Gold Ore . Simply place one or more silver vats on one of the side faces of a Furnace and begin smelting gold. Occasionally, some silver will be captured in the vat. Right-click on the vat to pick up the collected Silver Deposits . Multiple furnaces can be placed next to a single vat for better space efficiency. Vanilla hoppers (or pipes from other mods), can be used to automatically extract any collected silver. A Silver Vat is made from Iron ingots, a furnace and a bucket of water (you get the bucket back).

title: Silvered Hunter Armor
content: Silvered Hunter Armor is an upgrade to normal Witch Hunter Armor which adds significant protection from werewolves. When the wearer is attacked by a werewolf: The armor rating of the armor is doubled. The attacker will take a small amount of fire damage. The armor will take next to no damage from the attack. Silvered Hunter Armor is crafted with the normal Witch Hunter Armor piece and silver deposits . If Silver Ingots are available, they may be used in place of the Silver Deposits.

title: Snow Pressure Plate
content: A snow pressure plate is like a regular wooden pressure plate, just made from snow blocks. The are created in a crafting bench using 2 snow blocks (S). Crafting Bench -------------- S S

title: Snow Slab
content: Snow slabs are like all other slabs, just made from snow, they can be stacked to form a normal block just like all other slabs. Snow slabs are crafted in a crafting bench using 6 snow layer pieces (or 3 snow blocks prior to version 0.23) (S). Crafting Bench -------------- S S S

title: Snow Stairs
content: Snow stairs are simply stairs made out of snow. They a made in a crafting bench using 6 snow blocks (S). Crafting Bench -------------- S S S S S S

title: Snowbell Seeds
content: Snowbell seeds can be planted and eventually grow into the multistage snowbell plant. The fully grown snowbell plant can be harvested to obtain more Snowbell seeds, snowballs and occasionally a Icy Needle . Snowbell seeds can also be obtained as a random drop, by bashing tall grass .

title: Soft Clay Jar
content: Soft clay jars are the first step in producing a Clay Jar . After the soft clay jar is crafted using Clay , it will be fired in a furnace to produce the resulting Clay Jar . The recipe for clay jars in version 0.7 and lower was a little different, requiring only three clay:

title: Solidifying Brews
content: Solidifying Brews are used to change Hollow Tears into another form. First a brew of hollow tears must be thrown at the desired location (it may flow as needed). Then the desired solidifying brew is thrown at the hollow tears causing them to be replaced in their entirety with solid blocks of either Dirt, Stone, Sand or Sandstone. Alternatively Solifiying Brew (Erosion) may be used which will remove the Hollow Tears and all the blocks under them. The Solidifying Brews are made in a Kettle using a similar recipe: Solidifying Brew (Dirt) : Dirt, Mutandis , Foul Fume , Wood Ash , Odour of Purity and Spanish Moss . Solidifying Brew (Stone) : Stone, Mutandis , Foul Fume , Wood Ash , Odour of Purity and Spanish Moss . Solidifying Brew (Sand) : Sand, Mutandis , Foul Fume , Wood Ash , Odour of Purity and Spanish Moss . Solidifying Brew (Sandstone) : Sandstone, Mutandis , Foul Fume , Wood Ash , Odour of Purity and Spanish Moss . Solidifying Brew (Erosion) : Brew of Erosion , Mutandis , Foul Fume , Wood Ash , Odour of Purity and Spanish Moss .

title: Soul of Anguish Demon
content: The Soul of an Anguish demon when consumed allows a witch to cast the Soulfire effect using a Mystic Branch . The Soul is obtained from a Flame Imp .

title: Soul of Fear Demon
content: The Soul of a Fear Demon when consumed allows a witch to cast the Dark Mark effect using a Mystic Branch . The Soul is obtained from a Flame Imp .

title: Soul of Hunger Demon
content: The Soul of a Hunger demon when consumed allows a witch to cast the Fleshy Feast effect using a Mystic Branch . The Soul is obtained from a Flame Imp .

title: Soul of the World
content: Soul of the World is used in the Rite of Infusion to perform the Infusion of the Overworld as described in the book Witchcraft: Circle magic . Soul of the World is created in a kettle using the recipe shown in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Long-duration Regeneration Potion , Enchanted Golden Apple , Attuned Stone , Mandrake Root and Rowan Sapling .

title: Soul of Torment Demon
content: The Soul of a Torment demon when consumed allows a witch to cast the Torment effect using a Mystic Branch . The Soul is obtained from a Lord of Torment .

title: Spanish Moss
content: Spanish Moss is a flowering plant that grows on larger trees in much the same way as vine does. The moss is a common ingredient in the making of poppets . The moss is obtained with a random chance by using Mutandis on another plant, or by using shears on a placed Spanish Moss block (if shears are not used to harvest the block, it will be destroyed). Spanish Moss grows exactly like vine , although at a faster rate. The moss is not climbable.

title: Spear of the Huntsman
content: The Spear of the Huntsman is occasionally dropped when a Horned Huntsman is killed. It is a surprisingly durable wooden weapon that makes the wielder resistant to knock-back. If hit when blocking there is a chance a wolf will be summoned to attack the wielders assailant. Being a weapon from the Spirit World , it is somewhat effective at fighting its denizens.

title: Spectral Dust
content: Spectral Dust is found on spectral creatures. It is dropped when a Spectral Familiar is killed. It can also be dropped when other undead are killed with an Arthana , although the chance is very low.

title: Spectral Familiar
content: A Spectral Familiar is a helpful spirit that may be summoned using a Rite of Summoning , the details of which can be found in the book, Witchery: Circle Magic . The familiar is very tough, although it does very little, if any, damage. When a Spectral Familiar dies it will drop Spectral Dust . A summoned familiar will follow its summoner, or if right-clicked on will sit and stay until clicked again. Searching for ores If given a metal ingot, coal, redstone, lapis lazuli, an emerald or a diamond, the familiar will try to sniff out ore containing the given (or similar) material. If it finds any, the familiar will sit and make a noise. The ore will be located somewhere below where the familiar is sitting. The familiar will take on a slightly different color when searching for ores. This searching skill may be used multiple times, but each time costs one of the materials, and after the first time there is an increasing chance that the familiar will simply disappear, taking a potentially valuable item with it!

title: Spectral Stone
content: A Spectral Stone is a necromantic item used to trap certain spectral undead, such as Spectres, Banshees and Poltergeists. The stone must be used in a Rite of Binding along with Spectral Dust and a Boline to trap up to three of the same type of creature. The trapped undead can be deployed again by charging the stone and releasing when a sound is heard. The released undead will be bound to the witch who released them and may be controlled with a Necromantic Stone . The Spectral Stone is created using a Rite of Necromancy using a Necromantic Stone , Congealed Spirit , Condensed Fear , Spectral Dust and a Boline .

title: Spectre
content: A Spectre is an aggressive spectral creature, hard to see and their chill touch goes straight through armor. Use of Brew of Revealing to make them more visible. Spectres can be summoned with a Brazier . Using Graveyard Dust on this creature, if controlled by the player, will increase its maximum health by two points, to a maximum of 50 health.

title: Spinning Wheel
content: A Spinning Wheel is a device for spinning materials into thread. It is a magical device that draws power from a nearby Altar . To start spinning, place the raw material in the slot on the left, any additional ingredients in the bottom slots and the process will begin as long as there is sufficient power. The spinning wheel can be used to make Golden Thread , Fanciful Thread and Tormented Twine . Cobwebs can also be spun from 8 string. The Spinning Wheel is crafted using Item Frames , Wooden Planks , and Attuned Stone , a Stick and Wool .

title: Spirit of Otherwhere
content: Spirit of Otherwhere is used in the Rite of Infusion to perform the Infusion of Otherwhere as described in the book Witchcraft: Circle magic . Spirit of Otherwhere is created in a kettle using the recipe shown in the book Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions . Into the pot, throw Redstone Soup , Long-duration Potion of Swiftness , 2x Eye of Ender , Drop of Luck and Wool of Bat .

title: Spirit Portal
content: A Spirit Portal can only be created in the Spirit World . It can be used by witches that have used the Rite of Manifestation , to pass back into the real world as a ghost for a short time (although they take no items with them except for Icy Needles (which can be used to return to the Spirit World). When the player returns to the Spirit World (either because of death, time running out or use of an Icy Needle), all held items except for Icy Needles will be dropped. A Spirit portal is made with a 4x4 ring of snow blocks (the corners can be missed) in the same way as a nether portal is made (only not so tall). The portal is ignited by placing Flowing Spirit on its base. This can be done by using a Brew of Flowing Spirit or by using a Bucket of Flowing Spirit.

title: Spirit World
content: The spirit world is an alternate reality that mirrors the Overworld in topography. When people dream they often end up in the spirit world without realizing it (often appearing as one of its many denizens). Powerful witches can create Brews of Sleeping that allow them to pass their awake consciousness into the Spirit World, leaving their bodies and gear behind . The Spirit World is the only source of Wispy Cotton , Disturbed Cotton , Mellifluous Hunger the materials needed to make Dream Weavers . Also Brews of Flowing Spirit can only be made in the Spirit World. Very few things can pass into, or return from the Spirit World, keep this in mind when heading there ( Icy Needle always stay in the inventory). Wispy Cotton , Disturbed Cotton , Mellifluous Hunger and Brews of Flowing Spirit can pass from the Spirit World to the Real World but not the other way. Two important things to note: Circle magic does not function in the Spirit World; and Endermen cannot enter the Spirit World. Entering the Spirit World When a witch enters the Spirit World they will either experience a Dream or a Nightmare (it is almost guaranteed that a nightmare will occur just using a Brew of Sleeping). The world will change depending on the state of the dreamers, if all have nice dreams the world will be daytime, if any have a nightmare it will be night time. Visitors experiencing a nightmare will additionally be chased by a phyiscal embodiment of their Nightmare . To reduce the chances of a nightmare one can place a Dream Weaver of Nightmares nearby before Sleeping . Having a pool of flowing spirit and Wispy Cotton nearby also helps. Eating an Apple of Sleeping will also allow entry to the Spirit World, but it will always result in a nightmare. When a witch enters the spirit world, almost all of their gear will stay behind, being restored when they awaken. Use a chest if this sounds scary! Returning from the Spirit World A witch will always return from the Spirit World when they die (without consequence), they may also return earlier by using an Icy Needle on themselves to wake themselves up. Gear from the spirit world will stay there, being replaced by the witches normal gear when they awaken. If the sleeping body of a witch is killed, this will also force the witch to awaken. Manifesting as a Ghost Because the Spirit World so closely resembles the real world, it can be used to the advantage of a cunning witch to enter places in unseen ways. It is possible for a witch that has used the Rite of Manifestation to pass though Spirit Portals that have been created an enter the real world at the same location for a short period of time. A ghost player can return to the Spirit world using an Icy Needle , when their time expires or if they die. A ghost player can kill their sleeping body (causing them to awaken). Making nightmares worse Entering the spirit world while listening to the beating of Demon Hearts can cause nightmares much worse than usual. A dreamer may notice a somewhat reddish tint to the spirit world sky as well as the presence of fiery rain and the occasional demonic manifestation in addition to the usual nightmarish problems. To cause a demonic nightmare, a Brew of Sleeping must be drunk near to a Dream Weaver of Nightmares , a Flowing Spirit Pool and a beating Demon Heart . Fire and another heart increases the chance of the nightmare.

title: Spirit
content: Spirits are glowing creatures that spawn in and fly around the Spirit World . When hit enough, the become Subdued Spirits and may be picked up. Placing a subdued Spirit wakens it again. Subdues Spirits can be carried back from the Spirit World to the Overworld.

title: Splitting Bolt
content: A Splitting Bolt is ammunition for the Witch Hunter Pistol Crossbow . When fired the bolt splits into three Wooden Bolts which fan out. Each of the bolts will only do about 50% of the bolts normal damage after modifiers have been applied. The Splitting Bolt is made using Wooden Bolts , a Feather and String.

title: Statue of Broken Curses
content: The Statue of Broken Curses is a Creative Only item that may be placed by server admins to disable any directed curses (Misfortune, Insanity, Sinking, Overheating, Waking Nightmare, etc.) placed on a player within 32 blocks of the statue. If a player murders another player while in the area of effect of the statue, they will no longer receive its protection. This feature is disabled by default and may be enabled in the config file by setting the option: B:DecurseDirectedEnabled=true It is also possible to extend the radius of the protection (to a maximum of 128 blocks) by setting the option: I:DecurseDirectedProtectRadius=32 The block may only be removed by admins and the player who placed the block.

title: Statue of Hobgoblin Patron
content: Hobgoblins can be tricked into directing their worship away from their gods: Mog and Gulg, instead to a Witch (or Warlock) unscrupulous enough to do this. Doing so allows a witch's Mystic Branch powers to be magnified. First a Statue must be constructed using Stone and a Koboldite Ingot . Then, the statue must be bound to the witch using the appropriate Rite of Binding . Finally, the statue must be placed near to Hobgoblins , who may begin to worship it, and direct this power to the witch. The more worshipers the more power. For a small number of worshipers, the witch's infusion power will recharge within 64 blocks of the statue. For a medium number of worshipers, the witch may draw larger versions of a Symbol with their Mystic Branch to have a corresponding larger effect.
