The poppet is crafted using a Poppet , Yellow Flowers , Red Flowers , Red Mushroom , Brown Mushroom , Belladonna and Mandrake Root .

title: Water Artichoke Globe
content: A Water Artichoke Globe is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is harvested from a fully grown Water Artichoke plant that must be grown from Water Artichoke Seeds . Water Artichoke Globe has a curious property when eaten, it immediately fills your hunger bar, but then causes hunger over time until the hunger bar is back where it started.

title: Water Artichoke Seeds
content: Water Artichoke seeds can be planted and eventually grow into the multistage Water Artichoke plant. The fully grown Water Artichoke plant can be harvested to obtain more Water Artichoke seeds and a Water Artichoke Globe . Water Artichoke seeds can only be planted on still water blocks. Water Artichoke seeds can also be obtained as a random drop, by bashing tall grass .

title: Water Diviner
content: A water diviner is a helpful tool for locating if there is a water source somewhere below the clicked block. To use the diviner, right-click and hold on a block until either a success or fail sound is played (there will also be white or black smoke respectively). The water diviner is crafted using sticks , water bottles and a Tear of the Goddess .

title: Water Protection Poppet
content: The water protection poppet will redirect deadly drowning damage from the bound player to the poppet and refill the players breath. The poppet will be destroyed it in the process. For this effect to occur, the poppet must either remain on the protected being or be stored on a poppet shelf somewhere . The poppet is crafted from a Poppet , Ink Sacs and Water Artichoke Globes . in version 0.5.1 and earlier this poppet was crafted with Water Bottles in place of Water Artichoke Globes.

title: Waystone
content: A waystone is an etched piece of flint that is an important foci for circle magic involving transposition. A waystone can be bound to a specific location using the Rite of Binding described in the book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic . A bound waystone can also be made by dropping up to eight into a hollow 3x3 circle of Otherwhere runes and waiting. A waystone is crafted using Flint and a Bone Needle .

title: Web
content: Web has been replaced with Dense Web as of version 0.5.0.

title: Werewolf
content: A werewolf, is a Villager that has become infected with lycanthropy . Normally, the werewolf will act just like a normal villager. When there is a full moon, the villager will transform into their Wolfman form and attack players and villagers alike. The Wolfman form is very hard to kill, being resistant to most forms of damage. Silver weapons seem to be the best way to deal with them. Very rarely, a Wolfman will have been granted the ability to spread their affliction to other villagers and players, although this normally only comes about if the Werewolf receives the gift to do so directly from the deity responsible for this curse. Only the Wolfsbane flower is known to be able to detect an infected villager.

title: Whiff of Magic
content: A Whiff of Magic is an ingredient used in many recipes. It is acquired with a random chance when burning Rowan saplings in an oven .

title: Wicker Bundle
content: A Wicker Bundle is an aesthetic block similar to the Hay bale , the main difference being it cannot be crafted back into saplings. The wicker bundle can be placed in the world or used to create Bloodied Wicker Bundles . A player may encounter strawmen structures in their travels, but a proper wickerman following the same pattern is more authentic. The Wicker bundle is crafted using 9 saplings of any type .

title: Wild Bramble
content: Wild bramble is a plant that causes damage to any creature or item that passes though it. It is very difficult to break down and when you do it spreads all over the place. The only assured way to stop it spreading is to use a golden axe to harvest it (or dig it up). Wild Brambles are mutated from a Cactus plant up to 4 blocks high that is surrounded by Spanish moss on four sides. The moss must be over still water blocks. Between each Spanish moss block must be Grassper plants holding two blaze powder and two bonemeal . Using a mutating sprig on the bottom most block of the Cactus will mutate up to 4 cactus blocks (vertically stacked) into Wild Bramble.

title: Winged Monkey
content: A Winged Monkey, is quite literally a monkey with wings. The are useful for fetching creatures or carrying their owner to places. A winged monkey will defend its owner to the death. A Winged Monkey can be tamed by giving it raw pork or beef. Once tamed it can be instructed to sit by right-clicking it, do so again to make it stand. A tamed monkey will follow its owner and fight for them. A tamed Winged Monkey may be dyed by holding the dye and right-clicking on the Monkey. Giving a Winged Monkey a Taglock Kit bound to a creature or player, will cause the monkey to fly towards that entity, pick them up, and carry them back to the location where they were given the taglock kit. The entity will then be dropped and the Monkey will sit. The Taglock Kit will be consumed. Giving a Winged Monkey a Bound Waystone , or a Blooded Waystone , will cause the Monkey to pick up the player and then fly to the bound location with the player. When the bound location is reached, the player will be dropped off and the Monkey will sit. The waystone will not be consumed. A winged monkey is made via mutation, by using a Mutating Sprig on a cobweb, above water, containing an Owl and a Wolf. The cobweb must be surround on each direct side by a fully grown cocoa bean. Grasspers must be adjacent diagonally, containing two Mutandis Extremis , a Poppy and a Charged Attuned Stone . Once created, the monkey will need to be tamed.

title: Wispy Cotton
content: Wispy Cotton is a plant that only grows in the Spirit World , where it occurs naturally in most Biomes. It can be collected and replanted, but will only spread if planted in the Spirit World, on Dirt or Grass that is next to a pool of Flowing Spirit . If Wispy Cotton is harvested when someone is having a nightmare, Disturbed Cotton will be dropped instead. Both are important ingredient in making dream weavers. Disturbed Cotton cannot be replanted however. Having planted Wispy Cotton nearby when using a Brew of Sleeping near to a Dream Weaver of Nightmares will further reduce the chance of a nightmare by 10% for each cotton plant (up to 2). Wispy Cotton will remain in a players inventory when they return from the Spirit World.

title: Witch Hunter Armor
content: Witch hunters need to have protection from their prey, and to this end they have developed a curing process for leather that makes it inherently resistant to magics, especially black magic. The only caveat being that the entire body must be covered for the protection to have its effect. Witch Hunter Armor offers the same protection as leather armor but is much more durable (approaching that of iron armor). It is repaired in an anvil with normal leather pieces. It can also be dyed. Witch Hunter Armor offers varying degrees of magical mitigation so long as the entire set is worn: 25% chance to mitigate general magical damage 25% chance to mitigate the effects of a voodoo poppet 90% chance to mitigate the Curse of Waking Nightmare 90% chance to mitigate the Curse of Misfortune 90% chance to resist being summoned with a Rite of Transposition 100% chance to mitigate the effects of a Vampiric Poppet Protection Poppets (e.g. Earth, Voodoo Protection) bound to a Witch Hunter wearing the full armor set will also not function. A Nullifying Bolt's effects can only occur if the shooter is fully clad in Witch Hunter Armor. Witch Hunter Armor is made in a crafting table using Nullified Leather . Witch Hunter Armor can be upgraded to Silvered Hunter Armor for protection against werewolves.

title: Witch Hunter Crossbow Pistol
content: The weapon of choice for Witch Hunters is the Crossbow Pistol. Easy to conceal and flexible to use. The weapon gives the Witch Hunter the ability to launch surprise attacks on their targets, often allow multiple shots in quick succession from several preloaded Crossbow Pistols. The other advantage of fighting at range, is that it overcomes the inherent weakness of the armor a witch hunter typically uses. For the Crossbows Pistol to function, a supply of bolts needs to be in the player's inventory. The crossbow pistol can be loaded with one of the following types of bolts: Wooden Bolt - Basic ammunition, penetrates some witches defenses Splitting Bolt - Splits into three wooden bolts each doing half damage Bone Bolt - Does 50% more damage against undead and demons Nullifying Bolt - Drains 1/3 of the targets magical power (shooter must be wearing full Witch Hunter Armor ) Silver Bolt - Does double damage to werewolves and ignores their normal damage resistance. The crossbow is loaded by holding use (right-click) for a couple of seconds while sneaking (shift-pressed). If a bolt is already loaded, loading a second time will replace it with a bolt of a different type. The loaded Pistol Crossbow is fired by pressing and releasing the use button (right-click). To get a critical hit, the use key must be held for a longer duration (about the same as a bow). The Pistol Crossbow can be enchanted in and Enchanting Table like a normal bow, but the infinity enchant works very differently, it halves the durability loss of the Pistol Crossbow instead of granting unlimited bolts. The Pistol Crossbow is made in a crafting table using a Bow, Iron Ingots, a Stick, String and a Bone Needle .

title: Witch Hunter
content: Witch Hunters are people that have dedicated their lives to track down and destroy witches (and other magic users or cursed humans) who practice black magic. Each time a witch uses black magic (Curses, Voodoo Poppets or Vampiric Poppets) there is a chance some Witch Hunters may notice. If they do they will appear some time in the near future and attack the witch. Hunters are also famed for hunting out werewolves and vampires, and often come equipped for the job in hand: including Silver and Fiery Bolts. Top level vampire players should take care not to alienate nearby villagers, lest the hunters be called!

title: Witchcraft: Brews & Infusions
content: The book, Witchcraft: Brews & infusions, describes all of the recipes that may be created by a witch (or warlock) using a kettle . Each page in the book describes the brew or infusion, the recipe and power that are needed. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , Belladonna and Odour of Purity .

title: Witchcraft: Circle Magic
content: The book, Witchcraft: Circle Magic, describes all of the rites that may be performed by a witch (or warlock) using magic circles. Each page in the book describes the rite, the foci items, power and circles that are needed. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , Belladonna and a Whiff of Magic .

title: Witchcraft: Collecting Fumes
content: The book, Witchcraft: Collecting Fumes, describes the correct use of a witches oven and how it may be used to collect the different fumes such as Foul Fume and Whiff of Magic . The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , belladonna and Charcoal .

title: Witchcraft: Conjuration & Fetishes
content: The book Witchcraft: Conjuration & Fetishes describes how to summon spectral creatures with a Brazier and how to bind them to Fetishes. The book is crafted using a book, ink sac, feather, flint & steel and Wood Ash .

title: Witchcraft: Distilling
content: The book, Witchcraft: Distilling, describes the correct use of a Distillery and includes all of the available distillation recipes. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , Belladonna and Breath of the Goddess .

title: Witchcraft: Herbology
content: The book, Witchcraft: Herbology, describes the various new plants that are available to a new witch or warlock, how they are obtained and how they are cultivated. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather , Red Rose and Yellow Dandelion .

title: Witchcraft: Symbology
content: The book, Witchcraft: Symbology, describes the many symbols that can be drawn with a Mystic Branch to achieve magical effects. The book is crafted using an Empty Book , an Ink Sac , a Feather and an Ent Twig .

title: Witches' Brews
content: The book, Witches' Brews is the definitive guide to crafting, brewing and performing rituals with a Witches' Cauldron . The book is broken down into various sections describing the basics of crafting, potion brewing and ritual casting. It also contains a reference guide to all of the possible ingredients and what they do, split up in relevant sections. Unlike other books, this one features a system of linked pages, where keywords may be clicked on to jump to a different topic. A back button, and move up an index button exist to navigate backwards, while a next button allows the reader to view the next page in the current section. The book is created in a crafting table using a book (B), feather (F), ink sac (I), Dirt (D). Crafting Table -------------- D F B I

title: Witches' Cauldron
content: A Witches' Cauldron is a special type of cauldron that a witch may use to brew potions, cast spells or create magically infused items. The Witches' Cauldron is in Witchery 0.21 and above. A Witches Cauldron is made by placing a regular Cauldron in the world, and then using Anointing Paste on it. To brew a potion, cast a ritual or create an item, the cauldron must first be filled with water. This can be achieved with by using three buckets of water on it, or pumping water into it using pipes, or by using a Dispenser containing a Brew of Endless Water . Once a cauldron is full of water, it must boil. Lighting a fire beneath the cauldron is the only way for this to happen. Using a netherrak block will guarantee the fire does not go out, although if it does go out, it may be set to boil again without any consequences (unlike brewing in a Kettle!). When the water is boiling, ingredients may be added to the Cauldron (one after another), to create the desired effect. The book, Witches' Brews gives a complete overview of all ingredients and what they can do when added to a brew. Crafting The simplest use of a cauldron, is simply to add items to a set recipe to create a new item. The first such recipe that a new witch must learn is that of Mutandis . This mutagen is required to mutate plants into new, undiscovered species, that have relevance for witchcraft. To create Mutandis in a Witches' Cauldron, fill it with water and let it boil, then add, Mandrake Root , Exhale of the Horned One and an Egg . Then sit back and wait for a few pieces of Mutandis to be produced. You will likely need a full stack to get going. There are many other recipes listed in the Crafting section of the book Witches' Brews , that are created in a similar way. Brewing Potions Potions can be brewed in a Witches' Cauldron, a potion can have a number of different effects that will be applied one after the other. A potion has a so-called dispersal mechanism (e.g. is it drunk, thrown with an instant AOE effect, thrown with a gas effect, etc.). When a potion is complete, an empty glass bottle should be used on the cauldron to fill it with the potion. To start with much of the potion will be wasted when doing this and only a single bottle is likely to be acquired, but with practice and later the use of Witches' Gear, a Toad Familiar and special Potions it is possible to get up to 15 potions per brewing. There may even be very rare ways to get more! It is possible to use a glass bottle-filled dispenser (that is not pointing at the cauldron), to automatically bottle potions when it receives a redstone signal. You may need a hopper to catch them though. Capacity A typical potion is made by first adding ingredients that provide "space" for effects to be added. These ingredients are called capacity modifiers. They must be added in order to increase the capacity the final potion has for holding different types of effects. Think of capacity like adding slots to your potion, each effect you add afterwards requires a certain number of free slots. If we add Mandrake, we are adding one capacity slot to out potion, if we then add Netherwart, we are adding two further slots (for a total of 3), if we then add Tear of the Goddess, we further increase the slots by two (for a total of 5 slots). It is important to remember that adding the capacity modifiers must be done in-order, adding Netherwart after tear of the Goddess will do nothing. Power and Duration Before we start adding effects to the potion, we may want to add more power or increase the duration of the effect. This is done by adding power and/or duration modifiers. These again must be added in-order to increase the power (or duration) of the following effect form level I up-to level IV. To reiterate, this must be done for each effect, before you add the effect. So if we want to increase the following effect up to power level II, we would add Glowstone. To increase the duration of the following effect to level III, we would add redstone and then obsidian. Just like the capacity upgrades, the power and duration upgrades must be added in-order. It must be noted that a potion can only have a certain number of boosts, spread over all added effects, so the witch must decide two level IV effects and the rest level I, or four effects at level II, for example. Effects Now that we have provided space for an effect, and decided on the power and duration boosts, we can add an ingredient that determines the effect. Each effect will require a differing amount of capacity slots depending on how complex (or powerful) it is. For example, the Tilling Effect only consumes one capacity slot, whereas Shifting Seasons (changing a biome) requires 8 slots. This obviously means that we can create a potion that has many "level 1" effects, but only very few high level effects. After we have added an effect, we must then add power and duration boost before we add the next effect (if another effect is desired). All effects and their level (i.e. number of capacity slots needed) are listed in the book Witches' Brews . Let us assume we want to make a potion of fire resistance, damage boost III and long duration night vision. We would add ingredients as follows: Set capacity, each of these effects require 2 slots, so we must make 6 slots available, add: netherwart, tear of the goddess and diamond vapor Add fire resistance effect, add: magma cream Set power for damage effect to level III, add: glowstone, blaze rod Add damage effect, add: blaze powder Set duration for night vision effect to level II, add: redstone Add night vision effect, add: golden carrot Now use a empty glass bottle on the cauldron to obtain the potion (practice, witches' gear, toad familiars, and other potion effects will determine how many bottles you can get from a specific cauldron). Dispersal By default a potion will be of the drinkable variety, there are however ways to change that. After the potion effects have all been added, it is possible to add further ingredients to set the dispersal. But before we do this, we may want to augment the extent and duration (lingering) of the dispersal method. To do this (in much the same way as we did with power and duration of effects, we must add the appropriate ingredients, before we add the dispersal method (these ingredients for extent and lingering differ from the power and duration boosts). The following dispersal methods can be added to a potion: Splash - Adding either water artichoke or gunpowder to the potion will turn it into a splash potion, when thrown this potion will apply its effects to all creatures (and or blocks) near the impact point, to a range determined by the extent boosts. The further from the impact point the less effective the effects will be. Lingering boosts have no effect on this dispersal type. Gas - Adding Wool of Bat to the potion will turn it into a gas potion, when thrown it will explode into a gas cloud that will expand out to fill nearby air blocks. The effects of the potion will get applied (with a reduce effect) to any creatures in the gas cloud. The extent to which the cloud expands, and the amount of time it stays for is determined by the Extent and Linger boosts. Liquid - Adding XX to the potion will turn it into a liquid potion, when thrown it will break, releasing a liquid onto the floor that will flow out (like water). Any creatures in the liquid, or blocks touched by it can have the potion effects applied to it. The amount of time the liquid stays is determined by the linger boost (extent has no effect on this dispersal type). Triggered - Adding a Zombie Head to the potion will turn it into a triggered dispersal, normally used for cursing objects, when bottled, it may be thrown onto a button, level, wooden door or pressure plate, and when that item is next used, the potion effects will be applied to whomever used it. Multiple instances of the same potion may be applied to the same block to allow multiple charges. Altar Power The final element of brewing a potion, is the Alta Power cost of an ingredient. Each ingredient may require a certain amount of altar power to be used. If a cauldron is bubbline but there ar no particles flying out of it, then you do not have enough power in your altar. Other Modifiers There are other modifiers that may be used with a potion to achieve other general effects, such as reducing the drinking speed, changing the potions color, disabling an effect from applying to an entity or block, disabling particle effects, etc. A full list of these modifiers and corresponding ingredients can be found in the Witches' Brews book. Casting Spells with Rituals Instead of bottling a brew, it is possible to cast a full cauldron as a spell. This has a number of additional steps and caveats that must be considered. Targeting The first consideration is how to target the spell. This can be done in one of three ways, either: Targeted at the location of the cauldron, for this to occur just add Tongue of Dog to the finished Brew Targeted somewhere in the world (or another dimension) - First add a waystone bound to the location, then add Tongue of Dog Targetted on a player or creature - Add a taglock kit for that player or creature. Range The second consideration is the range of the target from the cauldron. If a target is selected that is far away, the spell will often fail, the range of the spell can be increased by using a larger coven (whether Coven Witches or other players with familiars). Up to 7 witches can participate in total to increase the range up to anywhere in the same world. To cast a ritual across dimensions, a full coven is needed and a Shade of Leonard must be summoned. Power boosts Power boosts applied to effects will not apply unless a coven of sufficient size is present (up to a maximum of 7 witches). Altar Power In general, casting a brew as a spell, uses more altar power then bottling potions, so be prepared for this. It is possible to surround the cauldron with circle glyphs to reduce the power costs, white circles reduce the costs moderately, infernal circles, reduce the costs significantly (but increase the risks). Please note these circles are different from those used in Circle Magic only rites. Side Effects Casting spells from cauldrons does carry the risk of side effects, such as aliments or worse, surrounding the cauldron with two white circles will mitigate these side effects, while using infernal circles (and obtaining cheaper power requirements) will increase the chance of a side effect. Casting with a Shade of Leonard nearby will always carry some risk of side-effects. Dispersal The dispersal method for a brew behaves differently when the brew is cast as a ritual (when using tongue of dog, an dispersal ingredient is always required): Splash - Location targeted, general will only hit the targeted point, although the effect may differ. Gas - This dispersal becomes an expanding wave from the targeted point, that applies the effect to any creature that it hits. Liquid - This dispersal becomes a rain effects, droplets falling from the sky, applying their effects to whatever they hit. Triggered - This will cause the effect to be added to an item that is put (thrown) on top of the cauldron when the ritual completes. If no item is above the cauldron the ritual fails. When this item is next used (right click), the applied effects will occur on the user. The same brew can be applied multiple times to the item. This method is the typical way to curse a book when read, or make poisonous apples, etc. Failed Rituals When a ritual fails, you will see colored smoke that will indicate the reason why it has failed. The brew will not be consumed in such cases and the ritual trigger (taglock kit or tongue of dog will be spat out). Red Smoke - The circles around the target (and/or source) sites are incorrect Blue Smoke - Coven witches are required Yellow Smoke - Not enough altar power Green Smoke - Target too far away Purple Smoke - Generic other reason

title: Witches Hand
content: The hand of one who went before (or witches hand for short) is one of the more gruesome trappings of witchcraft, required to focus the immense power of an infused being. A witches hand may be dropped when a witch is defeated; so those witchcraft practitioners looking to begin experimenting with infusions , should first start hunting for witches huts in swamp biomes . To maximize the chance of obtaining a witches hand, the killing blow to a witch should come from an arthana .

title: Witches Hat
content: Every witch and warlock (just like every wizard) needs a battered, pointy hat. One could even say that it's a trademark. The Witches Hat has the same durability and protection as leather armor and can be dyed and enchanted like leather armor too. If a brew is decanted while a witches hat is worn (with the exception of Redstone Soup ), there is a chance (about 35%) that an additional brew will be produced. Witches hats will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if a normal hat was worn. Other players however will not be able to see the hat! The Witches Hat is crafted from Impregnated Leather , Golden Thread and Glowstone Dust . Crafting a damaged witches hat with three Impregnated Leather will repair the hat. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Witches Robes
content: Witches robes are worn in the chest slot and have the same durability and protection as leather armor. They can be dyed and enchanted like leather armor too. If a brew is decanted while the robes are worn (with the exception of Redstone Soup and necromantic brews like the Brew of Raising ), there is a chance (about 35%) that an additional brew will be produced. This bonus stacks with the Witches Hat bonus (for a combined chance of 70%). While wearing the robes, creepers will generally ignore you ! Witches robes will turn invisible when the player is invisible. This is only a visual change, mobs will still react as if normal chest armor was worn. Other players however will not be able to see the robes! The Witches Robes are crafted from Impregnated Leather , Golden Thread and a Creeper Heart . Crafting damaged witches robes with four Impregnated Leather will repair the robes. Alternatively use an anvil and normal leather.

title: Witch's Ladder
content: A Witch's Ladder is an object that can be used as a Fetish when spectral undead are bound to it using a Rite of Binding . It is made using Feathers, String and Fanciful Thread .

title: Wolf Altar
content: A Wolf Altar is a way to commune with the deity responsible for the Werewolf Curse. Through the altar, a player infected with the curse may seek to expand their knowledge and capabilities as a werewolf. Those seeking to become cursed, may trick the deity into blessing a trapped wolfman with the ability to spread the curse. If a player is under the Curse of the Wolf , they may use the altar to be given tasks, that will allow them to become more powerful. At any time after a player has been gifted a Moon Charm , they may use the Altar with 3 Gold Ingots in hand, in order to obtain another Moon Charm . A Wolf Altar is made with Stone, Wolfsbane flower and either Wolf Heads or Hellhound Heads (does not matter which, and in what order).

title: Wolf Head
content: A wolf head can be dropped when a wolf or dog is killed. Likewise a Hellhound head can be dropped when a Hellhound is killed. Wolf and hellhound heads can be used in crafting recipes, or they can be placed in the world as decoration (much like skulls).

title: Wolf Token
content: The Bat/Wolf Token is a creative only item that when used for a second will increase the player's werewolf level by 1 until it reaches 10, then it will reset it to 0 (thus removing the Wolf Curse). If sneak is held (shift) when the token is used, the players vampire level will be increased by 1 until it reaches 10, then it will reset it to 0.

title: Wolfsbane Seeds
content: Wolfsbane seeds can be planted and eventually grow into the multistage Wolfsbane plant. The fully grown Wolfsbane plant can be harvested to obtain more Wolfsbane seeds and the Wolfsbane flower . Wolfsbane seeds can also be obtained as a random drop, by bashing tall grass .

title: Wolfsbane
content: Wolfsbane is an ingredient used in a few recipes dealing with werewolves. It is harvested from a fully grown Wolfsbane plant that must be grown from Wolfsbane seeds . Wolfsbane can be used on villagers and players, and will reveal if they are werewolves. Wolfsbane may also be brewed into a potion that prevents the shapeshifting of a werewolf, forcing them to stay in whatever form they were in.

title: Wolftrap
content: Some werewolves seem to be able to avoid Beartraps , so Hunters have found a way to adapt the bear trap into something that can capture only werewolves, the Wolftrap. Placed and set in the same way as a Beartrap, the Wolftrap will only activate if a werewolf mob walks over it in Wolf or Wolfman Form. Beware, breaking the wolftrap will permanently destroy it! A wolftrap is made in a crafting bench using a Beartrap , Silver Deposits , Wolfsbane and Null Catalyst .

title: Wood Ash
content: Wood ash is an ingredient used in various recipes. It is acquired by burning saplings (or any type) in a furnace or oven .

title: Wooden Bolt
content: A Wooden Bolt is ammunition for the Witch Hunter Pistol Crossbow . It does around 3 hearts damage. Because it is wooden it penetrates the protection of the Bark Belt and Baba Yaga 's thorns. Wooden Bolts are made from Sticks, Feathers and String.

title: Wooden Stake
content: A Wooden Stake through the heart is one of the few ways a Vampire may be killed, although it is not so easy to get them in a position where such a precise strike can be applied. The most effective way, is to use the Wooden Stake on a Vampire when it is sleeping! Doing so will instantly kill the vampire player. Staking is not a practical tool, but it is present for flavor. Staking can be disabled in the config file, using the key: B:AllowStakingVampires=false A Wooden Stake is made from a Stick surrounded by Garlic .

title: Wool of Bat
content: Wool of Bat is an ingredient dropped when a Bat is killed. The chance of wool of bat dropping is improved when the Bat is killed with an Arthana .

title: Wormwood
content: Wormwood is a plant grown from its seeds and harvested to allow summoning of the dead using a brazier. Wormwood seeds must be planted on tilled earth and will grow in stages eventually to two blocks in height (although each block may be harvested separately). Wormwood must initially be mutated using a Mutating Sprig on fully grown Wheat, surrounded by Wispy Cotton , with water diagonally in the corners.

title: Wormy Apple
content: A Wormy Apple is an apple that has one or more worms living within it. Eating such an apple will most likely poison the eater. The apple is an ingredient for other recipes. To create a Wormy Apple, place an Apple , Sugar and Rotten Flesh into a crafting table.

title: Woven Crour
content: Woven Crour is a special material that can only be made with vampire blood. It is a tough material, that offers some benefits to vampires when made into clothing . Making Woven Crour Place a block of White Wool into the world The player must be a vampire of level 4 or above, and have 125 (1/2 droplet) blood available in their blood pool Use a Bone Needle on the Block of Wool, to make Blood-stained Wool Break the block and put it into a furnace, this makes the material Woven Cruor.

title: Witchery 0.15.0 released
content: Witchery 0.15.0 is released, highlights: Spirit Dimension - Mirrors the real world, use a Brew of Sleeping to get there. Can be nightmarish or nice. Gear stays with your body in the real world! Use a chest if you are scared Spirit Portals and Rite of Manifestation - Return from the Spirit World though a Spirit Portal as a Ghost, get into secret places unnoticed (remember almost no objects can travel between worlds). Dream Weaver overhaul - Need string made using the Spinning Wheel with ingredients from the Spirit World. Dream Weaver of Intensity - Makes Dream Weaver effects stronger (some have less duration then though). Biting Belt - Something for the leg slot works the same way as Parasytic lice (free potion injection when hit). Brew of Sleeping - see above. Brew of Flowing Spirit - Helpful with Spirit World Brew of Wasting - instant defoliation (or starvation). Spinning Wheel - Rumpelstiltskin... Loads of other stuff, see the Version History .

title: Witchery 0.16.0 released
content: This version adds plenty of new gear for witches including the Bark Belt, Brew Bag and Ruby slippers. It also adds a new variation of the Spirit World, a new curse and forestry rite and the ability to target ritual effects elsewhere. It also fixes fixes and tweaks a fair few things, see the Version History for download link and changes.

title: Witchery 0.17.0 released
content: This version adds a brazier, used to summon spectral undead, and a Spectral Stone to bind them, or create fetishes with them to place around the overworld and spirit world for interesting effects. Infused Brews and solidifying brews to change landscapes. All features are in the Version History .

title: Witchery 0.18.0 released
content: This version adds Witch Hunters to hunt down witches who practice black magic. Players can also craft special Witch Hunter gear. Defeating Death can now result in special death gear getting dropped (collect the whole set and become death yourself!). All bug fixes and other changes including the download link can be found in the Version History .

title: Witchery 0.19.0 released
content: This version adds a touch of the demonic to Witchery. Summon an imp and learn dark magic from it, and maybe become the master of Torment. For all new stuff, tweaks and fixes as well as a download link, see the Version History .

title: Witchery 0.20.0 released
content: Adds Hobgoblins! Bring them home with a leash, build a village for them, put them to work in the mines, make them worship you for power. Use Owls to deliver items and messages, or aerial bombardment. All changes and download links in the Version History .

title: Witchery 0.21.0 released
content: Adds the ability to mix brews in a witches' cauldron containing one or more from over 100 effects. Also adds earmuffs, player compasses, pitfall blocks and a few other bits and bobs. See the version history for download link and all changes.

title: Witchery 0.22.0 released
content: Add Werewolves and silver weapons to fight them with. Curse of the Wolf can turn players into werewolves with shapeshifting!

title: Witchery 0.23.0 released
content: Adds vampirism for players, winged monkeys and towns. See the version history for all changes.

title: Witchery 0.24 released
content: Adds Magic Mirrors with a few reflection-based magical tricks.
